- SDK exposes ergonomic helpers for common semantic actions.
- Helpers produce expected SEMANTIC_ACTION/SEMANTIC_FOCUS protocol strings.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add typed surface placement role enum
description: Add a typed `SurfacePlacementRole` enum and helpers so SDK apps can switch on app-surface/decoration/overlay roles without string-matching `composition_plane`. Wire `SurfacePlacementContract::role()` through it while preserving existing string fields for serialization. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Triple-Ctrl-C exit guard; single Ctrl-C passes through
description: kittui-wm-demo (and the attach client) treat single Ctrl-C as an InputEvent::Char{ch: '\x03'} forwarded to the focused window. Track a counter that decays 1s after the last Ctrl-C; on count >= 3 within the window, restore terminal and exit. The 'q to quit' message in the footer changes to 'Ctrl-C Ctrl-C Ctrl-C to quit' once any backend is hosting an app that might consume Ctrl-C. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show heading anchor count in kittui-md rich status
description: Update kittui-md rich/interactive status line so heading-anchor coverage is visible alongside heading counts. Add targeted tests and keep changes narrow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add app discovery commands to native socket
description:
Large gap: no-arg native `kittwm` owns the live DISPLAY-like socket, but it does not support the app discovery commands available on the standalone daemon (`APPS`, `APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST`, `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST`). Inside a native WM session, attach clients should be able to discover launchable commands before calling `SPAWN_PTY`.

Scope:
- Teach `NativeSpawnQueue` to handle `APPS`, `APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST <query>`, and `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST <query>` by reusing existing daemon helpers.
- Keep existing native socket spawn/focus/close/layout/status behavior unchanged.
- Add targeted tests for APPS_JSON and query routing on native queue replies.
- Document the attach commands in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --attach -c APPS_JSON` works against a native session socket.
- Tests cover native queue APPS_JSON and APPS_FIRST responses.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui UI primitives: textbox, headings, title/banner/header/footer, textchip
description: Add reusable kittui UI component primitives so callers do not handroll common surfaces. Components requested: textbox, h1/h2/h3, title, banner, header, footer, textchip. Each should produce kittui scenes/layers with consistent spacing, typography-like scale (within terminal constraints), padding, border/glow/theme hooks, and deterministic sizing. Include snapshot/proof tests and small CLI/example output. Should be usable by markdown renderer and kittwm overlays.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add visible chrome metadata to raw-frame path
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm sessions use the raw RGBA fast path, but raw frames carry only image bytes/footprints. The reusable kittwm chrome theme is only visible on the slower Scene compose path, so live raw sessions lack visible title/focus chrome.

Scope:
- Extend `RawFrame` with lightweight chrome metadata: title string, focused flag, and tiled/floating mode.
- Populate metadata in `Compositor::raw_frames` from focus/mode/window id.
- Render a minimal terminal title strip in `session.rs` above/over each raw frame using the metadata so focused windows are visibly marked.
- Add targeted tests for raw frame metadata.

Acceptance:
- `raw_frames` marks the focused frame and provides title/mode metadata.
- Session code renders title text without affecting image placement semantics.
- `cargo test -p kittui-wm raw_frame` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Exercise link and image titles in kittui-md proof gallery
description: Update docs/examples/kittui-md-proof.md so sample links/images include Markdown title attributes, validating the newly preserved metadata through --links, --images, and --metadata-json.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon SPAWN creates tracked panes instead of detached processes
description: Next proposal: current SPAWN starts a detached process. Extend the kittwm daemon protocol/session state so socket-only spawn creates a tracked pane/window with layout/focus metadata and can be listed/attached by clients. Builds on bd-cddcf2/bd-624e22.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui inline theme tokens reusable library
description:
Extract inline component theme/style tokens into reusable library code.

Scope:
- Move Nord/glass/chrome/metal/neon inline color/style resolution out of `crates/kittui-cli/src/main.rs` into an appropriate reusable module/crate (likely `kittui-affordances`).
- Expose typed theme/style/color resolution APIs for CLI, kittwm chrome, kittwm-bar, and future prompt/status components.
- Preserve current CLI behavior and flags.
- Add library tests for default Nord glass and color overrides.
- Validate targeted tests/build and diff check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: update SDK plan for first-party app maturity
description:
Documentation debt: docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md still describes kittwm-terminal and kittwm-launch as skeleton-stage items even though terminal now has typed status/events and launcher maturity is landing. Update the SDK plan to reflect current first-party app status and remaining gaps.

Scope:
- Update Current state / First-party apps / Backlog mapping in docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md.
- Mention kittwm-terminal status/events and kittwm-launch matured backend/dry-run/status behavior if landed; otherwise describe terminal landed and launcher pending.
- Keep docs concise.

Acceptance:
- SDK plan no longer misleadingly describes already-landed first-party app work as only skeletons.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md modes and metadata outputs
description: Document the kittui-md CLI modes added during Markdown work: --plain, --rich, --interactive, --outline, and --metadata-json, including what metadata JSON contains and where the proof gallery lives. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: kittui-wm hosts an xterm via XQuartz on macOS
description: Integration test that skips if XQuartz isn't installed. Spawns XquartzServer, launches xterm against the display, waits for it to map, calls compose() and asserts at least one non-empty capture is returned. The demo gains --features xquartz to drive this end-to-end with tendril screenshot proof. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: CLI wrappers for text JSON reads
description:
CLI wrapper gap: daemon and SDK expose `READ_TEXT_JSON` / `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON`, but `kittwm` only has text wrappers.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm --read-text-json WINDOW` mapping to `READ_TEXT_JSON <window>`.
- Add `kittwm --read-scrollback-json WINDOW` mapping to `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON <window>`.
- Add help text and focused CLI request tests.
- Keep existing `--read-text` / `--read-scrollback` behavior unchanged.
- No daemon/session/SDK changes.

Acceptance:
- New wrappers preserve target token validation and emit the expected protocol commands.
- Targeted kittwm binary tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md --mode name selector
description: Add a --mode <name> selector for kittui-md so users and tools can choose any output mode by canonical flag or alias name without spelling the dedicated flag. Document it and add parser/conflict/unknown-mode tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui wm chrome: add standard animation flags
description:
Continue animation coverage for kittui scene elements that are not prompt/statusline or primitive shapes. Inventory:
- `kittui wm-chrome` renders reusable kittwm window chrome as a kittui scene but is static.
- `kittui wm-session` renders one or more window chrome scenes from SESSION_JSON but is static.
Acceptance: both commands support `--animated`, `--fps`, and `--frames`, default 60fps/180 frames/3s, add labelled phase-reactive glow layers, and have CLI scene-json/batch smoke tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-ffi: return channelized batch placement JSON
description:
Large gap: platform bindings can batch-place scenes, including at a runtime origin, but only receive one concatenated byte string. CLI users can request channelized JSON (`upload`, `placement`, `embed`, image ids, footprints), while FFI/TypeScript hosts cannot inspect, schedule, or separately write the upload/placement/embed channels.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_channels(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` returning JSON for a batch placed at a group origin.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin` and include `count`, `image_ids`, `footprints`, `upload_bytes`, `placement_bytes`, `embed_bytes`, and raw `upload`/`placement`/`embed` strings.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyChannels(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript and add tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI returns parseable JSON with channel strings and metadata for scene arrays.
- TypeScript returns a parsed object.
- Targeted FFI, ABI snapshot, and TypeScript tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add C-a g launcher shortcut
description: User request: `C-a g` should open the launcher. Add it to the default kittwm keymap and user-visible shortcut/help surfaces so it is discoverable and works alongside existing `C-a Enter`/`C-a d` launch bindings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Bound headless Chrome native test startup waits
description: Context: while validating bd-099358, cargo test -p kittui-wm native::tests --lib passed the PTY and terminal-state coverage but then hung until the harness timeout after headless_browser_data_url_screenshot_when_chrome_available had been running for over 60 seconds. The likely fix-shape is to make HeadlessBrowserApp::launch/read_devtools_port use a genuinely bounded startup wait and clean up the Chrome child on timeout, or gate the test with a faster availability probe. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-099358 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add native inspection CLI wrappers
description:
Large native-WM usability gap: kittwm has first-class CLI wrappers for automation and session save/restore, but core inspection still requires protocol strings such as `kittwm --attach -c PANES_JSON` and `SESSION_JSON`. This keeps common controller/shell workflows unnecessarily tied to raw socket verbs.

Scope:
- Add CLI flags for native socket inspection:
  - `--status-json`
  - `--panes`
  - `--panes-json`
  - `--session-json`
- Route through default socket resolution and print replies.
- Add help text, request-builder/normalization tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Flags generate the expected socket request strings.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket text injection
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: external controllers can spawn/focus/resize/move native panes, but cannot inject text into a pane through the socket. A DISPLAY-like terminal WM needs a first-class control-plane path to drive focused panes without synthesizing host TTY input.

Scope:
- Add native socket commands:
  - `SEND_TEXT <window|focused> <text>` sends UTF-8 text bytes to a pane.
  - `SEND_LINE <window|focused> <text>` sends UTF-8 text plus `\n`.
- Queue and handle commands in the native PTY session using existing pane lookup/focused semantics.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error text coverage and targeted tests.
- Document in README/docs if implemented.

Acceptance:
- Commands validate window/text arguments and return queued replies.
- Native loop sends bytes to the target pane.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: live top-bar scene text metadata
description:
Docs follow-up for live top-bar scene text metadata.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md or docs/README.md to mention that the live kittui scene chrome path carries top-bar state/text while pure terminal has ANSI fallback.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect live top-bar scene text/status behavior.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-bar: consume chrome reservation status
description:
kittwm-bar integration gap: after native STATUS_JSON exposes chrome reservation metadata and SDK typing is in progress, kittwm-bar should display/use that metadata when connected.

Scope:
- Update `kittwm-bar` model loading to consume typed chrome reservation metadata from SDK once available; if SDK bead is not landed yet, add runtime-safe fallback only after rebasing.
- Include top_bar_rows/tilable_rows in `--json` model if available, or add a nested optional chrome field through shared BarModel.
- Keep text output concise, maybe not showing metadata unless useful.
- Add tests for JSON/model shape with chrome metadata.
- Do not change daemon/runtime session behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-bar can reflect WM chrome reservation metadata from SDK/status.
- Text output remains stable/concise.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document render-many platform APIs
description:
Large gap: render-many APIs now exist in Rust, FFI, Python, TypeScript, and CLI, but DESIGN's ABI shape still only lists single-scene render_json and README does not mention binding-level render-many. Docs should reflect the platform contract.

Scope:
- Update DESIGN ABI shape to list `kittui_render_many_json` and summarize manifest fields.
- Update README platform binding bullet/quick docs to mention render-only single/batch APIs across TS/Python.
- Keep concise and run diff check.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention render_many manifests and base64 PNG entries.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs/wm.md: kittui-wm v1 architecture and operator guide
description:
Long-form doc covering: kittui-wm v1 architecture, how Xvfb is hosted, how pointer + key events flow, the compositor lifecycle, perf expectations, the WSL/Linux requirements, and the SSH operator story. Linked from README and DESIGN.md.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05

Render shortcut/help overlay as kittui components: panel/card, headings, chips for key chords, grouped rows, and translucent backdrop. It should reuse the shared shortcut catalog data but present as graphics in kittwm graphics mode. Acceptance: C-a ? shows kittui-rendered overlay with key chips and daily-driver hints; text fallback and SHORTCUTS_JSON remain unchanged.

title: kittwm: common inspection subcommand aliases
description:
Daily-driver UX source work: add easy subcommand aliases for common running-WM inspection.

Scope:
- Implement source changes (not docs-only) for easy aliases such as:
  - `kittwm status` -> existing status behavior or STATUS_JSON-friendly status output
  - `kittwm panes` -> existing `--panes`
  - `kittwm panes-json` -> existing `--panes-json`
  - `kittwm events [ms]` -> existing `--events` / `--events-ms`
- Keep existing flags working.
- Avoid overlapping `kittwm info` implementation; focus on aliases/parse dispatch.
- Add focused tests for at least three aliases.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users can discover/run common inspections as subcommands instead of remembering flags.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittwm: screenshot/artifact command for full graphical WM surface
description: Dogfood QA gap: need a way to capture the full composed kittwm graphical shell surface for review/goldens. Add a command/test harness to emit a PNG/scene artifact of a representative WM state including top bar, splits, borders, pane content, overlay, and translucency. Acceptance: one command or test fixture produces reviewable artifact; useful for visual regression and showcase screenshots.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: add native surface coverage fields to architecture contract
description: Strengthen the typed SDK architecture contract so first-party native surface entries explicitly state whether they are SDK-backed, kitty-graphics-native, and which kittui/kittwm rendering entry point they use. This makes the architecture artifact a more useful coverage matrix without touching live session, kittwm-bar implementation, or docs/help slices.

title: kittwm attach preserves daemon command arguments
description: The attach path uppercases the entire one-shot/REPL command before sending it to the daemon. That is okay for simple verbs but corrupts argument-bearing commands such as SPAWN, paths, URLs, and case-sensitive app queries. Add a helper that normalizes only the daemon verb while preserving the rest of the line, update attach help for PANES/SPAWN, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md tables mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --tables mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured HTML placeholder metadata
description: Inline/block HTML now renders as placeholders, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured HTML metadata. Add HTML metadata with kind/source, include it in JSON and metadata sections, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md reports image references in plain and rich footers
description: Markdown image placeholders are now captured as structured metadata, but kittui-md only reports links in its trailing metadata. Add image reference sections to --plain and rich status output so alt text and URLs are visible even when placeholders are off-screen. Include viewer tests for the formatter.

description: cargo test -p kittui-cache --lib intermittently fails 2/8 when run alongside other crate tests, but passes deterministically with --test-threads=1 or in isolation. Likely temp-dir race in still_round_trip / animation_round_trip. File deterministic test fixtures with unique temp dirs per test.

title: Add pluses-delimited metadata block regression coverage
description: Add targeted regression coverage that kittui-affordances/kittui-md preserve +++ pluses-delimited Markdown metadata blocks, not just YAML frontmatter. Keep changes focused to tests/docs if production code already handles it.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md math-json inspection mode
description: Add --math-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown math expressions, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/source records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.

title: kitwm runtime swap actions: Ctrl-A hjkl visible swap direction state
description: Wire keymap swap actions into live kitwm runtime state. Ctrl-A h/j/k/l should update a visible swap direction marker in the footer and log swap.left/down/up/right transitions. This implements the operator-requested Ctrl-A l swap-right family as visible behaviour before real tile reordering lands. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-c7a249.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: remap DOM roles to first-class semantic roles
description:
Follow-up after SDK added first-class semantic roles: browser DOM/ARIA adapter still maps links/pixel regions/etc. to `Custom("browser.*")` in some cases. Remap obvious cases to first-class SDK roles where available.

Scope:
- Update browser semantic role mapping to use `ComponentRole::Link` for link roles and `Canvas` for pixel/canvas/video-ish opaque regions where appropriate.
- Update tests that currently expect `Custom("browser.link")`.
- Preserve custom roles for menu_item/grid or other cases without first-class variants if needed.
- Do not change screenshot rendering or publishing behavior.

Acceptance:
- Browser semantic snapshots use new first-class SDK roles for links and canvas/pixel regions where appropriate.
- Targeted browser semantic tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md refs alias for references
description: Add --refs as a concise alias for kittui-md --references. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm doctor kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm doctor diagnostics available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add doctor-scene-json and doctor-kitty/doctor-graphics commands that render transport readiness as a labelled kittui scene, preserving existing doctor text/json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm doctor-scene-json emits a kittui Scene with labelled diagnostic layers.
- kittwm doctor-kitty/doctor-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Tests cover scene labels without probing kitty or requiring a live kittwm.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm events kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make top-level kittwm event stream inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add events-scene-json and events-kitty/events-graphics commands that render bounded EVENTS JSONL batches as a labelled kittui Scene, preserving existing events text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm events-scene-json [MS] emits a kittui Scene summarizing event count/kinds.
- kittwm events-kitty/events-graphics [MS] places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing events / --events / --events-ms behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from synthetic event lines.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive image scene helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: Python/TypeScript helpers now cover solid and gradient primitives, but embedded image scenes still require hand-authored `Node::Image` JSON. Images are a core renderer use case for shell scripts and platform previews.

Scope:
- Add Python `scene.image_layer(...)` and `scene.image_box(...)` helpers.
- Add TypeScript `scene.imageLayer(...)` and `scene.imageBox(...)` helpers.
- Support path sources and byte-array sources in JSON-compatible shape.
- Include fit and optional tint.
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS users can build valid image Scene JSON without hand-authoring schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add raw RGB medium upload helper
description:
Additive kitty transport follow-up: raw RGB (`f=24`) direct uploads exist, but file/temp/shared-memory medium helpers remain documented as only future-if-needed. Add a small medium helper mirroring raw RGBA so callers that already own/write RGB files or shm can emit `f=24` with `t=f|t=t|t=s`.

Scope:
- Add `upload_still_rgb_medium` mirroring `upload_still_rgba_medium` for `UploadMedium`.
- Emit `f=24,s=W,v=H,t=<medium>` for file/temp/shared-memory and reuse direct RGB helper for direct bytes.
- Add exact grammar tests for temp file and shared-memory names.
- Do not change renderer/kittwm defaults.

Acceptance:
- kittui-kitty supports additive raw RGB medium grammar.
- Existing RGB/RGBA/PNG tests pass.
- `cargo test -p kittui-kitty --lib` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md rich tables align cell text from Markdown markers
description: Use the preserved MarkdownTable alignment metadata in kittui-md rich table rendering so left/center/right aligned columns position their cell text within the table glyph cells. Add pure tests for alignment formatting and keep metadata JSON behavior unchanged.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix nix kittui-cli sandbox test failures
description:
Nix build regression: kittui-cli lib tests fail in sandbox.

Failures reported:
- daemon::tests::native_spawn_queue_reports_live_pane_status
- daemon::tests::native_spawn_queue_wait_text_does_not_block_ping
- session::native_pane_tests::native_pane_statuses_include_dirty_frame_metrics

Symptoms:
- WAIT_TEXT_MS timeout in nonblocking ping test.
- PTY child program `true` not found in sandbox test.

Scope:
- Make tests deterministic and sandbox-safe without weakening runtime behavior.
- Prefer direct PTY test program resolution via env/PATH fallback or test helper already used elsewhere.
- Avoid broad churn.
- Validate targeted tests and, if feasible, nix/cargo relevant build.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive position footer
description: Add an in-pager position footer for kittui-md --interactive showing source path, current offset, maximum offset, viewport rows, and total rows. Keep reload status messages and add targeted tests for footer rendering.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor terminal insert mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm implements insert-character CSI (`CSI n @`) but not terminal insert/replace mode (`CSI 4 h/l`, IRM). Editors and line-oriented TUIs can enable insert mode so printable output shifts existing cells right instead of overwriting them; without it, native pane rendering and READ_TEXT snapshots diverge from real terminals.

Scope:
- Track terminal insert mode in native `TerminalState`, default false.
- Handle `CSI 4 h` / `CSI 4 l` for set/reset insert mode (non-private mode).
- When insert mode is enabled, printable characters should insert one cell at the cursor before drawing, shifting row contents right and dropping the last cell.
- Reset insert mode during terminal resets; preserve it across resize like other modes.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves default replace behavior overwrites at cursor.
- Test proves insert mode shifts row contents right before drawing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add SurfaceSpec native readiness helpers
description: Add convenience methods on `SurfaceSpec` so SDK apps can ask whether a typed terminal/browser surface request is backed by the current first-party SDK + kitty-graphics-native contract without manually constructing `ArchitectureContract`. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser, bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md mode-info discovery
description: Add --mode-info <name> and --mode-info-json <name> no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can inspect one mode's canonical flag, aliases, description, and JSON top-level keys when applicable. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: adapt Xvfb and XQuartz backends to common Surface trait
description: Expose Xvfb/XQuartz app capture/input/resize as common kittwm Surface implementations so GUI apps can be launched and composed through the same handle model.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: JSON wait match socket and CLI wrappers
description:
Automation JSON surface gap: WAIT_TEXT/WAIT_OUTPUT return text `MATCH_*` lines while other read surfaces have JSON forms.

Scope:
- Add socket commands `WAIT_TEXT_JSON`, `WAIT_TEXT_JSON_MS`, `WAIT_OUTPUT_JSON`, and `WAIT_OUTPUT_JSON_MS` returning JSON on success with kind/window/bytes.
- Preserve existing text `WAIT_TEXT*` / `WAIT_OUTPUT*` behavior unchanged.
- Add CLI wrappers `--wait-text-json`, `--wait-text-json-ms`, `--wait-output-json`, and `--wait-output-json-ms`.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error help entries and focused tests.
- No SDK changes in this bead.

Acceptance:
- JSON wait commands return parseable JSON for matching text/output.
- Existing wait commands still return existing text form.
- Targeted kittui-cli tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: add NativeSurface exact-byte input hook
description:
Surface runtime gap: `NativeSurface` has text input but no common exact-byte input hook, even though PTY surfaces and kittwm automation support exact bytes.

Scope:
- Add `send_surface_bytes(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<()>` to `NativeSurface`.
- Default implementation should accept UTF-8 by delegating to `send_surface_text` and reject non-UTF8 bytes explicitly.
- Override for `PtyTerminalApp` to preserve exact bytes through `TerminalSurface::send_bytes`.
- Capture-only adapters remain unsupported through the default text path.
- Add focused tests for PTY byte send and default non-UTF8 rejection/capture-only unsupported behavior.
- Do not change daemon/socket behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface abstraction can represent exact-byte input where supported.
- PTY preserves raw bytes; other adapters fail explicitly as appropriate.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK semantic roles and event iterator status
description:
Docs follow-up after documented SDK semantic roles and bounded event iterator helper landed. Document new ComponentRole variants and `KittwmEventIter` / `events_iter_ms` / `event_iter_ms`.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md and docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md or docs/README.md.
- Mention adapter remapping to new roles is a follow-up where current adapters still emit Custom.
- Mention event iterator reuses existing bounded `events_ms` call and preserves daemon behavior.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect new SDK semantic role vocabulary and event iterator helper.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show tables and metadata blocks in kittui-md rich status
description: Update the kittui-md rich/interative status line so it reports table and metadata-block counts alongside the existing component/link/image/footnote/math/html/code counts. Add targeted tests and keep changes narrow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: plan adaptive graphics transport selection
description:
Performance architecture gap: kittui/kittwm currently choose between direct escape streaming / tmux passthrough / file-ish transport mostly from terminal probing and caller configuration. For high-rate WM surfaces we need an adaptive transport policy that chooses the fastest safe mode: direct stream for remote/ssh, file/tempfile or shared-memory for local terminals that support it, zlib for large raw frames when beneficial, and a tmux-safe pure terminal fallback unless explicitly overridden.

Scope:
- Add a durable design note or implementation plan for transport policy selection across kittui and kittwm.
- Define inputs: terminal capabilities, local vs SSH, tmux, kitty support, file/shm availability, payload size/frame rate, compression threshold.
- Define explicit env/config overrides and safe defaults.
- File/describe implementation follow-ups for shared-memory/file transport in raw-frame hot paths and optional compression thresholds.

Acceptance:
- In-repo docs capture automatic transport selection strategy and relation to zlib/raw-RGBA/tmux safeguards.
- Follow-up beads or TODO mapping exists for implementing shared-memory/local transport and compression thresholds.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: add package metadata and module entrypoint
description:
Large gap: the Python binding exists, but it is only importable by manually setting `PYTHONPATH`. Python platform users need minimal packaging metadata and a module entry point to discover the ABI/library path and smoke-test configuration.

Scope:
- Add `bindings/python/pyproject.toml` with package metadata for the stdlib ctypes binding.
- Add `python -m kittui` CLI entry point that prints ABI/probe JSON or library discovery information.
- Add unittest coverage for the module helper using the fake library path-discovery path where practical.
- Update README with packaging/entry-point examples.

Acceptance:
- `PYTHONPATH=bindings/python python3 -m kittui --help` works.
- `PYTHONPATH=bindings/python python3 -m unittest discover bindings/python` passes.
- No compiled shared library is required for tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: report live native panes over session socket
description:
Large gap: no-arg native `kittwm` now owns the live socket, but `STATUS`/`PANES` on that socket only report queue metadata, not the actual visible native panes. This makes the DISPLAY/socket model hard to inspect from shell scripts.

Scope:
- Extend the native session socket queue with a lightweight live pane status snapshot.
- Update the snapshot from the native session loop after split/close/socket spawn/resize/reap changes.
- Add `STATUS` and `PANES` replies that report visible native pane count and focused pane token.
- Add unit tests for status/panes formatting.

Acceptance:
- A live native session socket `STATUS` includes pending count, pane count, and focused window.
- `PANES` returns visible native pane records.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane resize weights
description:
Large gap: native kittwm pane layouts are always equal splits. A terminal WM needs basic pane resizing/weights so users and socket clients can give more space to a focused terminal without changing pane order or axis.

Scope:
- Add per-native-pane layout weights with default weight 1.
- Add socket command `RESIZE_PANE <window|focused> <grow|shrink|+N|-N>` queued into the native PTY loop.
- Make native pane layout allocation use weights for both columns and rows, with sane minimum/title-row constraints.
- Include weight in native pane status JSON/text and HELP/HELP_JSON.
- Add parser and weighted layout tests.

Acceptance:
- Socket resize command updates a pane weight and recomputes layout.
- Weighted native layout allocates more columns/rows to heavier panes.
- Targeted daemon/session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: add semantic scene/kitty output
description: Add kitty/kittui-native semantic inspection outputs to kittwm-browser: render a browser semantic snapshot through the existing kittui-wm semantic renderer as scene JSON or kitty graphics. Scope to kittwm_browser.rs only; avoid kittwm-terminal/launch/bar/session/ArchitectureContract areas currently handled by kittui-dev/dev-2.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Complete kitty graphics protocol conformance + ratakittui coverage
description: Operator-confirmed gap: kittui was supposed to be a careful extraction of the Cacophony TUI graphics lib against https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ but the showcase reproduces ENOENT image-not-found responses in Ghostty and produces only placeholder character grids. Audit shows multiple spec deviations: (1) unicode placeholders emit bare U+10EEEE with foreground color only, missing the (row, column, msb-of-image-id) combining diacritics that the spec requires for cell-to-pixel anchoring; (2) the q= quiet field is never set so kitty prints diagnostic responses inline; (3) declared Transport::File and Transport::Memory never adjust t= and still base64-stream via a=t; (4) animation upload uses a=t with r= instead of a=t for first frame then a=f for subsequent frames and the animation control verb shape is wrong; (5) placement command lacks p= placement-id support and X=/Y= subcell offset support; (6) tests assert substrings of the current malformed output instead of the protocol grammar. This epic tracks getting kittui to a verified spec-conformant baseline plus full ratakittui widget coverage proof. Child beads will land incrementally with grammar-pinned tests and a kittui proof CLI mode the operator can paste into Ghostty/kitty for visual confirmation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm native app: nested PTY terminal panes
description: Operator direction: support a native terminal app independent of X/Quartz. Implement a nested PTY backend for kittui-wm so terminal sessions can be resized, moved, composed as windows, and receive keyboard input, allowing kittwm to act as a tmux-like multiplexer as well as a graphical compositor. Acceptance: launch shell in a PTY, render terminal contents into kittui frames, resize with pane geometry, route typed input, and prove with an echo hello round trip. Parent epic: bd-031e54; directly helps bd-a9ec5b when xterm/XQuartz are unavailable. Filed from bd-a9ec5b session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: capability profile presets
description:
SDK capability ergonomics gap: `ClientCapabilities::restricted()` only allows `ReadText`, even though many low-risk inspection helpers now exist (status/panes/help/app catalog/session reads/events/semantic reads). Add clearer preset builders so SDK apps can choose local scopes without hand-assembling capability lists.

Scope:
- Add additive `ClientCapabilities` presets such as `none()`, `inspect_only()`, and `automation()` or similarly named helpers.
- Keep `restricted()` behavior compatible or delegate to an appropriate documented preset only if clearly safe.
- Add `allowed()`/`iter()` style accessor if useful for tests/docs.
- Update tests for preset contents and denials.
- Do not change daemon/runtime enforcement.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients have clearer local capability profiles.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: forward OSC 52 clipboard writes from native panes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm consumes inner PTY OSC sequences while rendering panes itself, so OSC 52 clipboard requests from apps do not reach the host terminal clipboard. A terminal WM should mediate clipboard OSC safely and transparently: inner apps should be able to set the host clipboard via Ghostty's OSC 52 support, while kittwm keeps its own pane model/rendering path.

Scope:
- Recognize OSC 52 clipboard sequences in native `TerminalState`.
- Queue sanitized OSC 52 set-clipboard sequences for host-terminal forwarding instead of rendering them or writing them back to the child PTY.
- Drain host-forwarded OSC sequences from `PtyTerminalApp` in the native session loop and write them to stdout outside pane frames.
- Preserve existing OSC title handling.
- Add focused tests for OSC 52 capture/sanitization and drain behavior.

Acceptance:
- Test proves OSC 52 with base64 payload queues a host OSC 52 sequence.
- Test proves empty/query-like payloads are ignored rather than forwarded as clipboard reads.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical launcher and picker overlays
description: Dogfood graphics gap: launcher/picker overlays should use kittui components rather than boxed ASCII. Render candidate rows, selected state, search/filter, footer hints, and translucent backdrop as kittui scenes. Acceptance: launch/picker overlays are graphical in graphics mode, restore underlying panes on close, and retain text fallback.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: honor workspace label env in chrome metadata
description:
Workspace metadata gap: native kittwm top bar, CHROME_JSON, and SDK status currently hard-code workspace "1" even though kittwm-bar already has `KITTWM_WORKSPACE` fallback.

Scope:
- Make live native session top bar and daemon chrome/status metadata use a shared/resolved workspace label from `KITTWM_WORKSPACE`, defaulting to `1`.
- Preserve current behavior when env is unset.
- Add targeted tests for env override in top-bar model and CHROME_JSON/status JSON.
- Do not implement multi-workspace runtime switching in this bead.

Acceptance:
- `KITTWM_WORKSPACE=dev kittwm` / socket status surfaces report `dev` in the top bar and chrome metadata.
- Existing default remains `1`.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md equations alias for math

title: kittwm: friendly info command
description:
Daily-driver UX: add a friendly `kittwm info` command for one-screen running-WM overview.

Scope:

- It should query `STATUS_JSON`, `CHROME_JSON`, and `PANES_JSON` from the selected socket/display and print a compact human-readable overview: socket path, running status, workspace/chrome rows, pane count/focus/layout, and pane ids/titles when available.
- If no daemon is reachable, print a helpful message with `kittwm` start hint and exit nonzero.
- Preserve existing `--status`, `--status-json`, `--panes`, `--panes-json`, `--chrome-json` behavior.
- Add focused formatting tests using sample JSON; avoid needing a live TUI session.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm info` is the obvious first inspection command for daily driver use.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown blockquotes as banner callouts
description: Fix Markdown blockquote rendering so paragraph endings inside a blockquote do not flush as normal text boxes before the blockquote closes. Track blockquote state, render quoted content as a banner/callout component, and add tests covering simple blockquotes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add HELP_JSON to standalone daemon
description:
Large gap: native kittwm sockets expose HELP_JSON, but the standalone daemon only has human-readable HELP text. This keeps the two socket/control-plane modes inconsistent for external automation and discovery.

Scope:
- Add `HELP_JSON` to the standalone daemon request handler.
- Return a machine-readable command catalog covering current standalone daemon commands (`PING`, `STATUS`, `STATUS_JSON`, `WINDOWS`, `DISPLAYS`, `APPS`, `APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST`, `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST`, `SPAWN`, `PANES`, `PANES_JSON`, `QUIT`, `HELP`, `HELP_JSON`).
- Keep existing HELP text behavior unchanged except adding HELP_JSON to the list.
- Add targeted daemon tests and docs/wm.md examples.

Acceptance:
- `client_request(server.path(), "HELP_JSON")` returns parseable JSON with a command record for `SPAWN <argv>`.
- Existing text HELP still works.
- `cargo test -p kittui-cli daemon::tests -- --nocapture` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui protocol audit: animation + absolute + relative placement reported broken; verify vs kitty spec
description: Operator report: kittui fundamentals are broken — animation, absolute placement, and relative placement all show incorrect behaviour. Audit our protocol usage end-to-end against the canonical kitty graphics protocol spec (a=t/f/d, q=2, m=, transmission, animation frames a=a, placement r/c, virtual placement U=1 + unicode placeholder, classic z-index, relative placement P/Q anchoring, deletion verbs). Compare every escape sequence we emit. Capture mismatches as child bugs and fix the worst behaviour-affecting items. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md math mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --math mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown inline and block HTML placeholders

creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical focus ring and pane selection affordances
description: Dogfood graphics gap: focus should be visually clear with kittui-rendered affordances rather than only title-row inversion/text. Add graphical focus ring, active pane glow/accent, and keyboard selection affordances that respect translucency/theme. Acceptance: focused pane is obvious in screenshots; focus changes update chrome without flicker or stale images.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md code-blocks-only mode for code extraction
description: Add a kittui-md --code-blocks mode that prints only parsed code blocks with language labels and source text. This complements --components and --metadata-json for human-readable code extraction. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish native PTY mouse reporting modes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native PTY panes do not track terminal mouse-reporting modes (`?1000`, `?1002`, `?1003`, `?1006`). kittwm needs this state before it can safely route host mouse reports into the focused/target pane, and controllers need to know whether a TUI is asking for mouse input.

Scope:
- Track native PTY mouse modes in `TerminalState`: basic mouse, button-motion, all-motion, and SGR coordinate encoding.
- Expose the state through `PtyTerminalApp`.
- Publish optional mouse mode booleans in native pane status JSON/text surfaces.
- Add parser/status tests and docs.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves `CSI ? 1000/1002/1003/1006 h/l` toggles state.
- Daemon status tests include mouse mode metadata.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-quartz: macOS virtual-display backend (CGVirtualDisplay + ScreenCaptureKit + CGEventPost)
description: Add a sibling crate kittui-quartz that implements the kittui-xvfb XServer trait against macOS Quartz. Creates a CGVirtualDisplay via the private CoreGraphics symbols (only macOS path to a real headless display without third-party drivers), captures it with ScreenCaptureKit, and routes pointer/key events via CGEventPost + Accessibility. Gated behind a 'quartz' feature; documents the private-API trade-off in docs/wm.md. Lands the XServer trait implementation now; the CGVirtualDisplayCreate FFI itself behind unsafe extern "C" symbols, with a graceful fallback to capturing the main display when virtual display creation fails. The kittui_wm_demo learns --features quartz to drive it on macOS the same way --features xvfb drives Xvfb on Linux.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-input: parse kitty mouse + key reporting
description:
New crate kittui-input that parses the kitty pointer protocol (SGR mouse 1006, motion 1003, focus 1004, hover 1006 with extensions) and CSI key reports into typed events. Includes subcell pixel coordinates where the host reports them. Exact-grammar tests for press/release/motion/scroll/drag/hover and modifier flags.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: bounded event iterator helper
description:
SDK ergonomics gap: `Kittwm::events_ms(ms)` returns a Vec after a bounded batch. Add a small iterator/stream-like wrapper over the same bounded call for clients that want to iterate events without managing the Vec directly.

Scope:
- Add an additive `Kittwm::event_iter_ms(ms)` or `events_iter_ms(ms)` helper returning an owning iterator over `KittwmEvent` backed by the existing `events_ms` call.
- Preserve current socket behavior and `events_ms` API.
- Gate with SubscribeEvents via the existing call path.
- Add targeted SDK tests for iterator behavior and capability denial if practical.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can iterate a bounded event batch ergonomically.
- Existing event APIs remain compatible.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: support stdin image bytes
description:
Large gap: `kittui image` is path-only, which weakens kittui as a shell/external-platform renderer. Scripts that generate or transform image bytes must write a temporary file before kittui can render/place them.

Scope:
- Teach `kittui image --src -` to read image bytes from stdin and build `Node::Image { src: ImageRef::Bytes { ... } }`.
- Preserve path behavior for normal paths.
- Add CLI/integration coverage that pipes a tiny PNG through stdin into `kittui image --src - --dry-run --json`.
- Update README/DESIGN examples if needed.

Acceptance:
- `printf ... | kittui image --src - -w 1 -h 1 --dry-run --json` works for valid image bytes.
- Path-based image rendering still compiles/works.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: mark raw RGB medium helper landed
description:
Docs follow-up after raw RGB medium helper landed. Update kitty protocol conformance docs to say raw RGB now covers direct and file/temp/shared-memory medium grammar, leaving visual proof/response probing as the remaining protocol work.

Scope:
- Update docs/protocol-conformance.md raw RGB/open work sections.
- Mention `upload_still_rgb_medium` and `f=24,t=f|t=t|t=s` grammar.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:

title: kittwm: translucent kittui window chrome and overlays
description: Dogfood graphics gap: window chrome, top bars, overlays, and help surfaces should demonstrate kittui translucency/alpha rather than opaque ASCII. Add translucent kittui scene chrome with alpha-blended backgrounds, focused accent, and readable text/labels. Acceptance: graphical chrome uses RGBA/translucent layers where supported, composited over pane surfaces without flicker; fallback path remains legible.

title: Demo example: kittui-wm-demo launches Xvfb + an X app

crates/kittui-cli/examples/kittui_wm_demo.rs that starts Xvfb at :99, launches xterm (or any X app), composites it as a floating kittui window in the current terminal with full pointer/keyboard routing, and shows the perf panel beside it. Documents the SSH-over-kittui story in docs/wm.md.

title: kittwm: pane control subcommand aliases

Daily-driver UX source work: add memorable pane-control subcommands.

title: animation first transmit emits redundant r=1; per-frame delay grammar should use a=t/a=f z= not a=a r=N z=
description: First animation frame uses a=t and currently includes r=1, which is redundant per spec (transmit is implicitly frame 1). Per-frame delays are also emitted as a=a,r=N,z=<ms> control commands; the canonical way is to set z= on the upload (a=t/a=f) command itself. Audit + adjust. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK JSON wait helpers
description:
Docs follow-up for SDK JSON wait match helpers.

Scope:
- After source lands, document `wait_text_match_json[_ms]` and `wait_output_match_json[_ms]` alongside existing wait helpers.
- Clarify they use JSON daemon commands and return `WaitMatch` while existing helpers remain available.
- Docs-only; no source changes.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention SDK JSON wait helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: use FFI batch placement for placeMany
description:
Large gap: FFI now exposes `kittui_place_many_json`, but the TypeScript binding's `placeMany()` still loops over `place(scene)`, crossing the JS/FFI boundary once per scene. JS/TS hosts cannot use the efficient batch renderer path.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_place_many_json` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js`.
- Change `Kittui.placeMany(scenes)` to call the batch FFI API once and return the concatenated batch bytes string.
- Add `placeManyList(scenes)` or equivalent only if needed for old per-scene array behavior; keep documented behavior aligned.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README.
- Add fake-library tests proving one batch FFI call is made.

Acceptance:
- `placeMany([scene, scene])` calls `kittui_place_many_json` once with a JSON array.
- Existing `place` and `placeAt` tests still pass.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md input format discovery outputs
description: Add --input-formats and --input-formats-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query supported input formats independently of the mode catalog. Document it and add parser/output tests.

title: kittwm: daily-driver hints in shortcut overlay

Daily-driver UX source work: enrich in-session shortcut/help output with daily-driver command hints.

Scope:
- Implement source changes (not docs-only) in the shared shortcut/help list so `C-a ?` / `kittwm shortcuts` also point to key external commands like `kittwm info`, `quickstart`, `examples`, and `cheat` without becoming too long.
- Preserve existing core keybinding rows.

Acceptance:
- In-session help gives a user a path to external daily-driver commands.

title: kitwm --launcher-query: Ctrl-A Enter launches first matching app candidate
description: Wire the searchable launcher candidate source into the live runtime launcher action. Add --launcher-query QUERY / KITTUI_WM_LAUNCH_QUERY so Ctrl-A Enter (and other launch actions) select the first matching PATH command or macOS app and launch it, falling back to KITWM_LAUNCH_CMD/xterm when unset. Log selected candidate and pid; footer still shows last launch pid. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix animation upload to use a=t/a=f frame appends and a=a control
description:
Animation upload currently uses repeated a=t,r=N which the spec does not document for appending frames. Per https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#animation the first frame uses a=t with f= and i= as usual, subsequent frames use a=f with the same i= to append, and the animation control sets gap/loop count via a=a with parameters s=, c=, z=. Audit kittui-kitty::upload_animation against the spec, fix the verb sequence, set per-frame delays via the appropriate field, add grammar tests, and ensure native looping plays without escape traffic per cycle.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Compositor multiplex: many backends, one session
description:
Generalise kittui-wm::compositor::Compositor from Compositor<S: XServer> to Compositor holding Vec<Box<dyn XServer>>. Introduce a globally-unique WindowKey = (backend_id, XWindowId) so two backends can't collide. Update hit_test, route_pointer, route_key, compose to dispatch by backend. Per-window mode + chrome theme still live in the compositor. All existing tests must keep passing.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add placement role z-index helpers
description: Add SDK helpers to resolve z-index and composition plane directly from `SurfacePlacementRole` (`ArchitectureContract::z_index_for_role`, `composition_plane_for_role`). This lets SDK apps use the typed role enum without converting back to raw plane strings. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.

title: kittwm: plan accessibility-tree semantic adapter
description:
Semantic adapter gap: arbitrary native GUI apps need pixel capture today unless toolkit/browser semantics are available. Plan the first platform accessibility-tree semantic adapter (macOS AX / Linux AT-SPI shape) that can expose labels/buttons/inputs/menus through kittwm semantic surfaces while retaining pixel fallback.

Scope:
- Add an in-repo design note for accessibility-tree semantic adapter architecture.
- Cover macOS AX and Linux AT-SPI high-level mapping into SDK ComponentRole/ActionKind.
- Define event/focus/action routing, sensitive value policy, permissions, and fallback behavior.
- File concrete implementation follow-ups for one platform spike.

Acceptance:
- Docs describe accessibility adapter approach, limitations, and security policy.
- Follow-up beads exist for implementation spikes.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose chrome reservation in status JSON
description:
SDK/runtime gap for top-bar integration: native STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON does not report the reserved chrome band or tilable area, so SDK clients like kittwm-bar cannot reason about top-clamped chrome vs tiled area.

Scope:
- Add native socket status metadata for chrome reservation: top_bar_rows, tilable_rows if inferable, and maybe workspace id (currently 1).
- Include metadata in STATUS_JSON and PANES_JSON without changing existing fields.
- Compute tilable_rows from pane/app geometry when panes exist, otherwise expose top_bar_rows/workspace only.
- Add targeted daemon tests for JSON shape.
- Do not change live session rendering.

Acceptance:
- SDK/CLI clients can inspect chrome reservation metadata from native status JSON.
- Existing status/panes JSON remains backward-compatible.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.

title: Add kittui-md footnotes-json inspection mode

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown footnote references
description: Add Markdown footnote reference handling so [^label] references are not silently dropped. Preserve references as visible placeholders in component text and add tests for footnote reference output.
