title: docs/wm.md v2: multi-backend mental model + operator workflow
description:
Rewrite docs/wm.md to lead with the multi-backend mental model: any window is local/remote/Quartz/XQuartz, rendered as homogenous kittui chrome. Include backend matrix from the design conversation, the SSH-into-Mac story, the mixed X11+macOS session example, and the limits (no Cocoa hosting without v3 CGVirtualDisplay).

Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-a6da4e
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: docs wm dot md v two rewrite

title: Wire kittui-wm NativeApp surfaces into live compositor panes
description: Follow-up to bd-03e525/bd-3f4b5d: native PTY and headless browser proof commands exist, but kittwm live session still composes XServer-backed Compositor windows only. Add a native-app compositor path that treats NativeFrame::Rgba/PNG surfaces as movable/resizable/focusable kittwm panes, routes keyboard/mouse into the focused NativeApp, and lets the launcher spawn native terminal/browser panes. Parent epic: bd-031e54. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-03e525/bd-3f4b5d session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: visual showcase smoke for graphics chrome, fonts, and split sizing
description: Create a focused visual/automated smoke path that exercises the intended kittwm showcase experience: empty workspace/top bar, split terminal panes, graphical chrome/shortcut overlay, real font terminal text, host-resize reflow, and no overlap/flicker. This can be a test, script, or doctor/check mode artifact that prevents regressions across the separate layout/chrome/font fixes. Acceptance: one command documents/runs the smoke; captures enough metadata or artifact output to verify pane bounds, graphical chrome, and real text rendering.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: make float toggle affect compositor window mode
description:
Large gap: kittwm capture-backed sessions expose `float.toggle` in the keymap/footer, but it only updates local status text. The compositor already supports tiled vs floating window modes, so the keybinding should change the focused window's real placement/chrome mode.

Scope:
- Add compositor helpers to resolve the focused-or-first window, query a window mode, and toggle the focused window between floating and tiled.
- Use the same focused-or-first logic in Scene and raw-frame composition so only one default frame/window is focused.
- Wire `Action::FloatToggle` in `session.rs` to the compositor toggle helper while preserving footer status.
- Add targeted compositor tests for mode toggling and Scene default focus uniqueness.

Acceptance:
- `float.toggle` updates the focused window's compositor mode and subsequent frames show tiled/floating mode changes.
- Raw and Scene composition both mark exactly one default focused window.
- Targeted tests plus `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render native shell chrome as kittui affordance scenes
description:
Large gap: live native kittwm shell chrome still primarily draws direct ANSI title/footer strings. Add an initial kittui/kittui-affordances scene renderer for the native shell/chrome view model so live chrome can move toward shared renderer affordances instead of bespoke ANSI-only drawing.

Scope:
- Add a renderer/helper that maps native shell pane/footer chrome metadata into kittui primitive scenes using kittui-affordances controls/components where practical.
- Keep existing ANSI/pure terminal behavior intact; this can start as a testable scene builder rather than default live renderer.
- Add tests proving focused/unfocused panes and footer produce primitive scenes.

Acceptance:
- Native shell/chrome view can be rendered to kittui Scene(s) through affordance builders.
- No high-level controls are added to kittui-core.
- Existing live renderer behavior is unchanged by default.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive links toggle
description: Add a native links toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press l to inspect document links without leaving the pager. Reuse existing link metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/link-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support native mouse drag motion events
description:
Mouse routing gap after host mouse routing: motion events are only injected when all-motion (`?1003`) is enabled and are always encoded as no-button motion. TUIs often request button-motion (`?1002`) and expect drag reports with the held button encoded (`32 + button`). Native kittwm should support drag motions without requiring all-motion.

Scope:
- Preserve the held mouse button from `InputEvent::MouseMove` when mapping host events.
- Encode `move-left`, `move-middle`, and `move-right` as SGR button-motion reports gated by `mouse_button_motion` or `mouse_all_motion`.
- Keep no-button hover `move` gated by `mouse_all_motion`.
- Add focused tests for drag/hover encoding and host-event mapping.
- Update docs for accepted `SEND_MOUSE`/routing event names if needed.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove left-drag works with button-motion mode and hover still requires all-motion.
- Targeted kittui-cli tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md outline-only mode for quick heading scans
description: Add a kittui-md --outline mode that parses Markdown and prints only the heading outline metadata, without rendering all components. This gives a fast scan/navigation view for long documents. Include parser and output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md json alias for metadata-json
description: Add --json as a concise alias for kittui-md --metadata-json. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check that the alias emits schema_version.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: add tonal panel command backed by affordances
description:
Large gap: DESIGN and module docs mention `kittui panel`, and `kittui-affordances` already provides tonal panel chrome, but the CLI still lacks a panel command. This blocks shell scripts from using the same native kittui chrome patterns that kittwm/ratakittui can use.

Scope:
- Add `kittui panel --tone assistant|tool|user -w W -h H [--animate]`.
- Build the scene through `kittui_affordances::panel_chrome(...).to_scene(...)` so CLI output matches the reusable affordance layer.
- Support `--scene-json`, `--json`, channel modes, and dry-run via existing emit path.
- Add targeted CLI tests and update README/DESIGN if needed.

Acceptance:
- `kittui panel --tone assistant -w 20 -h 4 --scene-json` emits valid Scene JSON.
- `--animate` creates a scene with animation metadata.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane mouse event injection
description:
Native mouse-control gap: kittwm now publishes which panes request mouse reporting, but controllers still have no stable native socket/CLI primitive to send mouse events to a pane. This blocks automation of TUIs that use mouse input and forces raw byte crafting.

Scope:
- Add a native socket command `SEND_MOUSE <window|focused> <event> <col> <row>` where event is `press-left`, `press-middle`, `press-right`, `release`, `move`, `scroll-up`, or `scroll-down`.
- Encode events as SGR mouse reports when the target pane has compatible mouse modes enabled (`mouse_sgr` and basic/all-motion as appropriate); return/log no-op otherwise.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --send-mouse WINDOW EVENT COL ROW`.
- Update HELP/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover SGR mouse payload encoding and disabled-mode no-op behavior.
- Daemon tests cover parsing/queueing/help.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add POSIX shm allocator for raw-frame transport
description:
Follow-up from bd-67a477. The raw-frame kitty protocol now has file/tempfile/shared-memory grammar and KITTUI_TRANSPORT=file tempfile upload, but POSIX shared-memory allocation is not wired yet. Implement a safe local shared-memory allocator for large raw RGBA frames where supported.

Scope:
- Add a local-only POSIX shm allocation path for raw RGBA uploads when policy selects shared memory.
- Keep tmux/remote defaults safe and fall back to direct/file when shm is unavailable.
- Ensure cleanup/unlink happens reliably.
- Add targeted tests for allocator naming/fallback and protocol grammar without requiring terminal support.

Acceptance:
- Local shm raw-frame upload path exists behind explicit policy/env selection.
- Unsupported platforms/failures fall back safely.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native PTY row and column split layouts
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes can split, but every split uses the same side-by-side column layout. A terminal WM needs both horizontal and vertical tiling orientations so users can choose editor-over-shell or side-by-side layouts.

Scope:
- Track a native pane layout axis in the no-arg `kittwm` session.
- Map `Ctrl-A %` / `Ctrl-A |` / `Ctrl-A v` to side-by-side columns and `Ctrl-A -` / `Ctrl-A "` / `Ctrl-A h` to stacked rows.
- Recompute per-pane title/app geometry for either axis on split, resize, close, and socket spawn.
- Update footer/docs and targeted layout tests.

Acceptance:
- Native pane layout tests cover both columns and rows.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document Python binding as first-party platform surface
description:
Large gap: README/DESIGN still describe Python bindings as future or only mention the C ABI generically, even though the first-party Python ctypes binding now exists. This makes the platform story stale for shell/external automation users.

Scope:
- Update README shipped surfaces and quick-start examples to include `bindings/python` and `python -m kittui`.
- Update DESIGN FFI/bindings section so Python is a committed first-party ctypes binding, not future work.
- Mention channelized batch placement and module discovery path.

Acceptance:
- Docs accurately describe Rust/CLI/C/TS/Python binding surfaces.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui docs: document animated render frame export
description: Follow-up after `kittui render` gained animated frame export: document the offline frame-export workflow in `docs/inline-animation.md` so users can generate PNG frame artifacts/goldens from animated Scene JSON. Acceptance: docs mention `kittui render <scene.json> --out-dir`, frame filenames, manifest metadata, and static-scene behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: expose semantic snapshot socket skeleton
description:
Follow-up from bd-911832/bd-6c4bc5. Expose the first native socket semantic snapshot/action/focus command skeletons so SDK clients have a control-plane target for semantic surfaces. Start conservatively by adapting existing terminal pane status/text into a semantic snapshot and returning explicit unsupported errors for action/focus until real semantic adapters exist.

Scope:
- Add native socket commands and HELP/HELP_JSON entries for SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT <window|focused>, SEMANTIC_ACTION <window|focused> <component> <action> <json>, and SEMANTIC_FOCUS <window|focused> <component>.
- SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT should return JSON using the SDK semantic protocol shape, with existing terminal pane text represented as a text_area/terminal-like node.
- Action/focus commands should validate shape and return clear unsupported errors rather than pretending to mutate terminal UI semantics.
- Add targeted daemon tests.

Acceptance:
- Semantic snapshot command returns stable JSON for focused/native pane.
- HELP_JSON lists semantic commands.
- Unsupported actions/focus return explicit ERR lines.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Markdown-to-kittui renderer using UI primitives
description: Implement a markdown renderer that parses markdown and emits kittui UI components rather than plain terminal text. Required blocks: headings, paragraphs/textboxes, blockquotes/callouts, lists, code blocks, thematic breaks, tables, and links. Links should render as highlighted chips. Renderer should preserve enough layout metadata to compose backgrounds/overlays using kitty placement semantics. Include tests for representative markdown and a CLI/example path to render a markdown file.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add categories to kittui-md mode discovery JSON
description: Extend kittui-md mode discovery JSON surfaces so modes carry a category such as render, inspect, json, discovery, or stats. Include categories in --modes-json, --mode-info-json, and --mode-search-json. Document it and add output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add fuzz targets and workspace integration smoke from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md calls for cargo-fuzz targets for Scene::from_json and CLI flag parsers plus a workspace integration smoke test that builds a runtime, renders a small scene, verifies PNG bytes, and checks placement escapes. Current repo has unit/golden/parity/ABI tests, but no fuzz/ directory and no root tests/it.rs smoke. Add bounded fuzz targets and a fast integration smoke that can run in CI without a real kitty terminal.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: add fuzz targets and integration tests

title: kittwm: investigate dirty-grid kitty frame updates
description:
Performance exploration: kitty's animation protocol can update image frames and may support lower-bandwidth/diff-like rendering strategies, but using it as a WM dirty-region transport could be subtle/buggy. Investigate and prototype a kittwm dirty-grid/delta frame transport that tracks changed regions per surface, updates only dirty regions or animation frames where safe, and falls back to full raw-RGBA uploads.

Scope:
- Research kitty graphics animation/frame update semantics and whether partial/delta updates are possible/safe for kittwm surfaces.
- Design a dirty-grid over native surface frames with configurable granularity and full-frame fallback.
- Prototype behind an opt-in env/config flag only.
- Include correctness tests for dirty-region detection and fallback behavior.
- Document risks around flicker, stale pixels, tmux passthrough, and terminal compatibility.

Acceptance:
- In-repo plan/prototype explains whether animation frame updates or other kitty protocol features are suitable.
- No default behavior changes unless proven safe.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: include top-bar text in scene chrome
description:
Live top-bar scene gap: the current kittui scene path uses gradient chrome but the text label is still drawn separately only in ANSI. Make the scene path carry the bar text in scene metadata/layers so the render artifact is self-describing and closer to kittwm-bar output.

Scope:
- Add top-bar text metadata/layer to the live top bar scene path where supported by existing kittui primitives/affordances.
- Keep pure terminal text fallback unchanged.
- Keep top-bar reserved band and empty workspace behavior.
- Add focused tests that the top-bar scene contains the rendered bar label or stable text-bearing metadata.
- Do not spawn kittwm-bar inside the WM.

Acceptance:
- Live top-bar scene artifact contains/identifies the actual bar text, not just empty/active state.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add SurfaceSpec placement contract
description: Add a typed SDK `SurfacePlacementContract` returned from `SurfaceSpec::placement_contract()` to package native readiness, composition plane, z-index, SDK entry, and kittui entry for a typed surface request. This gives apps one stable contract for where/how to place kitty graphics without coupling to ArchitectureContract internals. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: publish native pane geometry in socket status
description:
Large gap: native kittwm PANES_JSON reports pane identity/focus/title/weight, but not the actual terminal geometry. Socket clients cannot inspect where panes are or how weights resolved into cells, which limits external controllers and chrome/preview tools.

Scope:
- Extend native pane status with optional layout geometry: title cell rect and app cell rect.
- Publish geometry from the native PTY loop after weighted layouts are computed.
- Include geometry in PANES text/JSON and STATUS_JSON pane details without breaking existing fields.
- Add targeted tests for status serialization with geometry.

Acceptance:
- PANES_JSON includes x/y/cols/rows/app_x/app_y/app_cols/app_rows when a native session publishes layouts.
- Text PANES includes layout geometry.
- Existing native daemon/session tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md anchors inspection mode
description: Add --anchors as a focused output mode that prints heading level, anchor slug, and heading text from kittui-md outlines. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: expose native capability JSON
description: Add a lightweight `kittwm-browser --capabilities-json` inspection command that reports its SDK entry point, surface kind, kitty-graphics-native status, and kittui rendering/semantic output paths. This makes the first-party browser's SDK/kittui-native contract discoverable without launching Chrome. Scope to kittwm_browser.rs only; avoid kittwm.rs shortcuts, ArchitectureContract helpers, terminal/launch/bar/session work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: make kittwm-terminal status kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm-terminal status inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI, not only text. Add scene-json and kitty graphics status modes for the first-party SDK terminal helper while preserving existing --status text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-terminal can emit a kittui Scene JSON status card from typed SDK status/panes data.
- kittwm-terminal can place that scene through kittui Runtime as kitty graphics.
- Parser/model tests cover new modes without requiring a live kittwm socket.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm Compositor + Floating layout + LifecycleTracker integration
description:
Add Compositor type owning a kittui::Runtime, LifecycleTracker, and a window-id -> placement-id map. Adds LayoutNode::Floating { rect_px, draggable } and a per-frame composite() that turns Xvfb window snapshots into Scene::Image placements with chrome (border, glow, scanlines from the existing ratakittui design), then drains the LifecycleTracker so closed X windows emit kitty deletes.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: overlay interactions must not burn stale text into graphics surfaces
description: QA gap: launcher/help/picker overlays can leave stale text/ASCII behind if underlying image placeholders are not re-emitted or cleared. Audit overlay open/close paths for C-a ?, launcher overlay, picker, shortcuts, and future graphical overlays. Acceptance: overlay close restores underlying panes/chrome without stale cells or missing images; tests or smoke cover overlay open/close over split panes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add native socket pane move command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes can be spawned, focused, closed, renamed, and have their layout axis changed over the socket, but there is no socket/keymap way to reorder panes within the layout. As a terminal WM, pane order is core window-management state.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `MOVE_PANE <window|focused> <left|right|up|down|first|last>`.
- Queue and handle the command in the native PTY loop by reordering the pane vector and recomputing layout, preserving focus on the moved pane.
- Include the command in HELP/HELP_JSON and error text.
- Add targeted parser and pure reorder helper tests.

Acceptance:
- Socket command queues and drains a typed move command.
- Native pane move helper clamps at ends and preserves focus on the moved pane.
- `cargo test -p kittui-cli daemon::tests::native_spawn_queue_accepts_control_commands -- --nocapture`, native move helper test, and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish native surface side-effect events
description:
Runtime event gap: `kittui-wm::native::SurfaceEvent` captures title, bell, OSC52 clipboard set, and notification side effects, but the native socket `EVENTS` stream still mainly exposes status/pane/focus/layout/semantic events. Promote these surface side effects into the runtime event backlog.

Scope:
- Add a native queue API to publish drained `SurfaceEvent`s for a window.
- In the live native session loop, drain pane surface events and publish them after pane status update without changing host sequence forwarding.
- Event kinds should be stable and explicit, e.g. `surface_title_changed`, `surface_bell`, `surface_clipboard_set`, `surface_notification`.
- Include useful detail payloads while keeping clipboard payload base64 as-is and not adding clipboard read support.
- Add targeted daemon/session-adjacent tests for event JSON shape where practical.

Acceptance:
- `EVENTS [ms]` can report title/bell/clipboard/notification side effects from native panes.
- Existing host OSC52 forwarding and rendering behavior remain unchanged.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui inline chip prompt-safe zsh/bash modes
description:
Add prompt-safe inline chip output modes for interactive shells.

Scope:
- Add `--format prompt-zsh` and `--format prompt-bash` to `kittui inline chip`.
- Wrap non-printing graphics/upload/placement/cursor escapes appropriately (`%{...%}` for zsh, `\[...\]` for bash) while leaving width-bearing visible text outside wrappers.
- Preserve existing kitty/plain/ansi/tmux formats.
- Add tests proving wrappers contain graphics/control bytes and visible text remains outside.
- Validate with targeted kittui tests/build and diff check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show heading anchor count in kittui-md stats
description: Update kittui-md --stats/--summary so concise metadata output reports heading_anchors alongside headings, making the anchor feature visible in text summaries. Add targeted tests and docs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add zlib compression mode for graphics uploads
description:
Transport efficiency gap: kittui/kittwm raw kitty graphics uploads send full base64 RGBA frames, which is extremely expensive over local terminal streams and especially dangerous through tmux passthrough. Kitty graphics supports zlib compression via the `o=z` field. Add a global opt-in/auto compression mode for kitty uploads, with native kittwm using it for raw frame uploads when graphics are enabled.

Scope:
- Add zlib-compressed direct upload support to kittui-kitty for PNG/direct bytes and raw RGBA (`f=32`) payloads, using kitty `o=z` grammar.
- Add runtime/env selection for graphics compression (e.g. `KITTUI_KITTY_COMPRESSION=zlib|none|auto`).
- Use compression for native kittwm raw frame uploads outside tmux when beneficial/selected.
- Add grammar tests proving `o=z` appears and payload round-trips through zlib/base64 decode.
- Document that outside tmux graphics are emitted as terminal escape sequences by default, not socket transport, unless a file/shared-memory transport is selected elsewhere.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover zlib upload grammar for raw RGBA.
- Existing uncompressed upload grammar remains available.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: kittui-rendered pane title/status strips
description: Dogfood graphics gap: pane title/status strips should be graphical kittui components. Render title, command/process metadata, focus indicator, dirty/frame/transport status, and optional mode indicators as kittui chips/strips. Acceptance: pane title/status chrome is graphical and aligned with app area; focused state and status metadata are visible; no ASCII-only title rows in graphics mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm launch: spawn xterm or arbitrary command and report pid
description: Small launcher slice under bd-031e54 / bd-8d64b1. Add 'kitwm launch [--] [CMD ARGS...]' as a safe visible operator capability: default command is xterm, otherwise spawns the provided command, prints pid + argv, and exits. This proves launch plumbing before the in-session Mod+Return floating launcher UI. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: NativeSurface extended capability metadata
description:
Docs follow-up for extended NativeSurface capability metadata.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md, docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md, and/or docs/README.md to mention NativeSurface metadata now distinguishes text input from exact-byte input, focus notifications, and side-effect event draining.
- Clarify PTY advertises these new hooks; capture-only adapters do not.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect extended NativeSurface capability metadata.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket pane text snapshots
description:
Large terminal-WM control-plane gap: external kittwm controllers can inject text/keys but cannot read the current native PTY screen without scraping kitty output. A terminal WM needs a socket-level pane text snapshot for automation, accessibility, tests, and controller feedback loops.

Scope:
- Publish per-pane text snapshots from the native PTY loop to the in-process native socket queue without adding the full text to PANES_JSON.
- Add socket commands:
  - `READ_TEXT <window|focused>` returns a multi-line text snapshot ending with `END`.
  - `READ_TEXT_JSON <window|focused>` returns `{window, text}` JSON.
- Support `focused` using the live focused pane metadata.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error coverage and documentation examples.

Acceptance:
- READ_TEXT/READ_TEXT_JSON validate a window target and return clear errors if no pane matches.
- client_request_multi can read READ_TEXT output.
- Targeted daemon/native tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui examples: use shared animation constants
description: Follow-up after centralizing the standard animation contract: first-party examples still define local SHOWCASE_ANIMATION_* constants duplicating kittui-core/kittui constants. Replace those local constants with the shared `kittui::STANDARD_ANIMATION_*` exports so examples cannot drift. Validate showcase examples compile.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: examples: add composite app using terminal and browser surfaces
description: Add an example SDK app that spawns a terminal surface and browser/GUI surface, composes frames side-by-side, and routes input with coordinate offsets to validate reusable WM primitives.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md defaults discovery outputs
description: Add --defaults and --defaults-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query default mode, width range, stdin/file input behavior, and interactive defaults. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK typed wait results and event accessors
description:
Docs follow-up after typed wait-match results and event envelope accessors landed. Document `WaitMatchKind`, `WaitMatch`, `wait_text_match[_ms]`, `wait_output_match[_ms]`, `KittwmEvent::envelope`, `unknown_raw`, and `EventEnvelope::detail_*` helpers.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md and docs/wm.md or docs/README.md.
- Clarify raw wait helpers are preserved and typed helpers parse existing daemon replies.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect typed wait result and event accessor ergonomics.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix nix dirty-frame PTY test on Darwin
description:
Follow-up Nix Darwin sandbox failure: `session::native_pane_tests::native_pane_statuses_include_dirty_frame_metrics` still spawns a dummy PTY and fails in nix build because the test executable path is unavailable inside the PTY child spawn path.

Scope:

title: kittwm: render semantic component surfaces via kittui affordances
description:
Implementation gap: once semantic component surfaces exist, kittwm needs a renderer bridge that turns component trees into the same kittui primitives/chrome used by kittwm. This should let semantic SDK/toolkit surfaces render through pure terminal, kitty graphics, kittui scene, or remote/headless outputs without bespoke per-app drawing.

Scope:
- Define a kittwm semantic-surface renderer adapter that consumes component trees and produces kittui primitive scenes/chrome model output.
- Reuse first-party kittui-affordances controls instead of duplicating button/radio/text-input rendering in kittwm.
- Document/implement fallback behavior when a semantic node cannot be represented exactly.
- Add a small synthetic semantic surface test/example.

Acceptance:
- Synthetic component tree can render through kittui primitives with focus/selection state preserved.
- kittwm renderer bridge uses shared affordance components.
- No high-level UI controls are added to kittui-core.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittui-wm: add NativeSurface exact-byte input hook
description:
Surface runtime gap: `NativeSurface` has text input but no common exact-byte input hook, even though PTY surfaces and kittwm automation support exact bytes.

Scope:

- Default implementation should accept UTF-8 by delegating to `send_surface_text` and reject non-UTF8 bytes explicitly.
- Override for `PtyTerminalApp` to preserve exact bytes through `TerminalSurface::send_bytes`.
- Capture-only adapters remain unsupported through the default text path.
- Add focused tests for PTY byte send and default non-UTF8 rejection/capture-only unsupported behavior.
- Do not change daemon/socket behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface abstraction can represent exact-byte input where supported.
- PTY preserves raw bytes; other adapters fail explicitly as appropriate.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md about discovery outputs

title: Add kittui-md interactive reload key
description: Add a native reload key to kittui-md --interactive so users can refresh the Markdown file in-place after editing it. Document the keybinding, expose it through keybinding discovery, and add targeted tests for the pager action and reload helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes schema version

creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed layout focus and move helpers
description:
SDK control gap: socket/CLI expose layout and focus navigation operations (`FOCUS_NEXT`, `FOCUS_PREV`, `LAYOUT`, `BALANCE_PANES`, `MOVE_PANE`) but SDK clients still need raw protocol strings for these session-level controls. Add typed SDK helpers.

Scope:
- Add Kittwm helpers for focus_next, focus_prev, layout columns/rows or string, and balance_panes.
- Add SurfaceHandle::move_pane or typed move helper for left/right/up/down/first/last.
- Gate with ControlWindow capability.
- Add targeted SDK socket command-format and capability-denial tests.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:

- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Preserve Markdown link and image titles
description: Preserve pulldown-cmark link/image title attributes in kittui-affordances metadata and expose them through kittui-md JSON and focused link/image modes. Add targeted tests and docs if needed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed HELP_JSON catalog helper
description:
SDK introspection gap: native socket exposes `HELP_JSON` as a machine-readable command catalog, but SDK clients need raw protocol strings to read it. Add typed SDK helpers for the help catalog.

Scope:
- Add typed structs for `HELP_JSON` response (`HelpCatalog`, `HelpCommand` or similar).
- Add `Kittwm::help()` / `help_catalog()` helper.
- Gate with a low-risk read capability rather than raw request.

title: kitwm --attach REPL + WINDOWS/DISPLAYS protocol verbs
description: Extend daemon protocol with WINDOWS (lists Mac windows) and DISPLAYS (lists displays), and add 'kitwm --attach' that opens an interactive line-oriented REPL against the running daemon. REPL accepts PING/STATUS/WINDOWS/DISPLAYS/QUIT/help and prints replies; exits on Ctrl-D. Makes --attach real (no longer placeholder). Parent: bd-031e54 (decomposes bd-aaea73).

title: kittwm: clean empty first-launch workspace
description:
User-facing first-launch UX: default `kittwm` should open a clean empty workspace instead of immediately spawning a flickering shell pane/footer.

Scope:
- Change native kittwm default session to start with an empty workspace and a simple top bar showing current workspace id and time.
- Preserve opt-in old behavior via an env knob such as `KITTWM_STARTUP_TERMINAL=1`.
- Add a keyboard shortcut to launch the default terminal command from the empty workspace (e.g. Ctrl-A Enter / Ctrl-A t).
- Add Ctrl-A ? help overlay listing native shortcuts.
- Avoid noisy footer by default; keep concise top bar/status only.
- Keep socket spawning (`SPAWN_PTY`) working.
- Add focused tests for empty shell view/top bar/help text/terminal launch helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm` no longer starts with an automatic zsh pane by default.
- Empty workspace renders a stable top bar with workspace id and time.
- Ctrl-A ? surfaces a shortcut list.
- Shortcut launches a terminal pane from empty workspace.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add nix flake

creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm: support native PTY cursor save and restore
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm handles many cursor movement sequences but not cursor save/restore. Shell prompts and TUIs commonly use DECSC/DECRC (`ESC 7` / `ESC 8`) or SCO-style `CSI s` / `CSI u` around status updates; without support, subsequent output can land in the wrong cell and corrupt READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Track a saved cursor in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `ESC 7` / `ESC 8` and `CSI s` / `CSI u`.
- Preserve saved cursor sensibly across resize by clamping.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests demonstrate saving, moving, writing, restoring, and writing at the saved position for both ESC and CSI variants.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: kittwm text JSON read wrappers

Docs follow-up for `kittwm --read-text-json` and `--read-scrollback-json`.

Acceptance:

- `git diff --check` passes.

This helps apps and tests reason about app/chrome/overlay layering without hard-coded z-index comparisons. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.

Add a `kittui-md` (or `kittui md`) viewer binary/command that reads a Markdown file/stdin, renders it with rich kittui/kitty graphics using the UI component layer, and supports normal terminal use even outside kittwm. When launched inside kittwm it can use KITTWM_SOCKET/KITTWM_WINDOW context for future interactivity, but it must not require that context. Include navigation/scrolling plan, terminal size handling, and smoke tests.

title: Tiling layout integration: split/stack/tab + floating mix

Extend LayoutNode to allow Floating windows sitting on top of a tiled tree. Compositor renders the tiled tree first (one Scene::Image per pane), then Floating windows with z-order. Add resize and move keybindings (Mod+arrow / Mod+drag) routed through the Compositor.

title: kittwm: shell/app launch error UX for missing commands
description: QA gap: launching a missing command or invalid app should produce clear feedback in command output/status/logs and not leave ghost panes or stale pending queue entries. Cover kittwm spawn, launch aliases, apps-launch-first, and restore-session commands that reference unavailable programs. Acceptance: failures are visible/actionable, no ghost pane remains, pending/status are consistent.

title: bindings-python: add ctypes platform binding
description:
Large gap: kittui can now be used from Rust, CLI, C, and TypeScript, but Python shell/platform automation still has to hand-roll ctypes calls to the C ABI. Python is a common glue layer for terminal tooling and external platforms.

Scope:

Parse Ctrl-letter terminal bytes into ctrl-modified chars; load default or --keymap PATH; track prefix state; execute at least Ctrl-A Enter / Ctrl-A c / Ctrl-A | / Ctrl-A q visibly in the runtime. v1 can implement launch + quit and log not-yet-implemented actions, but the operator should see prefix mode and last action in the footer. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-89ae11.

title: docs: SDK exact bytes paste and mouse helper status
description:
Docs follow-up after SDK exact byte, paste, and mouse input helpers landed. Update SDK/WM docs to mention `SurfaceHandle::send_bytes`, `send_bytes_b64`, `paste_bytes`, `paste_bytes_b64`, `send_mouse`, and `MouseEvent`, all gated by SendInput.

Scope:

- Keep concise and docs-only.
- Mention the helpers map to existing `SEND_BYTES_B64`, `PASTE_BYTES_B64`, and `SEND_MOUSE` socket paths.

Acceptance:

- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: expose kittwm chrome scene command
description:
Large integration gap: kittwm has reusable `WindowChromeTheme`, but shell scripts and external render pipelines cannot ask `kittui` to build that same WM chrome as a scene/PNG. This keeps kittwm chrome separate from kittui as the renderer substrate.

Scope:
- Add `kittui wm-chrome` command that emits the kittwm window chrome scene using `kittui_wm::chrome::WindowChromeTheme`.
- Flags: `--title`, `--focused`, `--floating`, `-w/--width`, `-h/--height`.
- Support existing global modes (`--scene-json`, `--json`, render/place paths) through the normal emit pipeline.
- Add targeted CLI unit coverage.

Acceptance:
- `kittui --scene-json wm-chrome ...` produces a Scene with `wm-chrome:*:<title>` labels.
- Targeted kittui CLI tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes table layout metrics
description: Extend kittui-md --metadata-json table entries with computed column widths and footprint rows/cols from MarkdownTable. This lets downstream renderers inspect table sizing without recomputing it. Add tests for the JSON shape.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md counts inspection mode
description: Add --counts as a focused output mode that prints the same document counts exposed in metadata JSON, without full stats/source provenance or detailed arrays. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: machine-readable shortcut catalog
description:
First-launch shortcut discovery should have a machine-readable surface, not only text output / in-session overlay.

Scope:
- Add a structured native shortcut catalog in kittui-cli shared shortcut module.
- Add local CLI output via `kittwm shortcuts-json` and `kittwm --shortcuts-json`.
- Add native socket `SHORTCUTS_JSON` and include it in HELP / HELP_JSON / error help.
- Keep `kittwm shortcuts`, `--shortcuts`, and `C-a ?` text output unchanged.
- No live keybinding behavior changes.

Acceptance:
- JSON includes entries for terminal launch, help toggle, and exit shortcut.
- Targeted CLI/daemon/module tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm: flicker between frames — drop \x1b[H\x1b[J + only re-emit changed windows
description: Operator: 'image flickers between frames. should be a smooth consistent WM experience, only insides of windows are latency bound but can't cause main WM to flicker'. Cause: session::run_loop writes \x1b[H\x1b[J every frame to clear, then re-emits each window's upload+placement+placeholder. The clear wipes the previous frame before the new pixels arrive over the slow tmux passthrough, so the operator sees a black flash per frame. Fix: stop clearing the screen, kitty atomically replaces an image at the same id; only emit placement+placeholder on first frame per window or when footprint changes; never re-emit when nothing changed; track last-seen window set so removed windows get a single delete escape. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui affordances unified theme model for inline panels controls
description:
Deep audit gap: kittui now has reusable inline tokens, but broader affordance components still use separate Palette/Tone/control color logic. Create a first-class shared theme model for kittui-affordances so inline chips, panels, controls, markdown components, kittwm chrome, and kittwm-bar can all resolve from one coherent token source.

Scope:
- Define a reusable theme/token API beyond inline-only colors (surface fills, borders, highlights, focus, muted text, danger/warning/success, control states).
- Bridge existing `Palette`/`Tone`, `InlineChipColors`, panel chrome, and controls without breaking public behavior.
- Add tests proving representative components resolve from the shared theme source.
- Keep kittui-core primitive-only; this belongs in kittui-affordances or a sibling theme module.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: WM-thread architecture: per-backend Pump (capture+encode), non-blocking UI tick
description: Move capture + PNG/raw-RGB encode off the UI thread. Each attached backend becomes a Pump with its own worker thread that loops capture -> encode -> store-in-slot. MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly and returns whatever's there. UI thread keeps running input, resize, layout, hit-test at 60fps regardless of slow windows. Slow windows just show their previous frame until they catch up.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make fullscreen toggle affect compositor placement
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm exposes `fullscreen.toggle`, but it is still only footer state. As a terminal WM, fullscreen should change the focused window's real compositor placement/chrome, just like the recently wired focus and float actions.

Scope:
- Add compositor fullscreen state and helpers for focused-or-first fullscreen toggle/query.
- Add `Layout::bounds()` so fullscreen can occupy the tiled layout envelope when available.
- Make raw and Scene composition use the fullscreen target rect before tiled/floating mode.
- Carry fullscreen metadata in `RawFrame` and show it in the raw terminal chrome label.
- Wire `Action::FullscreenToggle` in `session.rs` to compositor fullscreen state while preserving footer text.
- Add targeted compositor tests for fullscreen target footprint and toggling.

Acceptance:
- `fullscreen.toggle` updates the focused window's compositor fullscreen state.
- Raw frames report fullscreen metadata and use layout bounds for focused fullscreen windows.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-bar: add first-party SDK top bar app
description:
User-facing first-launch follow-up: the empty workspace has an internal `kittui-bar`-style top bar, but we should also provide a small first-party SDK app that can render/query the same basic bar content.

Scope:
- Add a small `kittwm-bar` first-party binary in `kittui-cli`.
- It should use `kittwm-sdk` for status when a socket is available, and gracefully fall back to env/defaults when not connected.
- Print a concise one-line bar with current workspace id (initially `1`), pane count/empty state, and time.
- Include `--once` default behavior and a simple `--json` mode for machine-readable status.
- Add targeted tests for bar model/rendering without requiring a live daemon.
- Do not wire it into live session process spawning yet.

Acceptance:
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm-bar` works.
- Tests cover offline render/model behavior.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process ("server") and a thin viewer process ("client"), communicating over a Unix domain socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux's $TMUX or X11's $DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux's pane sharing) — useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock).\n\nMakes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-97fa40

Title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)

Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). Makes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-fb5d9d

Title: kittui-wm daemon: socket server + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY

New binary kittui-wm-daemon (or kittui-wm serve subcommand) that listens on a Unix socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock). Owns all backends + Pumps. Wire format: length-prefixed bincode. v1 messages: CmdListWindows, CmdTick, CmdInput(InputEvent), CmdResize { cols, rows }, CmdQuit. Daemon survives client disconnects. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-aaea73

Title: kittui-wm attach: thin viewer client

kittui-wm attach (or kittui-wm with no args + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY set) connects to the daemon socket and runs the existing run_loop as a renderer: receives scene snapshots from CmdTick replies, posts InputEvents on stdin parse, never touches backends directly. Multiple clients can attach to the same session simultaneously. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-b45025

Title: Prove: detach + reattach preserves session state

Integration test: spawn daemon with a FakeServer backend, attach client A, send pointer events, detach. Spawn client B (new process), attach to same socket, verify it sees the same session state (windows, focus, layout) immediately. Asserts the daemon outlived A and B starts at the same state, not a fresh one. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-1e1b87

Title: End-to-end SSH demo: serve here, attach from another terminal

Practical demo + script: launch kittui-wm serve on host A, ssh from host B with KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 forwarded, run kittui-wm attach. Confirm Quartz capture pixels travel over SSH and pointer events route back. Capture screenshots from both terminals to prove same-session-different-viewers. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui inline chip defaults to kitty graphics
description:
Follow-up inline chip behavior: default should be kittui/kitty graphics, not ANSI text.

User expectation:
- `kittui inline chip --text abcdef` should output inline text with a unicode placeholder at the left and enough trailing space for the chip width, e.g. `Uabcdef `, while also emitting/side-effecting kitty graphics for a pill-shaped chip background sized to text width + padding.
- Existing text-only implementation should remain available under explicit formats like ascii/plain/ansi/tmux.

Scope:
- Change default inline chip format to kitty/graphics-oriented output.
- Implement an inline kitty mode that builds a one-row chip scene using existing chip chrome and text-width sizing.
- Output should include a width-bearing placeholder/text fallback so shell layout has correct width.
- Keep `--format plain`, `--format ansi`, and `--format tmux` for non-graphics use.
- Add or rename ascii/plain as needed without breaking tests.

title: Raw RGB transmission via kitty f=32 in kittui-kitty
description: upload_still_ex grows a CaptureFormat option: Png (current default) or Rgba32 (f=32). When Rgba32, skip PNG encode and emit raw RGBA bytes base64'd directly. Update kittui::Runtime::place to accept a CaptureFormat hint on Scene or via a RuntimeBuilder option. Add an exact-grammar test that asserts the f=32 path emits a=t,f=32,s=W,v=H,m=0,q=2 followed by base64 RGBA. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kitwm launcher --select/--launch-selection: choose numbered preview row

Add --select N to highlight/print a selected candidate row and --launch-selection to spawn that selected row (PATH command directly, macOS app via open -a) and report pid. title: kittui-wm: add composite RGBA surface adapter
description:
Surface runtime gap: kittui-wm now has scene and RGBA frame NativeSurface adapters, but no small native composite surface that can combine child RGBA captures into a single frame for future child-frame present/runtime wiring.

Scope:
- Add a capture-only `CompositeFrameSurface` in `kittui_wm::native`.
- It should accept positioned RGBA child frames (or `RgbaFrameSurface` snapshots) and produce one `NativeFrame::Rgba` with simple source-over alpha blending into a fixed pixel canvas.
- Expose `SurfaceKind::Composite`, stable id/title metadata, capture-only capabilities, and frame size.
- Validate canvas dimensions and reject child placements that overflow impossible dimensions; clipping at canvas bounds is OK.
- Resize/input should return explicit unsupported errors.
- Add focused tests for metadata, composition, alpha blending, clipping, and invalid canvas rejection.
- Do not wire it into live kittwm defaults.

Acceptance:
- Runtime/composite code has a native capture surface for composing RGBA child frames.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: route browser launches through SDK browser surfaces
description: Make kittwm-launch use the typed kittwm-sdk Browser surface path instead of treating browser launches as raw terminal/PTY commands. This keeps first-party browser surfaces on the SDK native surface vocabulary and reduces duplicate launch quoting logic. Scope to kittwm-launch plus SDK helper/tests; avoid kittwm session reservation/control-plane internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-wm: add RGBA frame surface adapter
description:
Surface runtime gap: external/render-pipeline RGBA frames can be placed through kitty transport, but kittui-wm lacks a simple NativeSurface adapter for pre-rendered RGBA streams.

Scope:
- Add `RgbaFrameSurface` in `kittui_wm::native`.
- It should wrap/update a caller-provided RGBA frame, expose `SurfaceKind::Composite` or an appropriate existing kind with capture-only capabilities, stable id/title metadata, frame size, and `capture_surface()` returning `NativeFrame::Rgba`.
- Validate/update frame length against width*height*4 and reject invalid dimensions/payloads.
- Resize/input should return explicit unsupported errors.
- Add focused tests for metadata, capture, update, and invalid payload rejection.
- Do not wire it into live session defaults.

Acceptance:
- Runtime/composite code has a safe capture-only adapter for pre-rendered RGBA frames.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement deterministic Scene normalization before hashing
description: DESIGN.md says Scene::id() must hash a deterministic normalized scene: drop empty/zero-opacity layers, flatten normal composites, snap pixel rects to 1/64 px, clamp/sort gradient stops, and remove zero-width/transparent structural no-ops. Current crates/kittui-core/src/scene.rs hashes the raw serde form directly, so semantically equivalent scenes can miss the cache and produce different kitty ids. Add a normalizer in kittui-core, test id stability/equivalence with property or fixture tests, and keep serde JSON compatibility stable.

title: kittwm: policy-gated cached clipboard read
description:
Runtime policy gap: kittwm forwards nested-app OSC52 clipboard writes and emits `surface_clipboard_set`, but there is no explicit read policy or socket surface for retrieving the cached clipboard data.

Scope:
- Cache the latest OSC52 clipboard write observed through native surface events in daemon state.
- Add `CLIPBOARD_JSON` socket command that is denied by default unless `KITTWM_CLIPBOARD_READ=allow|1|true|yes` is set.
- When denied, return JSON with `allowed:false` and no payload.
- When allowed and cached, return JSON including selection, source window, payload_base64, payload byte length, and timestamp/seq metadata; do not read the host OS clipboard.
- When allowed but empty, return `available:false`.
- Add help/catalog entry and targeted daemon tests.
- Do not add SDK wrappers in this bead.

Acceptance:
- Clipboard reads are policy-gated and cache-only.
- Default behavior does not disclose clipboard payloads.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittwm: support native PTY scroll regions
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm scrolls the entire screen whenever output reaches the bottom row. Full-screen TUIs and pagers commonly set DEC scroll margins with `CSI top;bottom r` so only a viewport/body region scrolls while headers/status bars remain fixed. Without this, rendered panes and READ_TEXT snapshots corrupt status lines.

Scope:
- Track a vertical scroll region in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI top;bottom r`, including reset/defaults.
- Make newline-at-bottom scroll only the active region and keep content outside the region fixed.
- Clamp/reset margins sensibly across resize and alternate-screen creation.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test shows scrolling inside a region preserves header/footer lines.
- Test shows `CSI r` reset returns to whole-screen scrolling.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

Scope:

- Reuse existing socket/display targeting flags.

Acceptance: restored session visually matches intended tiling and status metadata.

title: Add renderer probe cache and GPU parity gating to Runtime Auto mode
description: DESIGN.md says Runtime Auto should try GPU once, run a CPU/GPU parity fixture with SSIM tolerance, persist gpu_status/gpu_adapter/gpu_parity_ssim in <cache>/probe.json, and skip future live probes until invalidated. kittui-cache has a ProbeRecord helper, but Runtime currently only lazily initializes GPU and permanently falls back within the process without parity checking or persisted probe decisions. Wire probe.json into RuntimeBuilder/Auto mode, implement probe --force invalidation, and test fallback behavior.

- Keep this in kittwm-sdk and avoid depending on kittui-wm or kittui-affordances.

title: Pump: per-backend worker thread for capture+encode

MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly; missing slots show last-known frame. SharedServer wraps Arc<dyn XServer> so a backend can be shared between the synchronous path and the pump. Update MultiCompositor::attach_async to spawn a Pump. Parent epic: bd-031e54

This helps SDK apps and first-party helpers place kitty graphics on the correct WM layer without hard-coding values. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection/helper binary/runtime/session changes.

title: kittwm: add native pane balance command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes now have weights and resize controls, but there is no first-class way to rebalance a layout back to equal weights. A terminal WM needs a quick balance/reset operation after manual resizing.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `BALANCE_PANES` queued into the native PTY loop.
- Handle it by resetting all pane weights to 1, recomputing layout, and redrawing.
- Add a local prefix keybinding (`Ctrl-A b`) for balance.
- Include command in HELP/HELP_JSON and parser tests.
- Add/extend tests for balance/reset helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- Socket `BALANCE_PANES` queues and drains a typed command.
- Native session can reset pane weights to equal values locally and over socket.
- Targeted daemon/session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Encode kitty unicode-placeholder diacritics per spec
description:
placeholder_text in crates/kittui-kitty currently emits bare U+10EEEE characters with only a \x1b[38:2:r:g:b]m foreground color set to the low 24 bits of the image id. The kitty graphics spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#unicode-placeholders) requires every placeholder cell to carry up to three combining diacritics encoding (row, column, most-significant-byte-of-image-id). Without these, kitty/Ghostty treat every cell as row 0 column 0 of the image and the placement does not anchor. Implement diacritic encoding from the spec's tables, support image ids whose top byte is non-zero, add exact-grammar unit tests, and snapshot the placeholder grid for a known fixture.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

- Validate existing behavior is unchanged.
- Add exact grammar tests, including zlib/auto behavior if helper supports compression.
- Do not alter kittwm raw RGBA hot path defaults.

Acceptance:
- `kittui-kitty` exposes an additive raw RGB upload helper with exact tests.
- Existing raw RGBA/PNG tests still pass.
- `cargo test -p kittui-kitty --lib` and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittui-xquartz: XQuartz-Xvfb backend for X11 on macOS
description:
New crate kittui-xquartz that spawns XQuartz in Xvfb mode (Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac) and connects via x11rb the same way kittui-xvfb::xvfb::XvfbServer does. Implements the XServer trait so the compositor sees XQuartz the same as any other backend. Documents the XQuartz install path (/opt/X11/bin/Xquartz, or via brew install --cask xquartz).

Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-dc80c6

Title: kittui-xquartz: spawn XQuartz in nolisten mode, attach via x11rb

New crate kittui-xquartz behind feature 'xquartz'. spawn() launches /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen $DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac or attaches to an existing :N. Implements XServer trait by delegating to kittui-xvfb's x11rb code path (extract that into a shared helper if needed). Detects XQuartz install at /opt/X11/bin or /Library/X11. Returns Unavailable if not found. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui: guard high-level placement on terminal capability support
description:
Large gap: kittui now detects terminal capabilities, but `Runtime::place` still emits kitty upload/placement/placeholder bytes even when `TerminalInfo` says kitty graphics or unicode placeholders are unsupported. That makes library/CLI hosts think output is safe when it is not.

Scope:
- Add a clear `KittuiError::UnsupportedTerminal` or equivalent for high-level placement.
- Make `Runtime::place`/`place_at` fail before rendering/uploading when `supports_kitty=false` or `supports_unicode_placeholders=false`.
- Preserve low-level raw APIs if needed, but document/guard high-level scene placement.
- Add targeted tests for both unsupported flags and an explicit supported override.

Acceptance:
- `Runtime::place` returns an error for unsupported kitty or unicode placeholders.
- Supported terminal overrides continue to place scenes.
- `cargo test -p kittui unsupported_terminal` and `cargo build -p kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: kittwm shortcuts JSON catalog
description:
Docs follow-up for machine-readable kittwm shortcut catalog.

Scope:
- After source lands, document `kittwm shortcuts-json`, `kittwm --shortcuts-json`, and socket `SHORTCUTS_JSON` near the first-launch/shortcut docs.
- Clarify text shortcuts and `C-a ?` remain unchanged.
- Docs-only; no code/runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention JSON shortcut catalog surfaces.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render pane/window chrome with kittui graphics
description: User report: native~N window chrome is disconnected from surface content and should also be rendered with kittui/kitty graphics, not plain ASCII. Implement graphical pane chrome (borders/title/status/footer as appropriate) aligned to the app surface and split geometry, using kittui components/affordances. Acceptance: focused/unfocused pane chrome is kittui-rendered in graphics mode, aligns with app_bounds and split layout, and does not overlap content; pure terminal fallback remains explicit/automatic where needed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md rejects multiple output modes
description: kittui-md currently accepts multiple output mode flags and silently lets the last one win. Add parser validation so --plain/--components/--outline/--references/--footnotes/--images/--tables/--code-blocks/--definitions/--math/--html/--stats/--metadata-json/--rich are mutually exclusive. Include parser tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: restore native sessions from manifest JSON
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: kittwm can now export a persistence-oriented `SESSION_JSON` manifest, but there is no native socket restore path. Session persistence remains half-built until a controller can hand the manifest back to the WM and have it recreate pane order, layout, titles, commands, weights, and focus.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON <json>` accepting the `SESSION_JSON` shape.
- Queue a native restore command that replaces current native panes with newly spawned PTYs from manifest commands.
- Restore layout axis, pane order, display titles, weights, and focused pane index.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage and docs example.

Acceptance:
- Daemon parser validates restore manifests and queues one restore command.
- Native loop applies the restore command and redraws.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: enforce native surface frame bounds on layout resize
description:
Native WM resize/layout bug: switching native panes between columns and rows can flicker badly because a pane may capture/render at its previous full logical size during the resize transition, while the WM places it into a smaller layout. The WM must enforce each window's frame bounds regardless of backend timing: resize the surface to its new logical cell area, but also crop/pad captured frames to the allocated pixel footprint before placement. Scaling/zooming should remain a separate future feature, not implicit pane resize behavior.

Scope:
- Add a native-session frame fitting/cropping helper for RGBA frames using current cell dimensions (8x16 today) and allocated app_cols/app_rows.
- Before kitty placement, ensure frame dimensions exactly match the allocated native pane footprint in pixels; crop overflow and pad underflow with terminal background.
- Preserve normal PTY resize calls so apps are still informed of new logical cols/rows.
- Add focused tests proving oversized frames are cropped and undersized frames are padded without scaling.
- Document resize semantics: WM reports logical size to surfaces and enforces bounds; zoom/scaling is separate future behavior.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover crop and pad behavior.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: macOS quickstart with XQuartz install + Nix integration
description:
Update docs/wm.md macOS section: brew install --cask xquartz, then cargo run --release -p kittui-cli --example kittui_wm_demo --features quartz,xquartz. Include a Nix fragment that adds /opt/X11/bin to PATH for the devshell on Darwin. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kittwm: implement native PTY erase CSI modes
description:
Large terminal fidelity gap: native PTY rendering treats all CSI `J` erase-display modes as clear-screen and all CSI `K` erase-line modes as erase-to-end. Real terminal apps use `CSI 1K`, `CSI 2K`, `CSI 0J`, and `CSI 1J`; incorrect erase semantics can corrupt native pane rendering and automation READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Implement CSI `K` modes 0/1/2: cursor-to-end, start-to-cursor, whole line.
- Implement CSI `J` modes 0/1/2: cursor-to-end-of-screen, start-of-screen-to-cursor, whole screen.
- Keep default mode semantics (`0`) unchanged.
- Add focused TerminalState parser tests.

Acceptance:
- Text snapshots reflect correct erase-line/display modes.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK app discovery helper status
description:
Docs follow-up after typed SDK app discovery helpers and launcher dogfooding landed. Update SDK/WM docs to mention `Kittwm::apps`, `app_first`, `app_launch_first`, and that `kittwm-launch` now uses typed SDK helpers for app backend discovery/launch.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md and/or docs/wm.md with concise current-state notes.
- Keep it documentation-only and avoid overlapping with SDK browser surface implementation.
- Mention capability gating at a high level: read discovery vs launch.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect typed SDK app discovery helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.

Preserve terminal fallback where required, but default visual path should use kittui graphics outside pure-terminal fallback. Acceptance: top bar and C-a ? shortcut/help overlay render as kittui scenes/components in graphics mode; text fallback remains available; tests/fixtures cover scene metadata and fallback.

title: Document narrow rustfmt workflow for kittui agents
description: Context: while implementing bd-c859be I ran cargo fmt --all and it rewrote dozens of unrelated files, requiring a manual git checkout of every non-touched path before commit. The fix-shape is to document the preferred narrow formatting command for touched Rust files or add a repo helper that formats only intended paths so agents do not create broad unrelated rustfmt churn. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-c859be session.

title: kittwm: include cursor in read-text replies
description:
Gap after publishing cursor metadata: `PANES_JSON`/`STATUS_JSON` include native cursor position, but the automation-focused `READ_TEXT`/`READ_TEXT_JSON` responses still return only text. Controllers that read text snapshots must make a second status request to pair text with cursor location.

Scope:
- Include cursor metadata in `READ_TEXT` header when available.
- Include `cursor_col` / `cursor_row` in `READ_TEXT_JSON` when available.
- Preserve existing text payload and END framing.
- Add tests and docs mention.

Acceptance:
- `READ_TEXT_JSON focused` includes cursor fields.
- Text `READ_TEXT` header includes `cursor=col,row` when known.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.

title: kittui-md proof gallery covers lists footnotes math HTML and metadata

Validate with kittui-md plain/outline/metadata-json smoke checks.

title: kittui-md reports Markdown heading outline metadata
