title: kittui-wm: add NativeSurface pointer input hook
description:
NativeSurface input gap: the common surface abstraction advertises text/key/mouse input broadly, but has no pointer/mouse hook. X/Quartz-style surfaces already have backend pointer injection.

Scope:
- Add small surface-level pointer types in `kittui_wm::native` (button + move/press/release event) without depending on kittui-input.
- Add `send_surface_pointer(&mut self, event: SurfacePointerEvent) -> Result<()>` to `NativeSurface` with explicit default unsupported error.
- Implement it for `XWindowSurface` by translating to `XPointerEvent` / `XButton` for the wrapped window.
- Leave PTY/browser/capture-only behavior unchanged for now.
- Add focused tests using a recording XServer test backend.
- Do not change live session defaults or socket behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface can model pointer input for XWindowSurface.
- Unsupported surfaces fail explicitly.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket relative focus commands
description:
Large gap: native kittwm socket clients can focus panes by explicit window token, but cannot perform relative focus navigation. Scripts/controllers first have to query PANES/PANES_JSON and compute the next window themselves, unlike the in-session keyboard focus cycle.

Scope:
- Add native socket commands `FOCUS_NEXT` and `FOCUS_PREV`.
- Drain/apply them in the native session loop using existing focus model.
- Include commands in HELP/HELP_JSON and docs.
- Add queue parsing tests and pure focus helper tests.

Acceptance:
- `FOCUS_NEXT` and `FOCUS_PREV` queue typed commands.
- Focus helper wraps correctly for multiple panes and handles empty/one-pane cases.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md HTML mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --html mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: empty workspace first-run hint
description:
Daily-driver UX: make the empty first-launch workspace self-teaching.

Scope:
- When native kittwm has zero panes, render a concise centered/visible hint in the terminal shell view explaining the first actions:
  - C-a Enter / C-a t opens a terminal
  - C-a ? shows shortcuts
  - Ctrl-] exits
  - from another shell: `kittwm quickstart` / `kittwm info`
- Preserve top bar and no-pane behavior; do not auto-spawn a terminal.
- Keep pure terminal renderer safe; scene chrome can remain unchanged unless easy.
- Add focused renderer test.

Acceptance:
- Starting `kittwm` into an empty workspace visibly teaches what to do next.
- Targeted kittui-cli session tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish native pane cursor position
description:
Large terminal automation gap: native pane status and READ_TEXT expose screen contents but not cursor position. Controllers cannot tell where a prompt/caret is, and external previews cannot draw cursor-aware chrome. The PTY parser already tracks cursor row/col, but it is not published.

Scope:
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::cursor_position()` from native terminal state.
- Add optional `cursor_col` / `cursor_row` to `NativePaneStatus`, PANES_JSON, and STATUS_JSON pane details.
- Include cursor position in text `PANES` output.
- Add tests and docs mention.

Acceptance:
- Native pane status includes cursor fields when available.
- Targeted kittui-wm/daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm daily help kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm daily-driver help surfaces available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add quickstart/examples/cheat scene-json and kitty/graphics commands that render existing text content as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm quickstart-scene-json, examples-scene-json, cheat-scene-json emit kittui Scenes summarizing the existing content.
- kittwm quickstart-kitty/examples-kitty/cheat-kitty and graphics aliases place scenes through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing quickstart/examples/cheat text output remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels for the three help surfaces.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish native pane pid and command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm status reports window/title/focus/weight/geometry, but not the underlying PTY child PID or command. Controllers cannot correlate panes with OS processes for monitoring, cleanup, or external tooling.

Scope:
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::process_id()` from portable-pty child metadata.
- Store command and pid on native panes when spawned.
- Add optional `pid` and `command` fields to `NativePaneStatus` and PANES text/JSON/status JSON.
- Add targeted tests for status serialization.

Acceptance:
- Native PANES/PANES_JSON/STATUS_JSON include command and pid when available.
- Existing native tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: implement native PTY terminal reset controls
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor terminal reset controls. Shells/TUIs can emit RIS (`ESC c`) or DECSTR soft reset (`CSI ! p`) to recover terminal state; without support, stale modes such as origin mode, disabled autowrap, hidden cursor, bracketed paste, mouse reporting, or SGR style can leak into later output and corrupt rendering/snapshots.

Scope:
- Implement full reset for `ESC c` (RIS): clear visible cells/scrollback, reset cursor/saved cursor/style, reset scroll region, and restore default terminal modes.
- Implement DECSTR soft reset for `CSI ! p`: reset modes/style/scroll region/cursor state without clearing visible text.
- Preserve existing title behavior unless a test indicates otherwise.
- Add focused parser snapshot/state tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests show RIS clears text and resets modes/style.
- Tests show DECSTR preserves text but resets modes/style and scroll region.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: expose render_json PNG bytes
description:
Large gap: the Python binding exposes placement APIs but not the lower-level `kittui_render_json` API. External Python platforms may need PNG bytes for non-terminal previews, tests, or embedding without emitting kitty placement escapes.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_render_json` and `kittui_bytes_free` in the Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.render(scene) -> bytes` that returns PNG bytes and frees the FFI byte buffer.
- Add fake-CDLL unittest coverage for render success and failure detail.
- Update Python README API list/example.

Acceptance:
- Python tests pass without a real shared library.
- `render` accepts dict/JSON scenes and returns bytes.
- FFI byte buffer ownership is released through `kittui_bytes_free`.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render live top bar via kittui scene path
description:
Live top-bar integration follow-up: kittwm-bar can emit a scene artifact, but live native session still renders the top bar internally as ANSI text rather than through the same kittui scene artifact path.

Scope:
- Reuse the kittwm-bar scene/bar rendering model from within the live session where practical, or extract a shared small model helper if needed.
- Keep the reserved top-bar band and empty workspace behavior.
- For graphics/affordance scene chrome paths, place/render the top bar as a kittui scene in the reserved band.
- Keep pure terminal renderer stable with ANSI top bar fallback.
- Add focused tests for top-bar scene metadata/placement in the reserved band.

Acceptance:
- Live top-bar chrome has a kittui scene path aligned with kittwm-bar output.
- Pure terminal fallback still works.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: reserve top-bar chrome band for tiling
description:
Live top-bar integration gap: `kittwm-bar` exists as a first-party SDK app/proof, and native session has an internal top bar, but the runtime does not yet have a clear reserved chrome band contract that clamps tiling below the bar across renderers.

Scope:
- Add an explicit native shell chrome reservation model, initially top bar rows = 1.
- Ensure pane layouts and app bounds are computed only within the tilable area below the top bar.
- Render the top bar as WM chrome in both pure terminal and graphics paths without overlapping panes.
- Keep empty workspace first-launch behavior.
- Keep `kittwm-bar` as a standalone SDK app for now; do not spawn it inside the WM in this bead.
- Add focused tests for reserved bounds, top-bar row, and pane app rows with/without panes.

Acceptance:
- Top bar is top-clamped, panes never render under it, and empty workspace remains stable.
- Targeted kittui-cli tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm apps: list launch candidates for future floating launcher
description: Add 'kitwm apps' subcommand that prints the current default launch command (KITWM_LAUNCH_CMD or xterm), whether it resolves on PATH, and a bounded list of launch candidates from PATH plus /Applications on macOS. This is the visible data-source slice for bd-8d64b1 before building the floating menu. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.

title: kittui examples: standardize animation defaults
description: Continue animation coverage into first-party examples and showcases. Inventory: `crates/kittui-cli/examples/showcase.rs` and `crates/kittui-cli/examples/ratatui_showcase.rs` still use fixed 8-frame / 800ms animation settings, unlike the new standard 60fps / 180-frame / 3s period. Acceptance: update examples to use the standard loop contract and validate examples still compile.

title: kittwm: validate status/chrome/panes geometry invariants
description: QA gap: STATUS_JSON, PANES_JSON, and CHROME_JSON should agree on workspace, top_bar_rows, tilable_rows, pane outer bounds, app bounds, focus, and layout after split/move/resize/host-resize. Add invariant tests over native session state. Acceptance: status surfaces are internally consistent and match rendered layout/chrome reservation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md interactive code-blocks toggle
description: Add a native code-blocks toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press s to inspect parsed code snippets without leaving the pager. Reuse existing code block metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/code-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime workspaces: Ctrl-A c/n/p create and switch visible workspace
description: Wire the keymap workspace actions into live kitwm runtime state. Ctrl-A c creates a new virtual workspace and switches to it; Ctrl-A n/p cycles next/prev; footer/log show current workspace index/count and last action. This is a visible foundation for configurable WM actions before true per-workspace window membership. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-5edc97.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: emit pane frame presented events
description:
Runtime event gap: native graphics rendering captures/presents pane frames but EVENTS subscribers cannot observe frame presentation without polling status/dirty metrics.

Scope:
- Emit `pane_frame_presented` from the native graphics render path after a pane frame is placed/embedded.
- Include conservative metadata only: window, renderer (`kitty`/graphics path), frame format, pixel width/height, app cell bounds, whether a new upload occurred, whether upload was skipped by dirty-frame policy, changed/total tiles if available, and elapsed capture/present microseconds when easy to measure.
- Do not emit raw frame bytes or pixel payloads.
- Preserve pure terminal rendering behavior and all existing status/dirty-frame behavior.
- Add targeted daemon/helper unit test for event JSON shape; avoid broad/integration rendering tests.

Acceptance:
- EVENTS subscribers can observe graphics frame presentation events.
- Dirty-frame skip decisions are visible as metadata, not payloads.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose native surface coverage JSON
description: Expose the SDK architecture contract's first-party native surface coverage matrix as a focused `kittwm native-surfaces-json` / `surface-coverage-json` inspection command. This gives operators and app authors a small machine-readable way to verify kittwm-terminal/browser/bar are SDK-backed and kitty-graphics-native without parsing the full architecture contract. Scope to kittwm CLI inspection only; no live session, bar implementation, Runtime/browser, or docs/help slices.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui affordances: animate top-level panel/chip/divider/title-bar
description:
Continue animation coverage beyond `kittui inline` into first-party top-level kittui affordance elements. Inventory:
- `kittui panel` currently has legacy `--animate` with fixed 8@800 pulse, not the new `--animated --fps --frames` contract.
- `kittui chip`, `kittui divider`, and `kittui title-bar` currently render static chrome scenes only.
- `kittui box` has older `--animate frames@cycle_ms`; keep it for compatibility but align first-party affordances with the new all-frames-up-front kitty-native animation controls.
Acceptance: panel/chip/divider/title-bar support `--animated`, `--fps`, and `--frames`, default to 60fps/180 frames/3s, add phase-reactive effect layers, and have scene-json tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: map SurfaceSpec to native surface contract
description: Add SDK helpers that connect typed `SurfaceSpec` requests to the architecture contract's first-party native surface coverage matrix. Apps should be able to ask `ArchitectureContract::native_surface_for_spec(&spec)` to verify that a terminal/browser request maps to an SDK-backed kitty-graphics-native kittui path. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/session/bar changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Document kittui-md links mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --links mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.

title: Show link and image titles in kittui-md plain metadata
description: Update kittui-md --plain metadata sections so link/image title attributes appear there too, matching --links, --images, --references, and metadata JSON. Add targeted tests.

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown emphasis and strong markers
description: Preserve inline emphasis and strong styling cues in kittui-md component text by carrying *...* and **...** markers through the Markdown renderer. Add tests for emphasis, strong, and nested plain text behavior while keeping link/list rendering intact.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: quickstart command for first-run daily-driver use
description:
Daily-driver UX: add a first-run `kittwm quickstart` command.

Scope:
- Add cooked-mode `kittwm quickstart` subcommand that prints a concise first-run tutorial/checklist.
- It should explain: start `kittwm`, launch terminal with C-a Enter/C-a t, use `kittwm info`, spawn/read/type/wait aliases, pane control aliases, save/restore, and where to get focused help.
- Include a short "if a WM is already running" section with safe commands only.
- Keep it presentation-only; no daemon/session behavior changes.
- Add focused test for major lines.

Acceptance:
- New users can run `kittwm quickstart` and see the path to using kittwm as a daily driver.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add send-file byte injection wrapper
description:
Large automation usability gap: `SEND_BYTES_B64` can inject exact bytes, but users still have to run an external base64 encoder and quote the result. Pasting files/scripts/binary payloads into native panes should be first-class from the kittwm CLI.

Scope:
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --send-file <window|focused> <path|->`.
- Read bytes from file or stdin, base64 encode internally, and send `SEND_BYTES_B64` via the default socket.
- Preserve exact bytes; reject invalid window tokens.
- Add help text, request-builder tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- `--send-file focused -` can encode stdin bytes into a SEND_BYTES_B64 request.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm panes kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm pane inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add panes-scene-json and panes-kitty/panes-graphics commands that render PANES_JSON as a labelled kittui Scene, preserving existing panes/panes-json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm panes-scene-json emits a kittui Scene with pane/focus/layout labels.
- kittwm panes-kitty/panes-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing panes/panes-json behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from synthetic PANES_JSON.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: refresh SDK plan current-state gaps
description:
Docs drift: docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md still lists several missing/immature items that have advanced significantly (probe diagnostics, semantic adapters, SDK helper coverage). Refresh current-state and remaining-gap language while preserving real future work like direct platform bindings, dedicated browser/X/Quartz protocols, and runtime-issued credentials.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md only.
- Keep docs-only; no architecture churn beyond status accuracy.
- Clarify what is now landed vs what remains.

Acceptance:
- SDK plan status matches current implementation and no longer overstates missing SDK helper/event/probe gaps.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: add semantic surfaces quickstart
description:
Documentation gap after the semantic surface stack landed. Add an end-to-end semantic surfaces quickstart tying together SDK protocol types, SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT/PUBLISH/ACTION/FOCUS socket commands, kittwm CLI wrappers, and the synthetic semantic app example.

Scope:
- Add docs explaining the current semantic surface pipeline and limitations.
- Include examples for printing a synthetic snapshot, publishing it, reading it back, and using SDK helpers.
- Document fallback PTY text-area snapshots and unsupported action/focus behavior.
- Link from docs/wm.md and/or docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md.

Acceptance:
- In-repo docs provide a concrete semantic quickstart workflow.
- Clearly states current limitations and next steps.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal: mature standalone SDK terminal app
description:
First-party app gap: `kittwm-terminal` exists as a skeleton, but it does not yet behave like a mature standalone first-party terminal app. Add typed status/event awareness and useful lifecycle behavior so it dogfoods the SDK rather than just spawning a PTY.

Scope:
- Audit current `kittwm-terminal` skeleton.
- Add status/event feedback using typed SDK panes/events where useful.
- Improve CLI/help around replace/new-window/title/command behavior.
- Keep the built-in terminal path unchanged.
- Add targeted tests for argument parsing/request construction.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-terminal is a more useful standalone SDK consumer.
- No regressions to built-in native terminal session.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: browser semantic CLI inspection mode
description:
Docs follow-up after browser semantic CLI inspection landed. Document `kittwm-browser --semantic-snapshot` / `--print-semantic`, compact/pretty JSON output, and relationship to default screenshot/publish behavior.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-browser-semantic-adapter.md and docs/README.md as needed.
- Include a concise CLI example for printing a semantic snapshot without a running WM socket.
- Clarify default render/publish behavior is unchanged.
- Mention this as a debugging/introspection path alongside publish/action routing.

Acceptance:
- Browser semantic docs reflect the new CLI inspection mode.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document rustfmt baseline or add touched-file formatting check
description: context: while validating bd-a9ec5b/bd-2a0133, cargo fmt --check reported formatting diffs across many unrelated existing files, making it noisy as a pre-reintegration check for a narrow kittwm fix. Suggested fix: either rustfmt the repo once or document/use a touched-file rustfmt check in AGENTS/SPEC so workers do not accidentally create huge formatting diffs. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-a9ec5b/bd-2a0133 session.

title: Showcase: cover every protocol mode end-to-end
description:
Rewrite crates/kittui-cli/examples/showcase.rs to walk the full protocol surface: Direct + Tmux + File + Memory transports; still + animated scenes; unicode-placeholder + absolute placement; multiple placements of the same scene; subcell offsets; delete + replace lifecycle; HiDPI cell-size override. Each section should be labelled and visually distinct in Ghostty/kitty. Verify against the kitty spec sections it exercises.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Multi-display enumeration in QuartzServer
description:
Add QuartzServer::displays() returning every connected CGDirectDisplayID with its CGRect bounds, scale factor, and a friendly name. Compositor exposes a way to spawn a capture target per display. Useful when the operator's Mac has multiple monitors and they want to mirror only one.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document macOS AX semantic adapter status
description:
Documentation follow-up after macOS AX semantic adapter spike lands. Update accessibility semantic docs and docs map with current macOS AX adapter status and permission diagnostics.

Scope:

- Explain that unsafe platform bindings / full live AX capture remain future work if applicable.
- Link the implementation status from docs/README.md.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect current macOS AX spike status.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: add KittuiSceneSurface adapter
description:
Surface runtime gap: kittui scenes are renderable primitives but are not exposed as first-class NativeSurface adapters in kittui-wm.

Scope:
- Add a small `KittuiSceneSurface` adapter in `kittui_wm::native`.
- It should wrap a `kittui::Scene`, expose `SurfaceKind::KittuiScene`, capture-only capabilities, stable id/title metadata, frame dimensions, and `capture_surface()` returning a PNG `NativeFrame` via the CPU renderer.
- Input/resize should return explicit unsupported errors rather than silently mutating scene geometry.
- Add focused unit tests for metadata and capture shape.
- Do not wire it into live kittwm session defaults yet.

Acceptance:
- kittui scenes can be treated as native capture surfaces by runtime/composite code.
- Tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: latency + bandwidth under SSH
description: Measure round-trip latency (input event to next frame back) over loopback SSH; document in docs/wm.md. Set a soft target of <200ms RTT on a same-machine SSH session. File a perf bead if exceeded. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui inline prompt status examples command
description:
Add runnable inline prompt/status examples and smoke command.

Scope:
- Add source-level `kittui inline examples` (or similar) that prints copy/paste examples for zsh, bash, and tmux statusline usage.
- Include examples for kitty/default graphics, prompt-safe modes, tmux format fallback, and plain/ANSI fallback.
- Add smoke tests for important lines and ensure examples stay in sync with supported modes.
- This is a CLI feature, not docs-only.
- Validate targeted kittui tests/build and diff check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: align docs map with refreshed SDK plan
description:
Docs follow-up after SDK plan current-state refresh. Update docs/README.md map/status if needed so it points readers to the refreshed SDK plan and summarizes remaining SDK/runtime gaps accurately.

Scope:

- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:

- `git diff --check` passes.

title: Add kittui-md exit code discovery outputs
description: Add --exit-codes and --exit-codes-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query exit code meanings independently of README text. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete terminal transport probing and File/Memory transfer modes
description: DESIGN.md specifies TerminalInfo::detect(), automatic tmux detection, and kitty File/Memory transports (`a=t,t=f` and `a=t,t=s`) in addition to Direct/TmuxPassthrough. Current TerminalInfo only provides defaults/overrides, and kittui-kitty treats File/Memory like direct chunked base64. Implement environment probing over TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/KITTY_WINDOW_ID/KITTY_PUBLIC_KEY/TMUX/WT_SESSION, CLI transport override, file-transfer emission, shared-memory transfer where supported with safe fallback, and tests for all transport encodings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor DEC auto-wrap mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm approximates line wrapping but does not honor DEC auto-wrap mode (`CSI ? 7 h/l`). Some terminal apps disable wrapping while drawing fixed-width regions/status cells; native panes currently still wrap when additional printable characters arrive past the right edge, corrupting the next line and READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Track DEC auto-wrap mode in native `TerminalState`, default enabled.
- Handle `CSI ? 7 h` / `CSI ? 7 l`.
- When auto-wrap is disabled, printable characters at/past the last column should overwrite/clamp at the last column instead of moving to the next line.
- Preserve existing default wrapping behavior.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves default wrap still advances to next line.
- Test proves disabled auto-wrap keeps writes on the last column.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md limits discovery outputs
description: Add --limits and --limits-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query numeric limits such as width bounds, terminal default clamps, and offset minimum independently of defaults. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor native PTY focus reporting
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: kittwm runs multiple native PTY panes but does not honor terminal focus reporting (`CSI ? 1004 h/l`). Terminal apps that enable focus events expect `ESC[I` on focus and `ESC[O` on blur; without this, nested shells/editors cannot react when the WM changes focused pane.

Scope:
- Track focus-reporting mode in native `TerminalState` and expose it through `PtyTerminalApp`.
- When native WM focus changes (keyboard and socket focus commands, close/remove fallback, spawn focus), send focus-out to the previous focused pane and focus-in to the new pane only when the app has enabled focus reporting.
- Add focused tests for mode tracking and focus payload generation.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves `CSI ? 1004 h/l` tracking.
- Unit test proves focus payloads are emitted only for enabled reporting and only on focus changes.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: HiDPI and pixel-density contract for graphical WM surfaces
description: Dogfood graphics gap: kittwm should be constant in pixel resolution/DPI and re-render at correct dimensions, not scale old bitmaps. Define/enforce cell size, pixel ratio, and surface frame dimensions for app frames and chrome scenes across host resize/HiDPI. Acceptance: graphical chrome/app surfaces regenerate at current pixel dimensions; no blurred/scaled bitmap after host resize; tests validate pixel dimensions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md mode search discovery
description: Add --mode-search <query> and --mode-search-json <query> no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can find modes by flag, alias, or description. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md stats-json inspection mode
description: Add --stats-json as a compact machine-readable counterpart to --stats/--summary, emitting schema_version plus source, render, and counts records without full metadata detail. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: dogfood kittui controls in settings/help panels
description: Dogfood opportunity: include actual kittui controls (buttons, toggles, chips, text inputs) in help/settings panels for renderer/theme/layout options. This validates component rendering and interaction inside kittwm. Acceptance: at least one in-session panel renders interactive kittui controls and routes keyboard actions through existing command paths.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add native pane balance command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes now have weights and resize controls, but there is no first-class way to rebalance a layout back to equal weights. A terminal WM needs a quick balance/reset operation after manual resizing.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `BALANCE_PANES` queued into the native PTY loop.
- Handle it by resetting all pane weights to 1, recomputing layout, and redrawing.
- Add a local prefix keybinding (`Ctrl-A b`) for balance.
- Include command in HELP/HELP_JSON and parser tests.
- Add/extend tests for balance/reset helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- Socket `BALANCE_PANES` queues and drains a typed command.
- Native session can reset pane weights to equal values locally and over socket.
- Targeted daemon/session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: answer basic terminal DSR and CPR queries
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm parses app output but does not answer basic Device Status Report queries. Many TUIs probe `CSI 5 n` and `CSI 6 n` expecting terminal OK / cursor-position responses; without CPR, apps can block, mis-detect cursor placement, or fall back to degraded terminal paths.

Scope:
- Add a pending terminal-response path from `TerminalState` back to the PTY writer.
- Handle `CSI 5 n` with `ESC[0n`.
- Handle `CSI 6 n` with `ESC[row;colR` using current 1-based cursor coordinates.
- Ensure the reader loop flushes responses to the child PTY promptly while preserving existing socket/send paths.
- Add focused unit tests for DSR/CPR response bytes.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove `CSI 5n` and `CSI 6n` queue the correct response bytes.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose adaptive graphics transport diagnostics
description: Expose adaptive graphics transport diagnostics in kittui/kittwm. Scope: report the selected transport, compression mode, fallback reason, local-vs-remote/tmux classification, and override source through kittwm inspection surfaces and a testable policy selector. Follow-up from bd-3c0dd1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: add documented semantic role variants
description:
Semantic protocol gap: docs describe common semantic roles such as link, heading, paragraph, code, image/canvas/terminal/browser document, list/list item/tree/tree item/row/cell, but `kittwm-sdk::ComponentRole` still maps many of these to `Custom(...)` in adapters. Add first-class SDK role variants for these documented roles while preserving Custom for vendor-specific types.

Scope:
- Add additive `ComponentRole` variants for common documented roles not yet present: Link, Heading, Paragraph, Code, Image, Canvas, Terminal, BrowserDocument, List, ListItem, Tree, TreeItem, Row, Cell (adjust if some already exist).
- Update serde tests to verify snake_case shape.
- Do not change daemon behavior; adapter remapping can be a follow-up if needed.
- Keep `kittui-core` untouched.

Acceptance:
- SDK semantic role enum better matches semantic protocol docs.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: wire swap actions to compositor z-order
description:
Large gap: swap/raise-style keybindings in capture-backed kittwm still only update footer counters. Now that hit-testing has deterministic render order, the compositor should expose real z-order operations so keyboard actions can raise/lower the focused window.

Scope:
- Add compositor z-order overrides used by raw and Scene composition order.
- Add helpers to raise/lower the focused-or-first window one step or to top/bottom.
- Wire swap keymap actions in session: right/down raise one step, left/up lower one step while preserving footer text.
- Add targeted tests proving composition order and hit-testing change after raise/lower.

Acceptance:
- Swap keybindings affect actual render/hit-test z-order in capture-backed sessions.
- `raise_focused`/`lower_focused` wrap/clamp deterministically.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: use dirty grid to skip unchanged raw frame uploads
description:
Follow-up from bd-510a36. Use the new kittui-wm dirty-grid helper to skip raw kitty frame uploads when a bounded RGBA frame is byte-identical at tile level. Keep behavior conservative: no partial updates and no visual changes when pixels differ.

Scope:
- Add opt-in `KITTWM_DIRTY_FRAMES=skip-unchanged` (or equivalent renderer policy) around the native raw-frame path.
- Maintain current full-frame delete+upload behavior whenever any tile changed or dimensions changed.
- Add tests proving identical frames skip upload and changed frames still upload normally.
- Keep tmux pure-terminal default unchanged.

Acceptance:
- Opt-in unchanged-frame skipping works for raw RGBA frames.
- No dirty-rectangle/overlay behavior is enabled by default.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md frontmatter inspection alias
description: Add --frontmatter as a user-friendly alias for the existing --metadata-blocks inspection mode. Document the alias and test parsing/output-mode conflict behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document macOS XQuartz and xterm prerequisites for kittwm proof
description: context: while working bd-a9ec5b and bd-2a0133 on ms-mac, the host lacked /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz and xterm, so the new XQuartz proof harness could only compile and skip. Suggested fix: document install prerequisites and/or provide a nix/devenv path for macOS XQuartz/xterm smoke testing. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-a9ec5b/bd-2a0133 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix kittwm frame deletion flicker
description: Operator reported visible flicker after recent old-frame deletion changes: every second frame appears lost when running kittwm. Investigate frame deletion/presentation path and make deletion non-destructive/stable. Filed from direct operator report during dev-2 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm keymap config language with tmux-style Ctrl-A defaults
description: Expose a first keybinding configuration language for kitwm. v1 should parse a simple text format mapping key chords to WM actions, ship tmux-like defaults with Ctrl-A prefix (workspace-create, split-vertical-launcher, swap-right, focus hjkl, launch), and provide a visible 'kitwm keymap' subcommand to print the resolved default/configured bindings. This is the config foundation before wiring every action into runtime. Parent: bd-031e54; related to launcher/keybinding work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: theme tokens for graphical shell chrome
description: Dogfood/theming gap: graphical WM chrome needs shared theme tokens for background, translucent panels, focused/unfocused borders, title strips, key chips, warnings, and typography. Define reusable theme model for kittwm shell chrome and apply to top bar, pane chrome, overlays. Acceptance: one theme source drives chrome scenes; easy to adjust alpha/colors; docs/tests cover token usage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: add render-only PNG command
description:
Large gap: kittui now has a true render-only PNG API in Rust/FFI/Python, but shell users still cannot ask the `kittui` CLI to render scene JSON to PNG bytes without emitting kitty placement escapes. That limits scripts and external tools that want previews/artifacts instead of terminal placement.

Scope:
- Add `kittui render <scene.json|-> [--out path]` to render a single Scene JSON to PNG bytes via `Runtime::render_png`.
- Write PNG to stdout by default or to `--out`; support global `--json`/`--dry-run` metadata with byte counts and output path.
- Reject scene-array batch input with a clear error for v1.
- Add integration tests for stdin scene -> PNG file and JSON metadata.

Acceptance:
- Render command output begins with PNG signature and contains no kitty escapes.
- It does not require terminal placement support.
- Targeted CLI tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON mode for outlines links and images
description: Add a kittui-md --metadata-json mode that parses Markdown and emits structured JSON for component counts, heading outline, links, images, and tables without rendering rich/plain components. Include tests for stable JSON shape.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md references mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include --references mode alongside plain/rich/outline/metadata-json/interactive, with an example command and one-line explanation. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: native pane status convenience accessors
description:
SDK ergonomics gap: `NativePaneDetail` contains many optional mode/status fields, but SDK clients must inspect raw Options directly. Add small ergonomic helpers for common pane capabilities/modes.

Scope:
- Add methods on `NativePaneDetail` for cursor tuple, app bounds, has_mouse_reporting/sgr/button/all motion, bracketed paste, application cursor keys, and dirty/transport presence where useful.
- Keep methods pure and additive; no daemon behavior changes.
- Add targeted unit tests over existing rich status JSON fixture.

Acceptance:
- SDK consumers have convenient typed accessors for native pane status/mode data.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: wrap semantic snapshot and action commands
description:
Follow-up from bd-6c4bc5 and bd-502737. Add SDK wrapper methods for the new semantic socket skeleton so clients can request semantic snapshots and receive explicit unsupported errors for semantic action/focus through typed APIs.

Scope:
- Add SurfaceHandle::semantic_snapshot() -> SemanticSurfaceSnapshot using SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT.
- Add SurfaceHandle::semantic_action(component, action, payload) and semantic_focus(component) wrappers.
- Enforce ReadSemanticTree and InvokeSemanticAction capabilities before socket I/O.
- Add tests proving command strings/capability denial using no-socket paths or a minimal test server if needed.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can call semantic snapshot/action/focus methods.
- Capabilities deny these operations before I/O when scoped out.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add connect and window handle skeleton
description: Add a kittwm-sdk crate with connect_from_env, typed socket targeting, WindowHandle identifiers, create/replace-current request skeletons, and initial docs/tests over the existing socket transport.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane move keybindings
description:
Large gap: native kittwm pane reordering exists over the socket (`MOVE_PANE`), but the local terminal WM keymap still cannot move the focused pane. Operators should be able to reorder panes without leaving the session.

Scope:
- Add native prefix keybindings: `Ctrl-A [` / `Ctrl-A ,` moves focused pane left/up, `Ctrl-A ]` / `Ctrl-A .` moves it right/down.
- Recompute layout, preserve focus on moved pane, and redraw.
- Update footer hint and native pane helper tests where useful.

Acceptance:
- Local move keybindings reorder the pane vector using existing move-target helper.
- Focus remains on the moved pane.
- Targeted native helper test and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm v1: Xvfb-backed WM rendering app windows as floating/tiling chrome
description: Operator direction: kittui-wm becomes a real terminal window manager that hosts Xvfb (or per-app Xephyr) in the agent process tree, captures each X window's framebuffer through SHM, composites them as floating or tiling kittui chrome inside the user's terminal, and routes kitty pointer/keyboard events back to the X server via XTestFake* so the hosted apps respond to clicks and keys. This is the substrate that turns kittui into a full WM over SSH: PATH inherits, env inherits, so anything spawned inside the nested X session picks up the operator's environment. v1 scope: (1) kittui-input parses kitty's mouse + key reporting; (2) kittui-xvfb spawns Xvfb, claims a DISPLAY, captures the root window via MIT-SHM and exposes per-window rects; (3) kittui-wm gains a Compositor that owns a kittui::Runtime + LifecycleTracker, places one Image scene per X window, and feeds pointer events back through XTest; (4) tiling and floating layouts implemented on top of LayoutNode::{Split,Stack,Tab,Floating}; (5) demo example that launches Xvfb + a small X app and renders it as a kittui window. Out of scope for v1: Wayland support, GPU passthrough, raw RGB(A) transmission, multi-display.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: restore Ctrl-C handling with triple-Ctrl-C exit guard
description: User report: Ctrl-C is not being captured/handled correctly. Desired behavior: Ctrl-C should normally be forwarded to the focused internal app/PTY, and kittwm should exit only after three Ctrl-C presses in a short window because nested apps may need one or two Ctrl-C presses themselves. Audit current raw input/keymap path and restore/implement triple-Ctrl-C guard without starving apps of Ctrl-C. Acceptance: single/double Ctrl-C are delivered to focused pane; third Ctrl-C within timeout exits kittwm; timeout resets counter; focused tests cover forwarding and exit guard.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md --mode name selector
description: Add a --mode <name> selector for kittui-md so users and tools can choose any output mode by canonical flag or alias name without spelling the dedicated flag. Document it and add parser/conflict/unknown-mode tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: implement native PTY index and reverse-index controls
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not implement terminal index/reverse-index controls (`ESC D`, `ESC E`, `ESC M` and C1 NEL/RI equivalents). TUIs and prompts use reverse index to insert/scroll at the top of a scroll region; missing it can corrupt snapshots and fixed headers.

Scope:
- Add index/next-line/reverse-index helpers to native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `ESC D` (IND), `ESC E` (NEL), `ESC M` (RI) plus C1 `0x84`, `0x85`, `0x8d` if surfaced by vte execute.
- Reverse index should scroll the active region down when the cursor is at `scroll_top`; otherwise move cursor up.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with and without scroll regions.

Acceptance:
- Tests show reverse-index inside a scroll region preserves outside lines and scrolls region downward.
- Tests show next-line moves to column 0 on the next line.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC origin mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap after scroll-region support: native kittwm does not honor DEC origin mode (`CSI ? 6 h/l`). TUIs that set scroll margins often enable origin mode so cursor addressing is relative to the scroll region; without it, writes land in the wrong rows and corrupt fixed headers/status lines.

Scope:
- Track DEC origin mode in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI ? 6 h` / `CSI ? 6 l`, resetting cursor to home on toggle.
- When origin mode is enabled, make `CSI H/f` row coordinates relative to `scroll_top` and clamped within `scroll_top..scroll_bottom`.
- Reset origin mode on full terminal reset if such helper exists; otherwise preserve existing behavior narrowly.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with scroll region + origin mode.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `CSI 1;1H` inside origin mode writes at scroll-region top, not absolute row 0.
- Tests show disabling origin mode restores absolute addressing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: App launcher inside kittui-wm: a kittui floating menu to spawn apps
description: Hotkey (Mod+Return by default) opens a kittui floating launcher: a text input + matching app list pulled from common locations ($PATH binaries via 'compgen -c' equivalent, /Applications/*.app, /opt/homebrew/bin, /nix/store/mlrgd5zljyrxl15ryn31w15w48f6yii5-cargo-1.94.1/share:/nix/store/c9vv24f9bdnhnms10nwijn6q8wjz00ak-just-1.51.0/share:/nix/store/cn0f42d8p48g718nh2zsgl96zjx207hi-nixfmt-1.2.0/share:/nix/store/7693fhfqg2hddwl3kbbpinrdqdp71nq2-pkg-config-wrapper-0.29.2/share:/nix/store/xvmhkpvfvmy4sfdkqwg9inq3qkpnx81b-libiconv-109.100.2/share:/Users/harryaskham/.nix-profile/share:/run/current-system/sw/share:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/share/applications). Selecting an entry spawns the app under the currently-active backend (Xvfb/XQuartz for X apps; Quartz capture mode for native Mac apps). v1 supports launching X apps inside XQuartz. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: remap accessibility roles to first-class SDK roles
description:
Follow-up after SDK added first-class semantic roles: accessibility adapter still collapses headings to Label and many document/list/tree/media roles to Custom or SelectList. Remap obvious accessibility role strings to first-class SDK roles.

Scope:

- Preserve existing control mappings and `Custom("accessibility.*")` fallback.
- Update/add targeted `kittui-wm` accessibility tests.
- Do not add platform FFI or change action routing behavior.

Acceptance:
- Accessibility semantic snapshots use first-class SDK roles for obvious document/list/tree/media roles.
- Targeted accessibility tests and `git diff --check` pass.

title: kittwm: plan semantic component surface protocol

Architecture gap: kittwm can model pixel/terminal surfaces, but not semantic UI surfaces from toolkit/accessibility/framework adapters. Capture the component protocol and runtime model needed for future Qt/GTK/browser/accessibility/native SDK adapters to publish labels, buttons, radio groups, text inputs, menus, split panes, etc. as kittwm/kittui-native UI rather than pixels.

Scope:
- Add a durable docs plan for SemanticSurface / ComponentTree / action IDs / focus / layout / event routing / renderer mapping.
- Explain fallback hierarchy: semantic component surface when available, terminal/cell surface, pixel capture otherwise.
- Include implications for pure terminal, kitty graphics, kittui scene, and remote streaming renderers.
- Map follow-on beads for one proof semantic adapter and for SDK transport types.

Acceptance:
- In-repo doc section or new doc defines semantic surface model and component protocol enough to drive implementation.
- Clearly states that arbitrary GUI apps still need capture fallback unless framework/accessibility semantics exist.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: add semantic surfaces documentation map

Documentation integration gap: semantic surface docs are now spread across SDK plan, semantic surfaces, browser adapter, accessibility adapter, and quickstart. Add a small docs index/README section tying these together so contributors can find the semantic architecture and implementation status.

title: Pointer + key routing back into Xvfb via XTestFake*

Route kittui-input events back to the active Xvfb-hosted window. Mouse hit-tests pick the topmost LayoutNode::Floating or tiling pane under the cursor and translate the local pixel coordinate into XTestFakeMotionEvent/XTestFakeButtonEvent; keys go through XTestFakeKeyEvent with the host's keymap. Mod state preserved across events.

title: kittwm-sdk: wrap native socket event stream
description:
SDK architecture follow-up: native kittwm exposes bounded JSON-lines `EVENTS [ms]`, but kittwm-sdk does not wrap it in typed APIs yet. Add a first typed event stream helper over the existing socket protocol.

Acceptance:

description:

Shell scripts cannot generate these common chrome scenes without writing Rust/FFI code, limiting kittui as a shell renderer and kittwm chrome preview tool.

Scope:

  - `kittui divider --left COLOR --right COLOR -w W`
  - `kittui title-bar --left COLOR --right COLOR -w W [-h H]`
- Build scenes through `kittui_affordances::{chip_chrome, divider_chrome, title_chrome}` and `Chrome::to_scene`.
- Support existing emit modes: `--scene-json`, `--json`, `--dry-run`, channels.
- Add targeted CLI tests and update README/DESIGN.

Acceptance:

- Tests assert expected footprints/layers.

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). Makes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

---

Duplicate of bd-624e22
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket wait-for-text command
description:
Large terminal-WM automation gap: native kittwm controllers can inject text/keys and read pane text snapshots, but scripts still have to poll `READ_TEXT` in a loop to wait for expected output. Terminal automation needs a first-class wait primitive on the WM socket.

- It returns a clear error on missing target or timeout.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.

- Preserve final cursor position after visible text.
- Keep plain/ansi/tmux fallbacks unchanged.

title: kittui-wm: add NativeSurface focus event hook
description:
Surface runtime gap: kittwm session routes terminal focus-in/out reports through PTY-specific helper code, but the common `NativeSurface` abstraction has no focus notification hook.

Scope:
- Add `send_surface_focus(&mut self, focused: bool) -> Result<()>` to `NativeSurface` with a default no-op or explicit unsupported behavior appropriate for capture-only surfaces.
- Override for `PtyTerminalApp` to send CSI focus-in/out (`ESC[I` / `ESC[O`) only when the terminal app has enabled focus reporting.
- Add/adjust `TerminalSurface` helper if useful.
- Add focused tests for PTY focus-reporting enabled/disabled behavior and capture-only/default behavior.
- Do not change socket commands or semantic focus behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface can model focus notifications for PTY surfaces.
- Existing session focus behavior remains compatible.

title: Implement real Mask, Clip, and BlendMode rendering semantics
description: DESIGN.md includes Node::Composite with BlendMode::{Normal, Add, Multiply, Screen}, Node::Mask, and Node::Clip. Current CPU rasterizer passes through Composite children using normal source-over and renders Mask/Clip children without applying mask alpha or clip bounds; GPU encode similarly ignores the blend/mask/clip semantics. Implement matching CPU/GPU semantics or explicit CPU-first semantics with golden tests for add/multiply/screen, masked content, and clipped content.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown footnote definitions

description:
Docs follow-up after SDK capability presets and NativePaneDetail accessors landed. Document ClientCapabilities::none / inspect_only / automation / restricted compatibility and the NativePaneDetail convenience accessors for bounds/cursor/modes/dirty/transport diagnostics.

description:
SDK follow-up from semantic runtime events. The daemon now emits semantic_snapshot_ready, semantic_focus_changed, semantic_action_invoked, and semantic_value_changed through EVENTS, but kittwm-sdk currently treats these as unknown KittwmEvent values. Add typed SDK parsing for semantic event kinds.

- Preserve unknown fallback behavior.
- Add parser tests for semantic event JSON detail fields.
- No daemon/runtime behavior changes.

Acceptance:

- Existing event parsing tests still pass.
- git diff --check passes.

title: kittwm: start and stop lifecycle aliases
description:
Daily-driver UX: support obvious lifecycle aliases.

- Add `kittwm stop` as an alias for `--kill` / daemon QUIT.

Docs follow-up after SDK exact byte, paste, and mouse input helpers landed. Update SDK/WM docs to mention `SurfaceHandle::send_bytes`, `send_bytes_b64`, `paste_bytes`, `paste_bytes_b64`, `send_mouse`, and `MouseEvent`, all gated by SendInput.

Acceptance:

- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support native PTY alternate screen
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm panes do not honor alternate-screen DEC private modes. Full-screen TUIs commonly enter `CSI ? 1049 h` and leave with `CSI ? 1049 l`; without support, their output contaminates the normal scrollback/snapshot and restoring the shell prompt view is impossible.

Scope:
- Track normal vs alternate screen buffers in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle DEC private `?1049h` / `?1049l` (and compatible `?47`/`?1047`) in CSI dispatch.
- Preserve current visible snapshot behavior while active; restore the normal buffer and cursor on leave.
- Resize both active and saved buffers sanely.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Entering alternate screen hides normal-buffer text from `text_snapshot`.
- Leaving alternate screen restores normal text/cursor.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui: separate render scene from terminal placement
description:
Large gap: kittui rendering and terminal placement are conflated. CompositionEntry carries a footprint, but Composer calls `runtime.place(&scene)`, so moving an existing rendered scene requires mutating the scene footprint and can change the scene/cache id.

Scope:
- Add a high-level `Runtime::place_at`/placement override API that renders/caches a scene using its scene-local footprint while placing it at a caller-supplied terminal footprint with matching dimensions.
- Update `Composer::apply` to honor `CompositionEntry.footprint` through the new API.
- Add regression tests showing the same scene id/image id can move to a different x/y and that Composer emits placement for moved entries without requiring a new scene.

Acceptance:
- Moving an entry in a Composition changes placement position but not scene id/image id.
- Existing `Runtime::place` behavior remains unchanged.
- Targeted kittui tests and build pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: render kittwm session manifest previews
description:
Large integration gap: kittwm can save/restore `SESSION_JSON`, and `kittui wm-chrome` can render one window frame, but there is no kittui-side command that turns a native kittwm session manifest into a preview/export scene set. This limits shell/external-platform workflows that want to inspect or render the WM layout without attaching to a live terminal.

Scope:
- Add `kittui wm-session <session.json|-> -w/--width W -h/--height H`.
- Parse `SESSION_JSON` manifests and build one `WindowChromeTheme` scene per pane.
- Honor manifest `layout` (`columns`/`rows`), pane order, weights, titles, and focused flags.
- Route through the existing batch emit pipeline so `--scene-json`, `--json`, and placement output work.
- Add targeted unit coverage.

Acceptance:
- `kittui --scene-json wm-session manifest.json ...` emits an array of scenes labelled with kittwm chrome labels.
- Weighted rows/columns affect scene footprints.
- Targeted kittui CLI tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: event envelope convenience accessors
description:
SDK event ergonomics gap: `KittwmEvent` variants carry `EventEnvelope`, but clients must match every variant just to inspect common metadata/detail fields. Add additive convenience accessors.

Scope:
- Add `KittwmEvent::envelope()` returning `Option<&EventEnvelope>` for all typed variants and `None` for Unknown, plus `raw()` if useful.
- Add small `EventEnvelope` detail helpers such as `detail_str`, `detail_bool`, `detail_u64` for common event payloads.
- Add targeted unit tests covering status/semantic/surface events and unknown fallback.
- Do not change parsing behavior or daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK event consumers can inspect common metadata/details without repetitive matches.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` event tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown table column alignment
description: Pulldown-cmark exposes table column alignments but kittui-md currently drops them. Preserve alignment metadata in MarkdownTable, include it in metadata JSON, and add tests for left/center/right alignment.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Implement user Shader node rendering and pipeline cache
description: DESIGN.md/Future ideas mention Node::Shader with per-scene WGSL pipeline compilation, caching, and GPU execution, while CPU should return a clear GPU-only error. Current GPU encode accepts Shader in the type system but draws nothing, and CPU returns UnsupportedImage. Implement shader validation, uniform binding, per-scene pipeline cache, error reporting, and tests that Shader nodes either render through GPU or fail clearly under CPU-only mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: spike macOS AX semantic adapter
description: Implement a macOS Accessibility proof for kittwm semantic surfaces. Associate a captured app/window with an AXUIElement window, extract a bounded semantic snapshot mapping common AX roles to ComponentNode values, and report Accessibility permission diagnostics. Follow-up from bd-4a49aa.

title: Add kittui-md notes alias for footnotes
description: Add --notes as a friendly alias for kittui-md --footnotes so users can inspect Markdown footnote references and definitions with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.

title: kittwm: fix split overlap and host-resize logical reflow
description: User report: splitting a terminal leaves the original pane at the same size, causing the new split to overlap/flicker. Resizing the host terminal scales the existing graphics instead of reflowing to a new logical split layout. Fix kittwm layout/render loop so pane frames are recomputed at the current host cell/pixel geometry, splits do not overlap, and host resize triggers logical rows/cols/layout updates rather than bitmap scaling. Acceptance: split panes occupy disjoint app/chrome regions; host terminal resize recomputes layout and re-captures at native resolution/DPI; targeted tests/smoke cover split and host-resize geometry.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: update first-party kittwm app docs
description:
Documentation follow-up after kittwm-terminal/kittwm-launch maturity. Update docs to describe first-party standalone SDK apps, including kittwm-terminal status/events and kittwm-launch backend/dry-run/status behavior after it lands.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md or docs/README.md with first-party app commands.
- Mention kittwm-terminal --status / --events-ms.

title: kittwm: support extended SGR colors in native PTY renderer
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap after basic SGR support: many modern shells and TUIs use 256-color and truecolor SGR sequences (`38;5;n`, `48;5;n`, `38;2;r;g;b`, `48;2;r;g;b`). Native kittwm currently preserves only the 16 ANSI colors, so many prompts/status bars still lose their intended colors.

Scope:
- Extend native `TerminalState` SGR parsing to handle semicolon and colon/subparameter forms for:
  - `38;5;n` / `48;5;n` 256-color foreground/background.
  - `38;2;r;g;b` / `48;2;r;g;b` truecolor foreground/background.
- Keep existing 16-color, reset/default, bold, and reverse behavior.
- Add parser/render tests for 256-color and truecolor cells.

Acceptance:
- Tests show 256-color and truecolor SGR are stored on cells and affect RGBA output.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: add inline script UI showcase bash script
description:
Add a first-party bash showcase script that demonstrates kittui as an inline-script UI substrate: title bar, partitioned panels, inline rows/chips/badges/segments/dividers, control-like affordances, footer, varied themes/styles, and animation flags with safe terminal fallback.

Acceptance:
- Script is executable and self-contained.
- Supports graphics/static/text modes and uses tmux-safe default fallback unless explicitly overridden.
- Validates with bash -n and a non-graphics smoke run.

Follow-up from synthetic semantic SDK app and SEMANTIC_PUBLISH. Teach the synthetic semantic SDK app example to publish its generated settings snapshot to the current/focused kittwm surface, not just print/query snapshots.

title: Fix kitty upload chunk syntax causing Ghostty ENOENT in showcase
description: Harry reported cargo run -p kittui-cli --example showcase in Ghostty prints placeholder glyphs plus kitty responses like Gi=<id>;ENOENT: image not found. Inspection found kittui-kitty encode_chunked emits upload commands as ESC_G,a=t,... with a leading comma before a=t; placement commands use ESC_Ga=p... and are parsed, so Ghostty reports placements for images whose uploads were ignored. Fix upload chunk control syntax, add regression coverage, and verify kittui-kitty tests. Filed from operator report.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add render-many-to-directory helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: FFI/Python/TypeScript can return render-many manifests with base64 PNGs, and the CLI can write batch PNGs to a directory, but platform bindings do not provide the equivalent file-output helper. External hosts have to manually decode base64 manifests and invent naming/manifest conventions.

Scope:
- Add Python `Kittui.render_many_to_dir(scenes, out_dir, prefix="scene")` helper.
- Add TypeScript `Kittui.renderManyToDir(scenes, outDir, options?)` helper.
- Write deterministic files `prefix-00000.png`, etc. and a `manifest.json` with filenames.
- Keep using existing `render_many` / `renderMany` FFI manifest, no ABI change.
- Add binding tests and README updates.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS helpers decode `png_base64` and write files/manifest.
- Existing Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive glow and scanline helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: Python/TypeScript helpers cover solid, gradient, and image primitives, but core effect primitives (`Glow`, `Scanlines`) still require hand-authored Scene JSON. These effects are useful for shell previews and kittwm/chrome-like external renderers.

Scope:
- Add Python `scene.glow_layer(...)`, `scene.glow_box(...)`, `scene.scanlines_layer(...)`, `scene.scanlines_box(...)` helpers.
- Add TypeScript `scene.glowLayer(...)`, `scene.glowBox(...)`, `scene.scanlinesLayer(...)`, `scene.scanlinesBox(...)` helpers.
- Keep helpers primitive-only and JSON-compatible.
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS users can build valid Glow and Scanlines scenes without hand-authoring schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Release prep: README v0.3 + DESIGN.md update + version bump
description:
README leads with kitwm + a 30-second-quickstart. DESIGN.md gets a kittui-wm v2 section reflecting the daemon/client model. Workspace version 0.3.0. CHANGELOG entry summarising the v2 epic landings. Tag release once all P0/P1 children of bd-031e54 close. Parent epic: bd-031e54

## Merged duplicate: bd-bc8121

Title: Backend manifest spec + persistent session file

Add a session file format (~/.config/kittui-wm/session.toml) describing which backends to spawn on startup, their config, default layouts, per-window chrome themes. kittui-wm loads it at startup; the operator picker writes back to it. Means the operator can configure 'on launch, attach Quartz to display 1, spawn XQuartz at :99 with xterm tiled top-left, mirror Safari floating bottom-right'.

Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Title: docs/wm.md v2: multi-backend mental model + operator workflow

Rewrite docs/wm.md to lead with the multi-backend mental model: any window is local/remote/Quartz/XQuartz, rendered as homogenous kittui chrome. Include backend matrix from the design conversation, the SSH-into-Mac story, the mixed X11+macOS session example, and the limits (no Cocoa hosting without v3 CGVirtualDisplay).

Parent epic: bd-031e54

Title: Doc: macOS quickstart with XQuartz install + Nix integration

Update docs/wm.md macOS section: brew install --cask xquartz, then cargo run --release -p kittui-cli --example kittui_wm_demo --features quartz,xquartz. Include a Nix fragment that adds /opt/X11/bin to PATH for the devshell on Darwin. Parent epic: bd-031e54
