Title: Doc: SSH operator walkthrough

docs/operator-walkthrough.md: step-by-step for the SSH-into-Mac use case. Includes generating an SSH key for kittui-wm, enabling Screen Recording for sshd, setting KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY, attaching, detaching, killing the session. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: mature standalone SDK launcher
description:
First-party app gap: `kittwm-launch` exists as a skeleton, but browser/app/terminal backend behavior and app discovery integration are still shallow. Mature it as a standalone SDK launcher.

Scope:
- Audit current `kittwm-launch` skeleton.
- Improve backend selection, app discovery replies, browser command behavior, status output, and error messages.
- Prefer typed SDK surfaces where available and explicit fallback where not.
- Add targeted tests for backend selection/request construction.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-launch is a more useful first-party SDK launcher.
- Existing CLI behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor application cursor-key mode in native panes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor DECCKM application cursor-key mode (`CSI ? 1 h/l`). Full-screen TUIs commonly enable it and expect arrow keys as SS3 `ESC O A/B/C/D`; native host-key routing and socket status currently ignore the mode, so navigation can be misinterpreted.

Scope:
- Track application cursor-key mode in native `TerminalState`, default false.
- Handle `CSI ? 1 h` / `CSI ? 1 l`.
- Expose the mode from `PtyTerminalApp` and native pane status/JSON/text listings.
- Route parsed host arrow key events to the focused native pane as SS3 arrows when the pane has application cursor mode enabled, otherwise preserve normal CSI arrows/raw behavior.
- Add focused tests for mode tracking and payload selection.

Acceptance:
- Tests show the mode toggles via parser sequences.
- Tests show arrow payloads switch between CSI and SS3 forms.
- Targeted cargo tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm-bar reserve chrome and render kitty-native
description:
Make first-party kittwm-bar a real SDK/chrome app: it can reserve/clear top-bar drawable space through typed kittwm-sdk, and it can render its kittui title-bar scene directly as kitty graphics rather than only text/JSON/scene-json.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-bar supports a graphics output mode that places the existing kittui scene.
- kittwm-bar supports --reserve and --release/--clear-reservation using Kittwm::reserve_chrome / clear_chrome_reservation.
- JSON model includes extended reservation metadata from CHROME_JSON.
- Tests cover CLI parse/model shape without touching live session internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: expose probe and unplace lifecycle APIs
description:
Large gap: the TypeScript koffi binding still does not expose FFI lifecycle/introspection functions `kittui_probe_json` and `kittui_unplace`, even though shell/CLI and C ABI users can inspect runtime transport/ABI details and delete uploaded images. JS/TS hosts need the same renderer lifecycle controls.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_probe_json` and `kittui_unplace` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js`.
- Add `probe()` returning parsed JSON and `unplace(imageId)` returning delete bytes.
- Accept decimal numbers and hex strings for image ids if practical.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README docs.
- Add fake-library tests that assert probe parsing and unplace forwarding.

Acceptance:
- `Kittui.probe()` returns an object parsed from FFI JSON.
- `Kittui.unplace(0x1234)` forwards image id and returns delete bytes.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: ensure graphics image lifecycle does not leak or flicker
description: QA gap: recent flicker came from delete-before-reupload. Audit kitty image id/placement lifecycle for steady frames, moved panes, closed panes, overlays, host resize, and renderer switches. Ensure deletes only happen when safe and resources are reclaimed without visible blank frames. Acceptance: tests/smoke cover no steady-state delete, delete on pane removal, re-place on footprint move, and no overlap/flicker regressions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md slugs alias for anchors
description: Add --slugs as a concise alias for kittui-md --anchors so users can inspect generated heading slug anchors with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: kitwm with no args opens a usable session, launches xterm, you can type into it
description: Integration: kitwm starts, app launcher opens via Mod+Return, type 'xterm', press Enter, xterm appears in the kittui terminal, type 'echo hello' inside it, the chars route through, the output is captured back. Final visual proof via tendril screenshot. This is the v2 acceptance criterion. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Markdown tables via kittui box-drawing glyph atlas and relative placement anchors
description: Implement markdown table rendering that replaces unicode box drawing characters with kittui image cells: every box-drawing glyph gets a unique cell-sized kittui image so a rendered table is visually connected as a whole when emitted through kitty unicode replacement. Use kitty virtual placement plus relative non-virtual positioning anchors (P=/Q=/H=/V=) to position these cell images consistently, and support placing a background image or panel beneath the table in z-space. Include exact protocol/placement tests and visual proof fixtures.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add explicit animation frame update primitives
description:
Follow-up from bd-510a36. Add typed kittui-kitty helpers for kitty animation/full-frame update experiments without coupling them to the live WM renderer.

Scope:
- Add explicit helpers for frame upload/update/control commands needed by full-frame animation/ring-buffer experiments.
- Keep helpers protocol-focused and tested for grammar.
- Do not change kittwm default rendering.

Acceptance:
- kittui-kitty exposes typed animation/update primitives with tests.
- Existing upload_animation behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md rich footer reports heading outline entries
description: Plain kittui-md output now reports a heading outline, but rich/interactive footer metadata still only lists links/images. Add a compact rich outline section that mirrors the plain outline indentation and test the formatter/status behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm attach: thin viewer client
description:
kittui-wm attach (or kittui-wm with no args + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY set) connects to the daemon socket and runs the existing run_loop as a renderer: receives scene snapshots from CmdTick replies, posts InputEvents on stdin parse, never touches backends directly. Multiple clients can attach to the same session simultaneously. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-624e22
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add explicit socket and display CLI flags
description:
Large DISPLAY-like usability gap: kittwm supports `KITTWM_SOCKET` / `KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY` env resolution, but CLI users must set environment variables to target a specific socket/display. First-class `--socket` and `--display` flags make the WM control plane easier and less error-prone for shell scripts and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add global CLI flags:
  - `--socket PATH` sets `KITTWM_SOCKET` / `KITTWM_SOCK` for this invocation.
  - `--display DISPLAY` sets `KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY` / `KITTWM_DISPLAY` for this invocation.
- Apply before any socket-resolving command (`--serve`, wrappers, save/restore, status, attach, etc.).
- Validate the flags are not both supplied.
- Update help/docs and add focused parse/normalization tests.

Acceptance:
- `--socket` and `--display` affect default socket resolution through existing daemon helper.
- Supplying both errors clearly.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render and publish native PTY cursor visibility
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm tracks cursor position but the rendered PTY surface does not draw a cursor and does not honor DEC cursor visibility (`CSI ? 25 h/l`). Shells/editors rely on a visible caret; controllers can inspect cursor metadata but users cannot see it in the pane render.

Scope:
- Track cursor visibility in native `TerminalState` and handle DEC private `?25h` / `?25l`.
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::cursor_visible()` and include optional `cursor_visible` in native pane status JSON/text surfaces.
- Render a visible cursor in `render_terminal_rgba` when enabled (without changing plain text snapshots).
- Update docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves cursor visibility mode toggles.
- Render test proves the cursor changes RGBA output at the cursor cell.
- Daemon status tests include cursor visibility metadata.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: expose runtime configure
description:
Large gap: FFI live runtime_configure is now real, but the Python binding still only configures at construction time. Long-lived Python hosts need to reconfigure renderer/transport/terminal capability without closing and reopening.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_runtime_configure` in Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.configure(config: dict)` returning None or self, using the same error handling/last_error path.
- Add fake-CDLL tests for success and failure.
- Update Python README API/example.

Acceptance:
- Python tests pass.
- configure forwards JSON config and errors include last_error.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Finish v1 CLI surface from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md lists a broader CLI than currently implemented: panel, image, place, scene emit, cache info/gc/clear, probe --force, --transport, --renderer, --upload-only/--placement-only/--embed-only, --dry-run, pixel and percentage sizing, richer colors, glow/scanline/shadow/label options, and animation curve validation. Some cache commands exist, but most common flags and subcommands do not. Fill out the v1 CLI surface in thin wrappers over Runtime/Composition and add integration tests for JSON/dry-run/raw output modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm daemon APPS_FIRST/APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST verbs
description: Expose the launcher selection primitive through the daemon protocol. Add APPS_FIRST QUERY to return the first matching launcher candidate, and APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST QUERY to launch it and report pid/kind/name. This lets 'kitwm --attach -c' exercise search+selection+launch against a running daemon. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-f92697/bd-68568a and decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK shortcuts catalog helper
description:
Docs follow-up for typed SDK shortcut catalog helper.

Scope:
- After source lands, document `Kittwm::shortcuts()` / `shortcuts_json()` and `SHORTCUTS_JSON` typed access.
- Mention relationship to `kittwm --shortcuts-json`, text shortcuts, and `C-a ?`.
- Docs-only; no source/runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention typed SDK helper and catalog shape at a high level.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitty animation control grammar wrong: s={loops},c={count} misuses state/current-frame fields
description: Per kitty spec, on a=a (animation control) the s field is state (1=stop, 2=loop, 3=play N times) and c is the current frame to display; loop count goes in v. We emit s={loops},c={framecount} which sets state to a bogus value and forces current frame to last. Result: animations don't loop correctly and display wrong frame on start. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: fix core tiling non-overlap invariants
description: User report: kittwm fundamental tiling is broken. Opening kittwm shows ugly chrome/empty boxes, opening terminals causes flicker/z-index fights, and multiple terminals draw over one another. Before fancy kitty renderer polish, fix the core tiling engine contract: panes must have disjoint outer bounds, disjoint app bounds, stable top-bar/footer reservations, no overlapping split allocations, and predictable focus/title geometry. Acceptance: add/strengthen deterministic layout tests for 0/1/2/3+ panes across column/row splits and resize sizes; fix any overlap/off-by-one issues in the tiling/layout helpers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md links-only mode for link inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --links mode that prints only parsed Markdown links with labels and URLs. This complements --references and --images for focused audits. Include output tests for populated and empty documents.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: daemon/client architecture in docs/wm.md
description: Long-form description of the daemon/client split: protocol, socket lifecycle, multi-client semantics, security (file permissions on the socket), comparison to tmux's server. Operator quickstart: kittui-wm serve & ; KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 kittui-wm attach. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket pane rename command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes have visible title rows and socket status records, but there is no scriptable way to name/rename panes. Titles fall back to the spawned command, which is poor WM chrome for long-running shells and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `RENAME_PANE <window> <title>`.
- Track an optional display title per native pane and use it in title chrome/status snapshots.
- Keep app command title as fallback when no display title is set.
- Add queue parsing and pane title helper tests.
- Document the attach command in docs/wm.md and include it in HELP/HELP_JSON.

Acceptance:
- `RENAME_PANE native-2 editor` over the native socket updates title/status for that pane.
- HELP/HELP_JSON list RENAME_PANE.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md footnotes mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --footnotes mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: protocol-conformance.md row for f=32 raw RGB
description: Add f=32 to the spec coverage table in docs/protocol-conformance.md. Mark as 'supported' once the kittui-kitty test and the demo measurement both land. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: WM-thread model + pump lifecycle in docs/wm.md
description: Add a section to docs/wm.md describing the UI thread vs Pump worker thread split, when frames go stale, how backpressure works (latest-frame-wins). Include a small ASCII diagram. Add a perf table updated with measured frame times from the demo against the FakeServer + Quartz pump. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: wait for text across screen and scrollback
description:
Gap after adding native scrollback: `WAIT_TEXT` only searches the current screen. Fast commands can print a sentinel and scroll it off-screen before controllers poll, so automation still misses output despite scrollback being available.

Scope:
- Add socket commands `WAIT_OUTPUT <window|focused> <needle>` and `WAIT_OUTPUT_MS <window|focused> <ms> <needle>` that search current screen plus native scrollback.
- Add CLI wrappers `--wait-output WINDOW TEXT` and `--wait-output-ms MS WINDOW TEXT`.
- Align client read timeout handling with the explicit millisecond form.
- Update help/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `WAIT_OUTPUT` matches scrollback-only text while `WAIT_TEXT` remains screen-oriented.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm CLI tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC Special Graphics line drawing
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor DEC Special Graphics character set selection (`ESC ( 0` / `ESC ( B`). Many curses apps still use ACS line-drawing bytes (`lqkx`, etc.) to draw borders. Native panes currently render those bytes literally, so boxes appear as `lqqk` / `x  x` in both the kitty-rendered frame and `READ_TEXT` snapshots.

Scope:
- Track G0 DEC Special Graphics selection in native `TerminalState`, default ASCII.
- Handle `ESC ( 0` to enable DEC Special Graphics and `ESC ( B` to return to ASCII.
- Map common DEC line-drawing glyphs (`l,m,k,j,q,x,t,u,v,w,n,plus/minus/dot variants`) to Unicode box-drawing/symbol characters during printable output.
- Reset charset selection on terminal reset; preserve it across resize like other modes.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves DEC ACS border bytes produce Unicode box-drawing glyphs.
- Test proves `ESC ( B` returns printable bytes to ASCII.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: add standalone SDK app launcher skeleton
description: Add kittwm-launch as a standalone client that detects/accepts backend selection, spawns terminal/browser/GUI surfaces through SDK, and can replace current or create new windows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: emit explicit pane resized events
description:
Runtime event gap: native event stream has pane_changed but no explicit resize event. Emit a stable `pane_resized` event when a pane's outer/app geometry changes between `PANES_JSON` publications.

Scope:
- Update daemon pane event publication only; no SDK parser changes in this slice.
- Event kind: `pane_resized`.
- Detail should include old/new geometry labels or objects for outer and app bounds where available.
- Preserve existing `pane_changed` behavior for backward compatibility.
- Add targeted daemon unit test for event JSON shape.

Acceptance:
- `EVENTS [ms]` subscribers can observe explicit pane resize geometry changes.
- Existing pane_changed/status events remain.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: expose render_many_png batch API
description:
Large gap: the Rust facade now has `render_many_png`, but FFI consumers still only have single-scene `kittui_render_json`. External platforms wanting batch preview artifacts need either many FFI round-trips or custom loops.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_render_many_json(runtime, scenes_json, out_json)` returning JSON manifest with count, per-image index/bytes/footprint/png_base64.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h`/ABI snapshot.
- Use `Runtime::render_many_png` internally.
- Add targeted FFI tests for PNG base64 manifest and non-array rejection.

Acceptance:
- `kittui_render_many_json` returns parseable JSON for scene arrays.
- Invalid non-array input returns BadScene with last_error.
- Targeted FFI and ABI snapshot tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC origin mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap after scroll-region support: native kittwm does not honor DEC origin mode (`CSI ? 6 h/l`). TUIs that set scroll margins often enable origin mode so cursor addressing is relative to the scroll region; without it, writes land in the wrong rows and corrupt fixed headers/status lines.

Scope:
- Track DEC origin mode in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI ? 6 h` / `CSI ? 6 l`, resetting cursor to home on toggle.
- When origin mode is enabled, make `CSI H/f` row coordinates relative to `scroll_top` and clamped within `scroll_top..scroll_bottom`.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with scroll region + origin mode.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `CSI 1;1H` inside origin mode writes at scroll-region top, not absolute row 0.
- Tests show disabling origin mode restores absolute addressing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md definitions mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --definitions mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: refresh semantic quickstart landed adapter status
description:
Docs cleanup: semantic quickstart still describes browser action routing and accessibility adapters as follow-up/future work even though browser action routing, safe accessibility adapter core, and action routing have landed. Refresh the limitations/status section.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-semantic-quickstart.md current limitations/status bullets.
- Mention browser DOM/ARIA extraction/publish/action routing and CLI semantic inspection current state.
- Mention accessibility safe core/action routing exists, direct platform bindings remain follow-up.

Acceptance:
- Quickstart no longer mislabels landed browser/accessibility semantic functionality as future work.
- `git diff --check` passes.

title: Add kittui-md mode category discovery

description: context: during bd-cddcf2/bd-d75324, the replace path was smoke-tested manually with KITTWM_WINDOW set, but the command parser/exec path has no automated test because it uses exec on Unix. Suggested fix: factor argv resolution (browser to kittwm-browser) and context validation into a pure helper with tests. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-cddcf2/bd-d75324/bd-4b9c96 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md schemas-json discovery mode
description: Add --schemas-json as a no-input discovery mode for kittui-md machine-readable outputs, emitting schema_version plus per-output top-level JSON keys and short descriptions. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md headings alias for outline
description: Add --headings as a friendly alias for kittui-md --outline/--toc so users can inspect document headings with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: visual regression goldens for graphical chrome states
description: QA gap for graphical shell: add visual/golden tests or artifact snapshots for empty workspace, one pane, split panes, focus change, overlay open, translucent chrome, and fallback terminal renderer. Acceptance: reproducible artifacts/goldens catch regressions in kittui-rendered chrome and dogfood surfaces.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add base64 byte injection command
description:
Large terminal automation gap: native kittwm socket input is line-oriented. `SEND_TEXT`/`SEND_LINE` cannot safely carry embedded newlines, NULs, escape sequences, or arbitrary paste payloads; scripts must work around protocol framing. A terminal WM control plane needs an encoded byte-injection path.

Scope:
- Add socket command `SEND_BYTES_B64 <window|focused> <base64>` that decodes bytes and sends them to the pane.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --send-bytes-b64 <window|focused> <base64>`.
- Reuse existing `SendBytes` native loop handling.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage, tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Base64 payloads decode to exact bytes and queue/send via `NativePaneCommand::SendBytes`.
- Invalid base64 and bad targets return clear errors.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: detach + reattach preserves session state
description:
Integration test: spawn daemon with a FakeServer backend, attach client A, send pointer events, detach. Spawn client B (new process), attach to same socket, verify it sees the same session state (windows, focus, layout) immediately. Asserts the daemon outlived A and B starts at the same state, not a fresh one. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kittui-md preserves markdown link labels in surrounding paragraph text
description: The markdown renderer emits links as highlighted chips, but currently removes the link label from the surrounding paragraph text, producing gaps such as 'A paragraph with a  and inline code'. Preserve the label in paragraph/table text while still emitting LinkChip metadata and chip components.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive help toggle
description: Add a native interactive pager help toggle for kittui-md so users can press ? or h to view keybindings while browsing. Reuse the keybinding catalog, document the control, and add targeted parser/pager tests where practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: apply kittwm chrome reservations to native layout
description:
Wire the chrome reservation control-plane contract into the native kittwm PTY layout so reserved top/bottom/side bands and tile gaps affect actual drawable pane geometry. This complements dev-2's z-order/non-overlap work and avoids touching runtime placement internals.

Acceptance:
- Native layout helpers clamp pane/app footprints to reported terminal rows/cols minus reservation bands.
- RESERVE_CHROME_JSON changes can be read by the live loop via NativeSpawnQueue.
- Tests cover top/bottom/side/gap reservation geometry.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render readable native terminal glyphs and reduce title flicker
description:
Native kittwm PTY panes currently render printable glyphs with synthetic pseudo-box strokes, making zsh/shell text unreadable despite interactivity. Replace the pseudo glyph renderer with a deterministic bitmap/vector glyph renderer for common terminal characters and reduce title-row flicker by avoiding unnecessary ANSI title redraws.

Scope:
- Draw readable ASCII glyphs in native PTY RGBA frames instead of pseudo boxes.
- Keep SGR foreground/background behavior and cursor rendering intact.
- Add support for common box-drawing glyphs from DEC Special Graphics.
- Avoid rewriting unchanged native pane title rows every frame; update on clear/focus/title/layout changes.
- Add focused renderer tests.

Acceptance:
- Renderer test demonstrates different readable glyph shapes for common text and not full-cell boxes.
- Title-row redraw cache is covered by a small unit test or refactor-friendly behavior.
- `cargo test -p kittui-wm ...`, `cargo test -p kittui-cli ...`, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed shortcuts catalog helper
description:
SDK gap: kittwm now exposes a machine-readable shortcut catalog through `SHORTCUTS_JSON`, but SDK clients lack typed access.

Scope:
- Add typed `ShortcutCatalog` / `ShortcutEntry` structs matching `SHORTCUTS_JSON`.
- Add `Kittwm::shortcuts()` / `shortcuts_json()` helper reading `SHORTCUTS_JSON`.
- Keep capability policy consistent with existing read-only catalog helpers.
- Add focused Unix socket unit test verifying command string and parsing of launch/help/exit entries.
- No daemon/session/CLI changes.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can call `client.shortcuts()` or `client.shortcuts_json()`.
- Targeted SDK tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: stable behavior under tmux, ssh, and non-kitty terminals
description: QA gap: daily-driver behavior differs under tmux, remote/ssh, non-kitty terminals, and explicit renderer overrides. Build a matrix/checklist or automated doctor smoke for KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER auto/terminal/kitty, tmux detection, remote detection, and fallback paths. Acceptance: documented/tested expected renderer/transport choices and no accidental kitty graphics passthrough in tmux-safe mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: bindings: add primitive scene builder helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: TypeScript has a few primitive scene helpers, but Python has none and TS lacks an easy valid solid scene builder. Platform users still have to hand-author verbose Scene JSON for common shell-renderer previews.

Scope:
- Add Python module-level `scene` helpers mirroring the TS helper surface.
- Add first-party valid solid-box helpers in both Python and TypeScript.
- Helpers should build JSON-compatible primitive-only Scene objects (no affordances in kittui core).
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TypeScript users can create a valid solid Scene without hand-authoring the schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix macOS kittui-cli link failure for missing libiconv
description: While validating bd-c3214b on ms-mac, cargo build -p kittui-cli and cargo test -p kittui-cli --bins compiled successfully but failed at link time with ld: library not found for -liconv. cargo check -p kittui-cli passes, so this appears to be host/toolchain linker configuration or an existing native dependency issue rather than a source compile error from the config change. Command evidence: cargo build -p kittui-cli -> cc ... -liconv ... ld: library not found for -liconv. Filed from bd-c3214b validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive clear-status key
description: Add a native clear-status key to kittui-md --interactive so users can dismiss reload success/failure messages while keeping the position footer visible. Expose it through keybinding discovery, document it, and add targeted pager/footer tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Wire f=32 into kittui-wm-demo + measure speedup
description: kittui-wm-demo with --features sck sets the Runtime to Rgba32. Measure frame budget from /tmp/kittui-wm.log before and after. Expectation: budget drops from ~60ms to <16ms on the M2. Capture screenshot via tendril of the demo running at smooth FPS. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md interactive pager: keyboard scrolling for rich view
description: Add an interactive pager mode for kittui-md rich rendering so long Markdown documents can be scrolled with keyboard controls instead of only using --offset/--height from the command line. Include a pure input/action helper with tests and keep --plain/one-shot rich modes working.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: stabilize kittwm graphical flicker and jank
description:
Investigate and reduce severe kittwm flicker/jank in graphical native mode. User reports kittwm is fundamentally unusable due to flicker and jank.

Acceptance:
- Identify high-impact cause(s) in native render path.
- Land a targeted stabilization fix with tests where possible.
- Preserve native-first graphical kittwm while making it usable; terminal fallback remains available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: inline affordances: add shared --animated/--fps/--frames flags
description:
Inventory of inline kittui elements that should support animation:
- `kittui inline chip`: one-line prompt/status chip; graphical kitty/prompt modes render via `inline_chip_scene`.
- `kittui inline badge`: compact label/count variant sharing `InlineChipArgs` and `inline_chip_scene`.
- `kittui inline segment`: prompt/status segment variant sharing `InlineChipArgs` and `inline_chip_scene`.
- `kittui inline divider`: rule/divider rendered by `inline_divider_scene`.
- `kittui inline row`: composed row of chip/badge/segment/divider items via `inline_row_scene` and inherited style/theme.
- Non-inline root commands such as `kittui chip`, panel/title/divider demos may have separate animation knobs, but the requested scope is inline elements.

Current animation substrate:
- `Scene.animation` already causes all frames to be rasterized/uploaded once through kitty native animation.
- CPU renderer currently animates `Node::Glow` intensity by phase, so style-specific inline animation can be expressed by adding phase-reactive glow layers plus an animation descriptor.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui: default RuntimeBuilder to detected terminal info
description:
Large gap: Rust/library callers that construct `Runtime::builder().build()` get `TerminalInfo::default_kitty()` instead of `TerminalInfo::detect()`, while the CLI separately detects terminal capabilities. This makes library/FFI hosts less reliable than the CLI.

Scope:
- Change `RuntimeBuilder::build()` to use `TerminalInfo::detect()` when no explicit terminal descriptor is supplied.
- Preserve `RuntimeBuilder::terminal(...)` as an exact host override.
- Add targeted tests proving detected transport/capabilities are used by default and explicit overrides win.

Acceptance:
- In a controlled `TMUX` environment, default runtime transport is tmux passthrough.
- With an explicit terminal override, runtime uses the override even if env detection would disagree.
- `cargo test -p kittui runtime_builder` and `cargo build -p kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed scrollback and wait helpers
description:
SDK automation gap: CLI/socket expose READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON, WAIT_TEXT_MS, and WAIT_OUTPUT_MS but `kittwm-sdk` only wraps `READ_TEXT_JSON`. Add typed SDK helpers for scrollback and waits so automation clients do not need raw protocol strings.

Scope:
- Add `ScrollbackSnapshot` typed response for `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON`.
- Add `SurfaceHandle::read_scrollback()`.
- Add `SurfaceHandle::wait_text_ms(ms, needle)` and `wait_output_ms(ms, needle)` plus convenience defaults if desired.
- Gate with existing read capability.
- Add targeted SDK unit/socket tests for command formatting and JSON parsing.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK automation clients can read scrollback and wait for screen/output text without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket key injection
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: native kittwm can inject text over the socket, but controllers still cannot send non-text terminal keys such as Ctrl-C, Escape, Tab, arrows, Home/End, or PageUp/PageDown. A terminal WM control plane needs key injection without relying on host TTY synthesis.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `SEND_KEY <window|focused> <key>`.
- Support common keys: enter/return, tab, escape/esc, backspace, delete, arrows, home/end, pageup/pagedown, and ctrl-a through ctrl-z spellings (`ctrl-c`, `C-c`).
- Queue bytes and handle in the native PTY session via existing focused/window lookup semantics.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error coverage and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- `SEND_KEY` validates window/key and returns queued replies or clear errors.
- Native loop sends the mapped bytes to the target pane.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add layered kittui configuration resolution
description: Operator direction: every user-facing kittui option should be settable via explicit CLI/API flag, KITTUI_* environment variable, or ~/.config/kittui/config.yaml, with precedence flag/API override > env override > YAML default. This lets scripts establish an env/config scope and call kittui many times or batch operations without repeating every flag, while apps can still force overrides. Design and implement a typed Config model, YAML loader (respecting XDG_CONFIG_HOME), KITTUI_* env parsing, source/precedence diagnostics for --json output, validation/error messages, and tests covering precedence and malformed config.

title: kittui-cli: allow compose batch placement origins
description:
Large gap: `kittui compose` supports arrays of scene JSON for shell/platform batch rendering, but rejects `--x/--y` placement overrides for batches. That makes it awkward for shell scripts to build reusable scene groups and place the whole group at a runtime origin.

Scope:

- Preserve each scene's relative offsets from the batch's minimum x/y.
- Keep single-scene override semantics unchanged.
- Add CLI tests replacing the old rejection test with batch offset assertions.

Acceptance:
- Batch compose with `--x` and/or `--y` succeeds in dry-run JSON.
- First/min batch scene is placed at the requested coordinate and other scenes preserve relative spacing.
- Existing single-scene override test still passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: deterministic pane ids and focus after rapid open/close
description: QA gap: rapid spawn/close/focus/move operations should keep deterministic pane ids and sane focus. Add tests for opening multiple panes, closing focused/non-focused panes, closing last pane, restoring empty workspace, and then spawning again. Acceptance: focus never points past pane list, ids do not collide, last-pane close returns to empty workspace, next spawn is predictable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm: single-command launcher that spawns daemon + attaches
description: Add a top-level bin 'kitwm' (or 'kittyui-wm') in kittui-cli that, when invoked with no args, does: 1. detect KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY or pick a free socket, 2. check whether a daemon is already listening; if yes attach to it, 3. if no, fork a daemon process that survives the parent and attach to it. With --serve, only run daemon; with --attach, only attach; with --kill, signal daemon shutdown. This is the user-facing entrypoint. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native automation CLI wrappers
description:
Large terminal-WM automation gap: native kittwm has socket commands for SEND_TEXT/SEND_LINE/SEND_KEY/READ_TEXT/WAIT_TEXT, but scripts still have to wrap them as `kittwm --attach -c ...`. First-class CLI wrappers make kittwm usable as a terminal automation tool without spelling socket protocol verbs directly.

Scope:

  - `--send-line <window|focused> <text>`
  - `--send-key <window|focused> <key>`
  - `--read-text <window|focused>`
  - `--wait-text <window|focused> <needle>`
- Send the corresponding native socket command via default socket resolution and print the reply.
- Add help text and focused request-builder tests.
- Update docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Flags generate the expected socket requests without changing payload case.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: wire keymap focus to raw-frame compositor focus
description:
Large gap: raw-frame kittwm chrome can now display focus, but focus actions are still cosmetic counters in the session. If no pointer click has focused a window, every raw frame can appear focused because the raw-frame metadata defaults per-window. Keyboard focus actions should drive the compositor's real focused window.

Scope:
- Add compositor helpers for deterministic focus: default focus, focus_next, focus_prev over current backend windows.
- Make raw-frame metadata mark exactly one focused frame by default.
- Wire session keymap focus actions (`focus.right/down` next, `focus.left/up` previous) to compositor focus helpers while preserving footer state text.
- Add targeted compositor tests for default single focus and focus cycling.

Acceptance:
- `raw_frames` marks only one frame focused before any pointer event.
- `focus_next`/`focus_prev` wrap deterministically.
- Keymap focus actions update compositor focus in capture-backed sessions.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime split actions: Ctrl-A | and - create visible pane splits with launcher
description: Wire keymap split.vertical.launcher and split.horizontal.launcher into live kitwm runtime state. Ctrl-A | / Ctrl-A - should increment visible pane/split state, launch KITWM_LAUNCH_CMD, log split orientation/count, and show pane count/orientation in footer. This is the next configurable WM action slice before true layout partition/window membership. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-5edc97/bd-7f00ef/bd-1c1a9e.

Send three Ctrl-C events within 500ms, assert run_loop returns. Send three Ctrl-C with 1.5s gaps between each, assert run_loop does NOT return and FakeServer received three Ctrl-Cs. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: Implement Image node rasterization and kittui image CLI
description: DESIGN.md defines Node::Image plus CLI `kittui image --src PATH -w W -h H --fit ... --tint ...`. Current CPU renderer returns UnsupportedImage for Node::Image and GPU encode drops image nodes; the CLI has no image subcommand. Implement PNG/JPEG image decoding behind the intended feature split, shared Fit geometry, CPU raster copy/tint path, GPU texture/atlas or documented CPU fallback path, image cache storage, and CLI coverage with tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive math toggle
description: Add a native math toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press m to inspect parsed math expressions without leaving the pager. Reuse existing math metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/math-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document new NativeSurface adapters
description:
Docs follow-up after native surface adapter work landed.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md, docs/wm.md, and/or docs/README.md to mention landed `KittuiSceneSurface`, `RgbaFrameSurface`, and `CompositeFrameSurface` as capture-only `NativeSurface` building blocks.
- Clarify these are runtime/composite primitives, not wired into default live kittwm behavior yet.
- Mention scene surfaces render through CPU renderer to PNG; RGBA/composite surfaces expose raw RGBA frames.
- Keep docs-only; do not touch native.rs.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect current surface adapter status and remaining live-runtime wiring gap.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md rich view wraps textbox content across rows
description: Rich kittui-md currently draws textbox content as one truncated line even though UiComponent computes multi-row heights. Add a wrapping text overlay helper so textboxes, banners, and headings can use their available rows, with pure unit tests for wrapping and truncation behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add save and restore session CLI flags
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: native kittwm now exposes `SESSION_JSON` and `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON`, but users/controllers still have to manually quote JSON through `kittwm --attach -c`. That makes persistence fragile in shell scripts and undermines the save/restore workflow.

Scope:
- Add first-class CLI flags:
  - `kittwm --save-session PATH` writes `SESSION_JSON` from the running socket (`-` for stdout).
  - `kittwm --restore-session PATH` reads JSON (`-` for stdin), compacts/validates it as JSON, and sends `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON <json>`.
- Reuse default socket resolution.
- Add help text and focused tests for restore command generation.

Acceptance:
- Save/restore avoids manual shell quoting hazards.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: crash recovery and daemon/socket stale state cleanup
description: QA gap: after crashes or forced kills, stale sockets, temp files, shm raw frames, image ids, logs, and session state can confuse next launch. Audit cleanup/recovery behavior. Acceptance: next kittwm launch after crash detects/removes stale socket/temp resources or gives clear instructions; tests cover stale socket and raw-frame temp cleanup.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm Ctrl-A r hot-reloads keymap from disk
description: Make the existing Ctrl-A r reload.config action actually reload the runtime keymap from KITTUI_WM_KEYMAP/--keymap PATH, falling back to defaults on failure while logging result and updating visible reload state. Add tests for reload success/failure state. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-1739ee.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Surface project config validation blockers in caco commands
description: While finishing bd-5c2b47 in the kittui project, every caco surface I tried (`caco msg speak`, `caco scratch append`, and `caco agent reintegrate`) failed because an unrelated `projects['picasso'].friend_projects[0].permissions` entry contained invalid permission `checkout_write`. The operator fixed the config, but the failure mode was surprising because a picasso config issue blocked kittui lifecycle/status narration and scratch health-note reconciliation. Consider making config validation errors identify the owning config path/project and, if safe, isolate unrelated project operations from invalid friend-project permission entries. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-5c2b47 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix native pane dummy helper PATH resolution in nix
description: Linux nix tests fail because dummy_native_pane_app calls PtyTerminalApp::spawn_program("true") and portable-pty attempts to execute literal true without PATH resolution. Resolve true from PATH before spawning so native pane tests pass in nix sandbox.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native app protocol: replace current pane or spawn via socket context
description: Operator direction: kittwm-native apps should be normal binaries aware of the WM/window context. Implement a socket/display protocol where commands like `kittwm replace browser` or binaries like `kittwm-browser` can replace the current container when `KITTWM_WINDOW` is set, or create a new window when only `KITTWM_SOCKET`/`KITTWM_DISPLAY` is set. This should feel like `DISPLAY=:... xterm` spawning a new X window. Parent epic: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: first-class semantic role remap status
description:
Docs follow-up after browser/accessibility adapters remapped obvious roles to first-class SDK roles. Update browser/accessibility semantic docs and semantic role docs to remove stale statements that link/canvas/tree/media roles are still always Custom.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-browser-semantic-adapter.md mapping table for Link and Canvas/pixel_region current behavior.
- Update docs/kittwm-accessibility-semantic-adapter.md mapping/status table for Heading/Link/Image/Canvas/List/Tree/Row/Cell current behavior.
- Update docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md note that browser/accessibility obvious remaps have landed while Custom remains for unsupported/vendor roles.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs match current first-class role remapping in browser and accessibility adapters.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: include pane details in native STATUS_JSON
description:
Large gap: native `PANES_JSON` now exposes weight and geometry, but `STATUS_JSON` still only returns counts/focus/layout. Controllers polling status need a single JSON call that includes focused pane details and geometry, instead of a second PANES_JSON round trip.

Scope:
- Extend native session `STATUS_JSON` with `focused_pane` and `panes_detail` using the same NativePaneStatus serialization as PANES_JSON.
- Keep existing top-level `pending`, `panes`, `focus`, and `layout` fields stable.
- Add targeted daemon test assertions for focused_pane and geometry in STATUS_JSON.

Acceptance:
- STATUS_JSON includes full focused pane object when present and null otherwise.
- STATUS_JSON includes panes_detail array with weight/geometry fields.
- Targeted daemon tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Investigate persistent agent claim visibility drift
description: Context: while working bd-883864 and bd-67a477, first-party caco bd claim returned assignee kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2 and status in_progress, but the lead agent repeatedly saw the same bead as open/unassigned until I retried claim. The bead show output also warned assignee_missing for this persistent agent id. This makes coordination noisy and can cause duplicate assignment concerns. Suggested fix: reconcile board/inventory display for persistent Pi agent ids or make claim/read surfaces converge faster and explain assignee_missing. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-67a477 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: friendly unknown-command guidance
description:
Daily-driver UX: make invalid `kittwm` commands self-correcting.

Scope:
- Replace bare `unknown arg` / unknown topic errors with friendly guidance that points to `kittwm quickstart`, `kittwm --help`, and `kittwm help topics`.
- Add lightweight suggestions for common mistakes/near-misses where practical (e.g. `pane` -> `panes`, `event` -> `events`, `shortcut` -> `shortcuts`).
- Keep existing valid commands unchanged.
- Add focused tests for unknown command and unknown topic error text.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Mistyped commands teach the next command to run instead of dead-ending.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md counts-json inspection mode
description: Add --counts-json as a focused machine-readable output mode that emits schema_version plus the same counts object used in metadata JSON, without detailed arrays. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: expose runtime config and placeAt
description:
Large gap: the TypeScript koffi binding still only exposes the old FFI constructor and `place(scene)`, while the C ABI now has `kittui_runtime_new_config` and `kittui_place_json_at`. JS/TS hosts cannot configure terminal capabilities/transport or move scene-local renders without mutating JSON.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_runtime_new_config` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js` and use it when options include runtime/terminal config beyond `cacheDir`.
- Wire `kittui_place_json_at` and expose `placeAt(scene, x, y)`.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README docs.
- Add binding tests that use a fake koffi library to assert constructor selection and placeAt calls without requiring a compiled cdylib.

Acceptance:
- TS tests cover configured constructor JSON and `placeAt` x/y forwarding.
- Existing `place`/`placeMany` remain compatible.
- `npm test` in `bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm default launcher overlay: Ctrl-A d opens boxed menu without extra flag
description: Make the live launcher overlay the default runtime behavior so kitwm (no args) has an actual in-session launcher menu. Ctrl-A d / Ctrl-A Enter should open the boxed overlay by default; add --no-launcher-overlay / KITTUI_WM_LAUNCHER_OVERLAY=0 to keep immediate-spawn behavior. Update help/tests and verify via live fake backend. Parent: bd-031e54; advances bd-8d64b1 end-goal.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md urls alias for links
description: Add --urls as a friendly alias for kittui-md --links so users can inspect Markdown link URLs with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md plain output indents multi-line component text
description: Plain kittui-md output currently prints multi-line component text with only the first line prefixed by [Kind], leaving subsequent lines unaligned. Format multi-line components with a stable prefix plus aligned continuation indentation, and add tests for code blocks or table text.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md metadata alias for metadata blocks
description: Add --metadata as a friendly alias for kittui-md --metadata-blocks/--frontmatter so users can inspect Markdown metadata blocks with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Per-window macOS capture via CGWindowListCreateImage
description:
Extend QuartzServer with list_app_windows() returning every titled macOS app window (CGWindowID + bounds + owning PID + title), and capture_window(window_id) backed by CGWindowListCreateImage. Adds WindowSpec variants so a single QuartzServer can expose either the full display, one display, or one specific app window. Keeps the existing main-display path as the default. Tests verify the public API on the agent's macOS host.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show render width in kittui-md stats
description: Update kittui-md --stats to include render.width_cells, matching metadata-json render provenance. Add targeted tests and docs, keeping changes narrow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document latest kittui-md utility modes
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --links, --footnotes, --images, --html, and --tables utility modes if any are missing after recent work. Validate with docs checks and kittui-md build.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md references-only mode for links images and footnotes
description: Add a kittui-md --references mode that prints only external/document references: links, images, footnote references, and footnote definitions. This complements --outline and --metadata-json with a human-readable references scan. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK scrollback and wait helper status
description:
Docs follow-up after typed SDK scrollback/wait helpers landed. Update SDK/WM docs to mention `ScrollbackSnapshot`, `SurfaceHandle::read_scrollback`, `wait_text[_ms]`, and `wait_output[_ms]` alongside existing CLI/socket automation paths.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md and docs/wm.md or docs/README.md.
- Mention helpers are gated by read capability.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect typed SDK read/wait automation helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: refresh semantic surfaces adapter status
description:
Docs drift: docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md still describes browser DOM/ARIA and accessibility adapters as planned, even though browser extraction/publish/action/CLI inspection and accessibility safe core/action routing/role remaps have landed. Refresh the adapter status section without changing protocol design.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md adapter/source sections and follow-up list.
- Mention landed browser DOM/ARIA/DevTools and accessibility safe core/action routing status.
- Clarify direct platform bindings and richer lifecycle remain follow-up work.
- Keep docs-only and avoid overlap with kitty probe docs.

Acceptance:
- Semantic surfaces protocol doc no longer calls landed browser/accessibility adapter work merely planned.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm native surface coverage kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm native surface coverage inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add native-surfaces-scene-json/surface-coverage-scene-json and native-surfaces-kitty/surface-coverage-kitty aliases that render ArchitectureContract first-party native surface coverage as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing text/json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm native-surfaces-scene-json emits a kittui Scene summarizing all_ready and surface count.
- kittwm native-surfaces-kitty/native-surfaces-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing native-surfaces and native-surfaces-json outputs remain unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from current ArchitectureContract coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed pane frame presented event
description:
SDK/docs follow-up for runtime `pane_frame_presented` events.

Scope:
- Add typed SDK parsing for `KittwmEvent::PaneFramePresented` using existing `EventEnvelope` detail accessors/envelope style.
- Add parser test using non-payload frame metadata.
- Update docs event lists and SDK plan status to reflect resize/input/frame event coverage.
- Do not change daemon/session runtime behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients get typed frame-presented events.
- Docs list `pane_frame_presented` with metadata and no-payload safety.
- Targeted kittwm-sdk event tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: performance budget for split rendering and resize
description: QA gap: once graphics chrome/fonts/splits are fixed, measure frame time and input latency for one pane, two panes, four panes, overlay open/close, and host resize. Add performance doctor/bench outputs or scripts. Acceptance: reproducible benchmark reports p50/p95 frame time, upload bytes, skipped uploads, and latency; regressions can be compared across changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md exposes structured math metadata
description: Math expressions now render as placeholders, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured math data. Add inline/display math metadata, include it in metadata JSON and plain/rich metadata sections, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete FFI ABI surface and generated header workflow
description: DESIGN.md defines a larger C ABI: runtime_configure, render_json, unplace, last_error, probe_json, bytes_free, abi_version_check, explicit byte lengths, cbindgen header snapshots, and ABI drift checks. Current kittui-ffi exposes runtime_new/free, place_json, string_free, abi_version, and a hand-maintained header/test scaffold. Implement the remaining ABI functions, preserve panic-safe boundaries, generate/update kittui.h through an xtask/cbindgen workflow, and add ABI snapshot validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: complete ffi abi surface and header workflow

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes source document metrics
description: Add source-level metrics to kittui-md --metadata-json, including byte length and line count, so downstream tools can correlate rendered metadata with input size. Keep schema_version stable and add tests for the metrics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document render batch PNG output
description:
Large gap: docs mention single-scene `kittui render --out`, but not the new scene-array `--out-dir` batch render path. Shell/platform users need to discover deterministic per-scene PNG artifact generation.

Scope:
- Update README/DESIGN render examples to mention `kittui render scenes.json --out-dir DIR`.
- Document deterministic `scene-00000.png` naming and JSON/dry-run manifest briefly.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention batch render-only PNG output for scene arrays.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser binary: first-class native browser app
description: Operator direction: browser should be a first-class native kittwm app, not a demo subcommand. Provide a `kittwm-browser` binary (or equivalent) that uses the kittwm native app protocol to replace/spawn a browser pane, then drives headless Chrome for screenshots and input. Parent epic: bd-031e54; depends on native app protocol wiring.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose native PTY scrollback snapshots
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: native kittwm panes expose only the current screen via READ_TEXT. Once output scrolls off-screen, controllers and users cannot inspect prior output without polling fast enough. A terminal WM needs a native scrollback buffer and a socket/CLI surface for shell automation.

Scope:
- Track bounded native PTY scrollback lines when the screen scrolls.
- Expose a `PtyTerminalApp::scrollback_snapshot()` helper.
- Carry scrollback side-data through native pane status without serializing it in status JSON.
- Add socket commands `READ_SCROLLBACK <window|focused>` and `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON <window|focused>`.
- Add a `kittwm --read-scrollback WINDOW` CLI wrapper.
- Update help/docs and focused tests.

Acceptance:
- A parser test proves lines pushed off screen enter scrollback.
- Daemon tests prove text and JSON scrollback replies work.
- Targeted kittui-wm/kittui-cli tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: add place_json_at placement override
description:
Large gap: Rust and CLI now expose scene-local rendering with placement overrides (`Runtime::place_at`, `kittui compose --x/--y`), but FFI hosts can only call `kittui_place_json`, which always uses the scene's embedded footprint. Non-Rust platforms still have to mutate scene JSON just to move a rendered asset.

Scope:
- Add `kittui_place_json_at(runtime, scene_json, x, y, out)` to render/cache a scene and place it at a host-supplied terminal position while preserving scene-local dimensions/identity.
- Reuse `Runtime::place_at` internally.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h` / ABI snapshot tests.
- Add targeted FFI tests proving the emitted bytes place at the requested coordinate.

Acceptance:
- FFI test calls `kittui_place_json_at(..., 5, 6, ...)` and observes cursor placement for row 7 col 6.
- Existing `kittui_place_json` behavior remains unchanged.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi --test abi_snapshot` and `cargo build -p kittui-ffi` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm-terminal events kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm-terminal event inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI, not only text. Add event scene-json and kitty graphics modes for bounded SDK event batches, preserving existing --events-ms text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-terminal supports --events-scene-json MS and --events-kitty/--events-graphics MS.
- Event scene labels summarize count, timeout, and event kinds for inspection artifacts.
- Existing --events-ms text behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover parser/model/scene labels without a live kittwm socket.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: composite surface captures RGBA child surfaces
description:
Runtime adapter follow-up: after `push_surface_frame`, composites still require callers to separately call `capture_surface()` on child surfaces.

Scope:
- Add `CompositeFrameSurface::push_surface_capture(x, y, &mut impl NativeSurface)` that captures a child surface and ingests it through `push_surface_frame`.
- Return the captured `SurfaceFrame` or otherwise expose enough result to inspect what was captured.
- Accept RGBA child captures and reject PNG captures via the existing explicit unsupported path.
- Add focused tests using `RgbaFrameSurface` for accepted capture and `KittuiSceneSurface` for PNG rejection.
- Do not wire into live kittwm defaults.

Acceptance:
- Composite code can capture-and-ingest RGBA NativeSurface children in one helper.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: SurfaceCapabilities accessor helpers
description:
NativeSurface metadata ergonomics follow-up: after adding extended capability booleans, callers still manually inspect fields.

Scope:
- Add small accessor methods on `SurfaceCapabilities` for common checks: `can_capture`, `can_send_text`, `can_send_bytes`, `can_receive_focus_events`, `can_emit_surface_events`, `can_resize`, `has_title`, `can_restore`.
- Keep methods pure and simple; do not change field names or serialization (none today).
- Add focused tests covering PTY and capture-only adapters.
- Do not change runtime behavior.

Acceptance:
- Callers have stable method names for extended capability checks.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK session helper status
description:
Docs follow-up after typed SDK session helpers landed. Document `SessionManifest`, `SessionPane`, `Kittwm::session`, and `Kittwm::restore_session` alongside existing CLI `--save-session` / `--restore-session` workflows.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md and docs/wm.md or docs/README.md with concise current-state notes.
- Mention capability gating at a high level (read session vs restore/control).
- Keep docs-only and do not overlap with SDK event parser work.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect typed SDK session save/restore helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui proof CLI command and Rust harness
description:
Add 'kittui proof' subcommand that, for each combination of (transport, animation on/off, unicode-placeholder vs absolute placement, single vs multi-placement, with/without subcell offset), prints labeled output of the exact escape bytes (hex + raw) and an optional --emit mode that prints them straight to the terminal so the operator can paste/run in Ghostty/kitty for visual verification. Mirror in Rust integration tests that snapshot the protocol bytes for each combination so 'cargo test -p kittui-cli proof' fails on any drift.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: NativeSurface pointer input hook
description:
Docs follow-up for NativeSurface pointer input hook.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md, docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md, and/or docs/README.md to mention `NativeSurface::send_surface_pointer` and the current XWindowSurface implementation.
- Clarify PTY/socket SGR mouse routing remains separate and live session defaults are unchanged.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect pointer hook status and current limitations.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured footnote metadata
description: Footnote references and definitions now render visibly, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured footnote data. Add footnote metadata for definitions, include it in JSON/plain/rich metadata where appropriate, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm bench: measure capture pipeline (captures/s, p50/p95/p99, bytes/s)
description: Add 'kitwm bench [--seconds N] [--capture SPEC]' that runs raw_frames() in a tight loop for N seconds (default 3), records per-iteration latency, and prints: captures/s, mean/p50/p95/p99/max latency, total bytes captured, throughput MB/s. Supports --json. No raw mode, no rendering. Gives operators a real perf number for their host without spelunking /tmp/kittui-wm.log. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --pick-window: live Mac window picker that captures the chosen one
description: Add --pick-window flag to kitwm. On macOS with --features sck, lists CGWindowList::list_app_windows() in a kittui-rendered menu, lets the operator type to filter, arrows to navigate, Enter to surface that window as the capture target. Then runs the regular kitwm session against QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)). Visible new capability per overnight loop directive. Parent: bd-ed1c33.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xquartz: spawn XQuartz in nolisten mode, attach via x11rb
description:
New crate kittui-xquartz behind feature 'xquartz'. spawn() launches /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen $DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac or attaches to an existing :N. Implements XServer trait by delegating to kittui-xvfb's x11rb code path (extract that into a shared helper if needed). Detects XQuartz install at /opt/X11/bin or /Library/X11. Returns Unavailable if not found. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-72e4e6
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: doctor daily-driver readiness hints

Daily-driver UX source work: improve `kittwm doctor` with actionable daily-driver recommendations.

Scope:
