Acceptance:
- `kittwm doctor` helps a user understand what to do next to make kittwm usable.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: expose batch placement origin API
description:
Large gap: Rust and CLI now support batch placement origins, but the FFI/TypeScript platform bindings still only expose batch placement at embedded scene coordinates. External platforms using kittui as their renderer need the same group-origin primitive.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_at(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` using `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin`.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyAt(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript bindings/types.
- Add targeted FFI and TypeScript tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI batch-at-origin returns bytes for a scene array and rejects invalid input consistently with existing batch API.
- TypeScript `placeManyAt` calls the new symbol and returns a string.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi place_many_json_at -- --nocapture`, ABI snapshot, and `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical empty workspace landing surface
description: Dogfood graphics gap: empty workspace should be a kittui-rendered landing surface, not mostly text. Render a graphical empty state with top bar, subtle background/translucent panel, quick actions, and hints for terminal/help/examples. Acceptance: launching kittwm with no panes presents a polished kittui scene in graphics mode; keyboard shortcuts still work; terminal fallback remains concise.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: reorganize CLI help for daily-driver use
description:
Daily-driver UX: `kittwm` help is too hard to read.

Scope:
- Rework the `kittwm` printed help into a concise, grouped command tree with common daily-driver flows first.
- Keep all existing commands/flags working; this is presentation only.
- Include clear sections for starting kittwm, inspecting a running WM, pane control, input/automation, semantic/session surfaces, and diagnostics/backends.
- Include examples for common workflows (launch terminal, inspect panes/status, read text, wait for output).
- Add/adjust focused test to assert major section headings and key commands are present.
- No daemon/socket/runtime behavior changes.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --help` is scannable and teaches basic usage without reading docs.
- Targeted kittwm binary tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive group/clip/composite scene helpers
description:
Platform renderer substrate gap: Python/TypeScript primitive scene helpers cover rectangles, gradients, images, glows, and scanlines, but not the core scene-composition primitives (`group`, `clip`, `composite`, `mask`). External platforms and shell-adjacent scripts need these to build reusable kittui/kittwm chrome and effects without hand-authoring raw JSON.

Scope:
- Add Python scene helpers for `group`, `clip`, `composite`, and `mask` nodes/layers.
- Add TypeScript scene helpers and d.ts typings for the same primitives.
- Keep helpers primitive-only; do not add high-level widgets to core.
- Update README/tests for both bindings.

Acceptance:
- Python unit tests cover JSON shape for new helpers.
- TypeScript tests cover JSON shape for new helpers.
- `PYTHONPATH=bindings/python python3 -m unittest discover bindings/python`, `npm test --prefix bindings/ts`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add document counts to kittui-md metadata JSON
description: Add a top-level counts object to kittui-md --metadata-json/--json with component, heading, heading_anchor, link, image, table, footnote, definition, math, html, metadata_block, and code_block counts. Add targeted tests and docs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: make zlib auto compression threshold-based
description: Implement threshold-based kitty zlib compression decisions for raw graphics frames. Scope: make KITTUI_KITTY_COMPRESSION=auto size/cadence aware rather than unconditional zlib, add KITTUI_ZLIB_MIN_BYTES, and cover small/large raw frame decisions with tests. Follow-up from bd-3c0dd1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui: add batch placement origin API
description:
Large gap: the CLI can now move JSON scene batches as a group, but the core Rust renderer API still only exposes `place_batch(&[Scene])` at embedded scene coordinates. External platform bindings and shell host libraries need the same group-origin primitive without mutating every scene footprint themselves.

Scope:
- Add `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin(&[Scene], x, y)` to place a scene batch with its minimum x/y remapped to a runtime origin while preserving relative spacing.
- Factor batch concatenation through placement results so `place_batch` remains unchanged.
- Add targeted `kittui` library tests for group-origin placement and empty batches.
- Update CLI compose batch origin handling to use the new library API instead of cloning/mutating scene footprints.

Acceptance:
- Core API returns batch footprints at the requested origin with relative offsets preserved.
- Empty batches still succeed.
- CLI compose batch origin tests continue to pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: clean kittwm first-launch UX
description:
Docs follow-up for clean first-launch kittwm UX.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md and docs/README.md as needed to describe empty workspace default, top bar, terminal-launch shortcut, Ctrl-A ? help overlay, and env knob for startup terminal compatibility if added.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs match the new default launch behavior.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: route host mouse reports into native panes
description:
Large native WM gap: the kittwm native terminal loop enables SGR mouse reporting on the host terminal, but stdin bytes are forwarded mostly raw to the focused PTY. Host mouse reports are not translated to pane-local coordinates or routed to the pane under the pointer, so mouse-aware TUIs cannot be used naturally in native panes and panes may receive inappropriate outer-terminal escape bytes.

Scope:
- Parse host SGR mouse input in the native terminal loop using `kittui_input`.
- Route mouse events to the pane whose app area contains the pointer, translating host cell coordinates to pane-local 1-indexed coordinates.
- Reuse native mouse event payload encoding and only inject when the target pane has compatible mouse reporting modes.
- Focus the clicked/scroll-target pane when routing a press/scroll event.
- Preserve existing keyboard/prefix behavior.
- Add focused unit tests for coordinate translation/routing helpers.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove host coordinates map to pane-local mouse payloads and reject title/outside areas.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md grid alias for tables
description: Add --grid as a friendly alias for kittui-md --tables so users can inspect Markdown table/grid layout with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: expose surface events on NativeSurface
description:
Surface runtime gap: side-effect events (title/bell/clipboard/notification) are currently available on PTY internals but not part of the common `NativeSurface` abstraction.

Scope:
- Add a default `take_surface_events(&mut self) -> Vec<SurfaceEvent>` method to the `NativeSurface` trait.
- Override it for `PtyTerminalApp` by delegating to its terminal surface event drain.
- Leave capture-only adapters default-empty.
- Add focused tests showing PTY/native surface trait drains events and capture-only adapters return empty.
- Do not change daemon event publication semantics or live session behavior beyond using/keeping equivalent drain paths if touched.

Acceptance:
- Common native surface interface can drain semantic side-effect events.
- Existing side-effect behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xvfb: spawn Xvfb, claim display, SHM capture
description:
New crate kittui-xvfb that owns an Xvfb subprocess (or attaches to an existing :N display), connects via XCB, lists toplevel windows, and captures their framebuffer regions via MIT-SHM. Emits per-window (id, rect, rgba_bytes) snapshots and damage rectangles. Test exercises the spawn + connect path under a Linux runner and gracefully skips on macOS.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui panel test: update animation default expectations
description: Follow-up after standardizing panel animation defaults: `crates/kittui-cli/tests/panel_command.rs` still expects legacy 8-frame/800ms metadata for `kittui panel --animate`. Update smoke coverage to assert the new standard 180-frame/3000ms compatibility behavior and include `--animated` where appropriate.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: bindings-python: expose render_many manifest
description:
Large gap: FFI now exposes render-many manifests, but Python still only exposes single-scene `render`. Python automation needs the one-call batch render manifest for preview/artifact pipelines.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_render_many_json` in Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.render_many(scenes) -> dict` returning parsed manifest with base64 PNG entries.
- Add fake-CDLL tests for success and failure detail.
- Update Python README API/example.

Acceptance:
- Python tests pass.
- `render_many` accepts dict/JSON mixed scenes and returns parsed manifest.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md references-json inspection mode
description: Add --references-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for combined Markdown references: links, images, footnote references, and footnote definitions. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: make render_json return PNG bytes
description:
Large gap: the FFI/header/Python binding call `kittui_render_json` a render-only PNG API, but the implementation currently calls `Runtime::place` and returns terminal escape bytes. External platforms needing previews/non-terminal embedding get the wrong bytes.

Scope:
- Add public `Runtime::render_png(&Scene) -> Vec<u8>` that renders without terminal capability checks, placement, upload state, or kitty escapes.
- Make `kittui_render_json` parse a scene and return PNG bytes from `Runtime::render_png`.
- Add Rust/FFI tests that assert PNG signature and no kitty escapes for render_json.
- Re-run Python binding tests to ensure its new render method now matches real ABI semantics.

Acceptance:
- `kittui_render_json` returns bytes beginning with the PNG signature.
- Render-only path does not require kitty terminal support.
- Targeted kittui/kittui-ffi/Python tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add native surface placement contract helpers
description: Add SDK helpers to build `SurfacePlacementContract` directly from `NativeSurfaceContract` / `ArchitectureContract` by surface name or kind. This lets apps inspect chrome surfaces like kittwm-bar as placement contracts even when they are not spawned via `SurfaceSpec`. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: semantic publish/action surfaces should survive pane lifecycle
description: QA gap: semantic snapshots/published component trees/actions may become stale across pane close, replace, restore, focus changes, and browser app restarts. Audit state cleanup and stale-component behavior. Acceptance: closed/replaced panes do not serve stale semantic trees; focused semantic actions route only to current pane; tests cover publish then close/replace.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: refresh accessibility adapter status in docs map
description:
Docs drift: docs/README.md still says accessibility-tree adapters are planned even though the safe adapter core, mapping, action routing, and role remaps have landed. Refresh the docs map/status bullet.

Scope:
- Update docs/README.md accessibility status only.
- Mention safe adapter core/action routing/role remaps are landed; direct macOS AX/Linux AT-SPI platform bindings remain follow-up.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs map accurately reflects accessibility adapter status.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: mouse coordinate routing across chrome, top bar, and app area
description: QA gap: host SGR mouse reports must be translated correctly through reserved top bar, pane chrome, and app bounds. Add coverage for clicks/drag/scroll in top bar, border/title chrome, pane app area, and outside panes. Acceptance: app receives only app-area coordinates in pane-local cells when mouse modes are enabled; chrome/top-bar clicks do not corrupt app input.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm keymap defaults: Ctrl-A d launcher alias inspired by collective WM bindings
description: Use ~/collective/modules/home-manager/wm.nix as inspiration for terminal-safe defaults. Add Ctrl-A d as an open-launcher alias (collective uses Mod+d) while keeping Ctrl-A Enter launch and existing Ctrl-A C-hjkl focus / h-j-k-l swap. Ensure kitwm keymap and --check show the new alias and runtime Ctrl-A d opens the launcher overlay/launch action. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1/customizable bindings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md code-blocks-json inspection mode
description: Add --code-blocks-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown fenced/indented code blocks, emitting schema_version plus indexed language/text records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add batch/many render entrypoints that share resolved config and runtime state
description: Operator direction: common usage should support setting config/env once, then calling kittui many times or in a batch in that scope. Add well-documented batch entrypoints that resolve config once, reuse Runtime/cache/probe state, render/place many scenes efficiently, and expose this through CLI (e.g. JSONL or manifest input), Rust library, and FFI/TS binding surfaces. Ensure per-item overrides remain possible and tests verify repeated calls do not re-probe/re-upload unnecessarily.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make layout actions rebuild tiled slots
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm exposes `layout.toggle-split` and `layout.balance`, but these still only update footer state. The compositor/layout already supports tiled slots, so layout actions should rebuild actual tiled rectangles for current windows.

Scope:
- Make the session own a mutable layout snapshot derived from the provided initial layout.
- Add a helper that rebuilds tiled slots for current compositor windows in row or column orientation using the current layout bounds or visible window rect bounds.
- Wire `ToggleSplit` and `BalanceWindows` to rebuild actual `Layout` slots while preserving footer state.
- Add tests for layout tiling helpers.

Acceptance:
- Toggle split changes real tiled window footprints in capture-backed sessions.
- Balance recomputes equal slots for current windows.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix nix build PTY shell resolution
description: nix run/build failed in kittui-cli lib test native_pane_statuses_include_dirty_frame_metrics because PtyTerminalApp fell back to bare sh when /bin/sh was unavailable in the pure build environment. Fix by resolving sh/bash on PATH before falling back. Filed from operator report during dev-2 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md stats mode summarizes document counts
description: Add a kittui-md --stats mode that prints concise human-readable counts for source bytes/lines, components, headings, links, images, tables, footnotes, definitions, math, HTML, and code blocks. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md components-json inspection mode
description: Add --components-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for generated Markdown UI components, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/rect/text records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui affordance controls: add animation option to ControlComponent
description: Continue animation coverage into `kittui-affordances` reusable control components. Inventory: button, checkbox, radio, radio_group, text_input, text_area, select_list, menu, slider, progress, tabs, and split_pane lower through `ControlComponent::to_scene` but have no animation option. Acceptance: controls expose an animation builder/options with 60fps/180-frame/3s defaults, `to_scene` emits a loop-closing Scene.animation and labelled phase-reactive layer, gallery components can opt in later, and unit tests cover representative controls.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm --attach -c CMD: one-shot daemon command for scripting
description: Add -c/--command flag to 'kitwm --attach' so operators can fire a single daemon command and read the reply without entering the interactive REPL. Useful for scripting: 'kitwm --attach -c WINDOWS | jq...'. Exit code maps to daemon ERR/OK. Parent: bd-031e54 (extends bd-5b8dd6).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md mode categories catalog
description: Add --mode-categories and --mode-categories-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list supported mode categories and counts before filtering by category. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Support placement_id (p=) and subcell offset (X=, Y=) on placement_command
description:
placement_command currently emits a=p,U=1,i=,c=,r= with no placement-id and no subcell pixel offset. The spec allows multiple placements of the same image via p= and subcell origin via X=/Y= for fractional positioning. Extend placement_command (or add a new builder) to accept placement_id and pixel offsets; thread through Runtime::place; expose via CLI.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: guard raw kitty frame memory and tmux graphics
description:
Runtime memory/safety bug: native kittwm's kitty-graphics path uploads full raw RGBA frames every tick. In tmux this can explode memory because passthrough graphics payloads are large, and the raw-frame hot path does not explicitly delete prior image data before replacing an image id. Add immediate safeguards: explicit image deletion before raw frame re-upload, and default native kittwm to the pure terminal renderer when running under tmux unless the user opts into graphics.

Scope:
- Update `Runtime::place_raw_frame` to emit a kitty image delete for an existing raw-frame image id before uploading the replacement frame.
- Preserve stable image ids/placements and current direct-Ghostty behavior as much as possible.
- Detect `$TMUX` in native kittwm and default to `KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER=terminal` behavior unless `KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER=kitty|graphics` is explicitly set.
- Document why tmux graphics are guarded and how to override.
- Add tests for raw-frame delete-before-upload grammar and renderer selection.

Acceptance:
- Raw frame replacement includes delete grammar for already-placed images.
- tmux defaults to terminal renderer; explicit graphics override works.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: local command catalog
description:
Daily-driver UX: add a local command catalog that does not require a running WM.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm commands` for a grouped text catalog of local subcommands/aliases.
- Add `kittwm commands-json` for machine-readable local command catalog JSON.
- Catalog should include common lifecycle, help, inspect, action, pane-control, app/session/diagnostic commands and aliases.
- Keep socket `HELP_JSON` unchanged (that catalogs daemon verbs); this catalogs local CLI commands.
- Add focused tests for text and JSON outputs.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users/tools can discover local CLI aliases without starting kittwm.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: CLI shortcut list for clean first launch
description:
Clean first-launch UX follow-up: Ctrl-A ? shows in-session help, but there is no non-interactive CLI wrapper for the same native shortcut list.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm --shortcuts` (or similar) to print the native shortcut list used by Ctrl-A ?.
- Reuse/share the existing help list where practical.
- Add targeted CLI test for output containing terminal launch, help toggle, and exit shortcuts.
- Do not change live session input behavior.

Acceptance:
- Users can discover shortcuts without launching or attaching to kittwm.
- In-session help and CLI help stay aligned from shared data.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: add scene-json/stdin compose pipeline
description:
Large gap: kittui is intended to be a core renderer for shell scripts/external platforms, but `kittui compose` is path-only and the CLI cannot emit reusable scene JSON for pipelines.

Scope:
- Teach `kittui compose -` to read a scene JSON document from stdin.
- Add a scene JSON output mode for simple scene-generating commands (`box`, `gradient`, `glow`) so shell scripts can pipe generated scenes into `compose`.
- Ensure JSON is the actual serializable `Scene` shape consumed by compose, not just byte-count metadata.
- Update README/DESIGN examples where they currently imply `compose -`/scene pipelines.

Acceptance:
- `kittui box --scene-json ... | kittui compose - --dry-run --json` works.
- Tests cover stdin compose and scene-json validity for at least one generated scene.
- Existing placement/dry-run behaviour remains compatible.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: align client timeout with WAIT_TEXT_MS
description:
Gap after adding `WAIT_TEXT_MS`: the socket parser allows waits up to 60000ms, but the shared client helpers still use a fixed 10s read timeout. Long explicit waits can still fail client-side before the daemon replies.

Scope:
- Compute client read timeout from the outgoing command.
- For `WAIT_TEXT_MS <window> <ms> ...`, use `ms + small margin`, capped safely above the daemon maximum.
- Keep normal commands at the existing default timeout.
- Add unit tests for timeout selection.

Acceptance:
- `WAIT_TEXT_MS ... 60000 ...` gets a client timeout longer than 60 seconds.
- Normal commands keep the 10 second timeout.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane resize keybindings
description:
Large gap: native kittwm pane weights can be controlled over the socket, but the local terminal WM keymap still cannot resize panes directly. A terminal WM should let the operator grow/shrink the focused pane without leaving the session or using an external client.

Scope:
- Add prefix keybindings in the native PTY loop: `Ctrl-A +`/`Ctrl-A =` grows the focused pane, `Ctrl-A _`/`Ctrl-A <` shrinks it.
- Recompute weighted layouts and clear/redraw after key-driven resize.
- Update the native footer hint to mention resize keys and current focused weight.
- Add a small pure helper test if needed.

Acceptance:
- Focused pane weight changes with local keybindings and remains clamped to at least 1.
- Layout is recomputed immediately after key-driven resize.
- `cargo test -p kittui-cli session::native_pane_tests::native_adjust_weight_clamps_to_one -- --nocapture` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace CGDisplayCreateImage with ScreenCaptureKit on macOS 12.3+
description: QuartzServer::capture currently uses CGDisplayCreateImage, which is soft-deprecated on macOS 14 and returns a black/dimmed frame on macOS 14/15/26 even when Screen Recording is granted. Operator reported black screen + ~1 FPS on macOS 26.4. Replace with ScreenCaptureKit (SCStream + SCStreamConfiguration) running on its own thread with latest-frame-wins, falling back to the legacy path only on macOS < 12.3. Add a non-black-frame assertion to the round-trip test. Affects both whole-display and per-window capture paths in CaptureTarget.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Expand kittui-wm from placeholder substrate to split/stack/tab layout semantics
description: DESIGN.md says kittui-wm currently reserves WindowTree/WindowGeometry but full split/stack/tab semantics and per-window scene generation should arrive after tmux showcase exercises diff-driven composition. Current crate description/source mark kittui-wm as a placeholder scaffold. Implement basic split, stack, and tab layout models, z-order handling, conversion to Composition entries, and tests for deterministic geometry output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: route accessibility semantic actions
description: Route semantic focus/actions through platform accessibility objects for AX/AT-SPI adapters. Support focus, activate, toggle, set value, insert text, select, and stale-component/permission errors. Follow-up from bd-4a49aa.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: typed surface side-effect events
description:
Follow-up after native surface side-effect events landed: teach `kittwm-sdk` typed event parsing about `surface_title_changed`, `surface_bell`, `surface_clipboard_set`, and `surface_notification` instead of leaving them as unknown events.

Scope:
- Add `KittwmEvent` variants for the four surface side-effect event kinds.
- Update `KittwmEvent::kind()` and parser mapping.
- Add targeted event parser tests for title, bell, clipboard, and notification detail payloads.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK event subscribers get typed variants for native side-effect events.
- `cargo test -p kittwm-sdk event -- --nocapture` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add JSON native socket status and panes
description:
Large gap: native kittwm socket control is now scriptable, but STATUS/PANES are human-oriented text. Shell and external controllers need machine-readable JSON for robust automation.

Scope:
- Add native session socket commands `STATUS_JSON` and `PANES_JSON`.
- Include pending command count, pane count, focused window, layout axis, and per-pane window/title/focused records.
- Keep existing text `STATUS` and `PANES` behavior.
- Add targeted queue reply tests and document commands in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- `STATUS_JSON` returns parseable JSON with `pending`, `panes`, `focus`, and `layout`.
- `PANES_JSON` returns parseable JSON with `panes_detail` records.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: model clipboard bell and notification surface events
description: Promote OSC clipboard, bell, title, and notification side effects into semantic SurfaceEvent values with runtime policy for host forwarding, OS clipboard, denial, and shell display.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add semantic snapshot publish path
description:
Semantic runtime follow-up: the SDK can build semantic snapshots and the daemon can expose a synthetic snapshot for PTY panes, but there is no runtime queue/publish path for first-party semantic SDK apps to publish their own component trees. Add a small design/implementation slice for a semantic snapshot publish verb and storage model.

Scope:
- Define/add native socket command skeleton for SEMANTIC_PUBLISH <window|focused> <snapshot-json> or an equivalent surface-specific publish path.
- Validate schema_version/surface/root minimally using SDK protocol types.
- Store latest semantic snapshot per pane/window in daemon state and have SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT prefer published semantic tree over terminal text fallback.
- Add tests for publish/readback and invalid JSON rejection.

Acceptance:
- First-party SDK examples have a daemon endpoint to publish semantic snapshots.
- Existing PTY text-area fallback still works when no snapshot is published.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Window picker: floating panel + Tab cycle to surface a Mac/X window
description:
Inside the running kittui-wm session, hotkey (default Mod+Space) opens a kittui floating panel listing every window from QuartzServer::list_app_windows() + every X11 window across attached backends. Arrow keys navigate, Enter surfaces the window (calls QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)) and attaches as a new backend, or pins an existing one). Mod+W cycles surfaced windows. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-6a36e1
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane control CLI wrappers
description:
Large terminal-WM usability gap: native kittwm now has first-class CLI wrappers for inspection and automation, but core pane control (spawn/focus/layout/move/resize/rename/close/balance) still requires raw `kittwm --attach -c ...` protocol strings. A terminal WM should expose these as stable CLI verbs/flags for shell scripts and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add CLI wrappers for native pane control:
  - `--spawn-pty <cmd>`
  - `--focus-pane <window>` / `--focus-next` / `--focus-prev`
  - `--close-pane <window|focused>`
  - `--layout <columns|rows>`
  - `--move-pane <window|focused> <dir>`
  - `--resize-pane <window|focused> <amount>`
  - `--balance-panes`
  - `--rename-pane <window> <title>`
- Route through default socket resolution, preserve command/title payload case, validate simple tokens where useful.
- Add help text, request-builder tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Wrappers generate expected native socket requests.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document browser semantic publishing workflow
description:
Documentation follow-up after browser semantic extractor and publish loop landed. Update semantic docs/quickstart to explain browser DOM/ARIA semantic extraction and best-effort publishing from kittwm-browser.

Scope:
- Document how kittwm-browser publishes DOM/ARIA semantic snapshots when KITTWM_SOCKET/KITTWM_WINDOW are present.
- Explain screenshot fallback, debounce/duplicate behavior, and limitations.
- Link to browser semantic adapter plan docs.
- Include quick commands for launching browser and reading SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT.

Acceptance:
- Docs describe current browser semantic publishing behavior and limitations.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md widgets alias for components
description: Add --widgets as a friendly alias for kittui-md --components so users can inspect generated UI component records with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Markdown renderer proof gallery for components, links, and image-backed tables
description: Add a proof/gallery command or example that renders a markdown document exercising all new UI components: h1/h2/h3, title/banner/header/footer, textboxes, textchips/link chips, code blocks, lists, and image-backed tables. Include screenshots or protocol output fixtures showing connected table borders, highlighted links, and a z-ordered background image underneath a table. This should become the acceptance harness for the markdown renderer epic.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: update native kittwm socket controls
description:
Large gap: native kittwm socket/control-plane docs are stale. The code now supports move, resize, balance, weights, and pane geometry in PANES_JSON, but docs only mention spawn/focus/status/basic layout. Users and external controllers need the command catalog and metadata model documented.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md native session/socket section with MOVE_PANE, RESIZE_PANE, BALANCE_PANES, RENAME_PANE, HELP_JSON, and PANES_JSON geometry/weight fields.
- Update README native status bullets/attach examples for move/resize/balance.
- Keep examples concise and accurate.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention the new native socket commands and local resize/balance keybindings.
- Docs mention PANES_JSON includes weight and geometry fields.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm-launch plan kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm-launch dry-run/planning inspectable as kittui/kitty-native UI. The launcher already uses typed SDK SurfaceSpec for terminal/browser; expose that plan as a labelled kittui scene and optional kitty graphics so first-party helper UX is not text-only.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-launch supports --plan-scene-json and --plan-kitty/--plan-graphics for LaunchPlan.
- Plan scene labels include backend/mode/query/command for inspection artifacts.
- Existing --dry-run/--status behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover parser and scene labels without a live kittwm socket.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed exact bytes paste and mouse helpers
description:
SDK automation gap: socket/CLI expose exact byte input, bracketed paste payloads, and mouse injection (`SEND_BYTES_B64`, `PASTE_BYTES_B64`, `SEND_MOUSE`) but SDK clients only have text/line/key helpers. Add typed SDK helpers for these remaining input paths.

Scope:
- Add `SurfaceHandle::send_bytes_b64` or `send_bytes(&[u8])` using base64 internally.
- Add `SurfaceHandle::paste_bytes(&[u8])` using `PASTE_BYTES_B64`.
- Add a typed/simple mouse helper for known event labels and pane-local col/row.
- Gate with existing SendInput capability.
- Add targeted SDK unit/socket tests for command formatting and capability denial.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can use exact bytes, paste bytes, and mouse events without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make wait-text timeout configurable and client-safe
description:
Large automation reliability gap: `WAIT_TEXT` waits up to 5s, but kittwm socket clients use a 2s read timeout. The first long wait path added to the WM control plane can therefore fail client-side before the daemon replies. Scripts also cannot tune the wait duration.

Scope:
- Raise client read timeout enough for WAIT_TEXT by default.
- Add `WAIT_TEXT_MS <window|focused> <ms> <needle>` for explicit timeouts.
- Add `kittwm --wait-text-ms <ms> <window|focused> <needle>` CLI wrapper.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage, tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Default `WAIT_TEXT` no longer exceeds client read timeout.
- Explicit millisecond timeout validates arguments and queues/waits correctly.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: relative placement (P=,Q=,H=,V=) not implemented
description: Kitty placement grammar supports anchoring relative to another placement via P=<src image id>, Q=<src placement id>, H=<col offset>, V=<row offset>. PlacementOptions has none of these fields, so 'relative placement' is just missing. File as separate work since it's additive grammar + new tests. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --serve + --status: minimal Unix-socket daemon protocol (PING/STATUS)
description: kitwm --serve binds a Unix socket at /tmp/kitwm-$USER.sock (override via KITWM_SOCK env), spawns an accept thread, and answers single-line text requests: 'PING' -> 'PONG'; 'STATUS' -> 'pid=N uptime=Xs sock=...'. kitwm --status connects to that socket, sends 'STATUS', prints the reply, exits non-zero if no daemon. Lays the protocol foundation for bd-aaea73 attach client without committing to a wire format yet. RAII Drop on the server unlinks the socket. Parent: bd-031e54 (decomposed from bd-fb5d9d).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render kittwm-bar as live graphical component by default
description: Dogfood graphics gap: kittwm-bar exists as a scene artifact but the live top bar still often behaves like text. Integrate the kittwm-bar model as the default live graphical top bar in graphics mode, including workspace, state, time, and display/chrome metadata. Acceptance: live top bar is a kittui-rendered scene/component by default outside pure-terminal fallback; --scene-json artifact matches live rendering labels/metadata.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: kittui-rendered split borders and gutters
description: Dogfood graphics gap: split pane borders/gutters should be kittui-rendered graphical surfaces, not ASCII/text chrome. Implement kittui scene/component borders for split boundaries, focused/unfocused states, and gutters that align with pane app_bounds and top-bar reservation. Acceptance: split panes show graphical kittui-rendered borders/gutters in graphics mode; borders do not overlap app content; terminal fallback remains explicit/automatic.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: typed chrome JSON helper
description:
SDK gap: daemon exposes `CHROME_JSON` and CLI has `kittwm --chrome-json`, but `kittwm-sdk` lacks a typed convenience helper for it.

Scope:
- Add `Kittwm::chrome()` / `chrome_json()` returning `ChromeReservationStatus` by sending `CHROME_JSON`.
- Reuse existing `ChromeReservationStatus` type and accessors.
- Keep capability policy consistent with existing status/panes helpers (inspection-only, no mutation).
- Add a focused Unix socket unit test that verifies the helper sends `CHROME_JSON` and parses workspace/top_bar_rows/tilable_rows.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- SDK consumers can call `client.chrome()` or `client.chrome_json()`.
- Targeted SDK tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui primitives: add standard animation flags to box/gradient/glow
description:
Continue animation coverage for primitive `kittui` scene commands after inline and top-level affordances. Inventory:
- `kittui box` has legacy `--animate frames@cycle_ms`; add the standard `--animated --fps --frames` contract while preserving legacy behavior.
- `kittui gradient` is static; add the standard contract and a labelled glow/sheen animation layer.
- `kittui glow` is static despite containing a phase-reactive glow node; add the standard contract.
Acceptance: box/gradient/glow support 60fps/180-frame/3s defaults, scene JSON exposes the animation, and smoke tests cover the new flags.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: document accessibility semantic action routing status
description:
Documentation follow-up after accessibility action routing lands. Update accessibility semantic docs and docs map with current platform-neutral action routing core status and remaining platform-specific bindings work.

Scope:
- Document supported accessibility semantic actions and error behavior.
- Update docs/README status for accessibility action routing.
- Clarify what remains platform binding work vs safe core.

Acceptance:
- Docs match landed accessibility routing core.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add SurfaceSpec composition plane helpers
description: Add SDK convenience methods on `SurfaceSpec` to derive its architecture composition plane and z-index from the current first-party native surface contract. This lets apps ask a typed surface request where it belongs in the kitty graphics compositor without manually calling `native_surface_contract`. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: support compose JSON scene arrays
description:
Large gap: kittui is intended to be a shell/external-platform renderer, but `kittui compose -` accepts only a single Scene JSON document. Hosts that generate many scene assets must invoke the CLI repeatedly instead of sending one JSON array and getting one batch placement result.

Scope:
- Teach `kittui compose <path>|-` to accept either one Scene or an array of Scenes.
- For arrays, use the existing runtime batch placement path and emit concatenated upload/placement/embed streams.
- Add JSON/dry-run output for batch compose with count, image ids, footprints, byte counts, and optional channel strings under `--json-bytes`.
- Reject `--x/--y` placement overrides on batch input for now with a clear error.
- Add targeted integration tests for stdin array batch compose.

Acceptance:
- `printf '[scene, scene]' | kittui compose - --dry-run --json` returns batch JSON with count 2.
- Non-JSON batch compose emits non-empty terminal bytes.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed chrome reservation status
description:
SDK follow-up after runtime exposes chrome reservation metadata in STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON.

Scope:
- Add typed SDK structs/fields/accessors for chrome reservation metadata (workspace id, top_bar_rows, tilable_rows if available).
- Preserve backwards-compatible parsing when fields are absent.
- Add focused SDK serde/accessor tests.
- Do not change daemon/runtime behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can inspect top-bar reservation metadata without raw JSON access.
- Tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md snippets alias for code blocks
description: Add --snippets as a friendly alias for kittui-md --code-blocks so users can extract Markdown code snippets with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: add batch place_many_json API
description:
Large gap: Rust and CLI now support batch scene placement (`Runtime::place_batch`, `kittui compose [scene...]`), but FFI/TS hosts still need to loop one scene per FFI boundary crossing. External platforms need a batch ABI to use kittui as an efficient renderer backend.

Scope:
- Add `kittui_place_many_json(runtime, scenes_json, out)` accepting a JSON array of Scene documents.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch` internally and return concatenated upload+placement+embed bytes.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h` / ABI snapshot tests.
- Add targeted FFI tests for two-scene batch placement and bad non-array input.

Acceptance:
- FFI test passes an array with two scenes and receives non-empty bytes with kitty escapes/placeholders.
- Non-array input returns `BadScene` or equivalent and sets last_error.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi place_many` and `cargo test -p kittui-ffi --test abi_snapshot` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md table glyph atlas: render box drawing as connected image cells
description: Next proposal: complete the table glyph layout by generating a real cell-sized box-drawing glyph atlas and rendering markdown tables through image cells anchored with relative placement. Include a z-ordered background image/panel below tables. Builds on bd-aa77c5/bd-04718f.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed cached clipboard helper
description:
SDK gap after runtime/CLI clipboard policy work: `CLIPBOARD_JSON` exists but kittwm-sdk has no typed helper.

Scope:
- Add typed structs for `CLIPBOARD_JSON` replies and `Kittwm::clipboard()` / `clipboard_json()` helper.
- Gate helper with existing Clipboard capability if available; otherwise add/use a narrow read capability without changing daemon behavior.
- Preserve default-deny semantics: denied JSON parses without payload.
- Add mock transport tests for denied, allowed-empty, and allowed-cached replies.
- Do not change daemon or CLI behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can inspect cached clipboard policy state without raw protocol strings.
- Payload fields are optional/available only when daemon returns them.
- Targeted kittwm-sdk tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: return channelized batch placement JSON
description:
Large gap: platform bindings can batch-place scenes, including at a runtime origin, but only receive one concatenated byte string. CLI users can request channelized JSON (`upload`, `placement`, `embed`, image ids, footprints), while FFI/TypeScript hosts cannot inspect, schedule, or separately write the upload/placement/embed channels.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_channels(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` returning JSON for a batch placed at a group origin.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin` and include `count`, `image_ids`, `footprints`, `upload_bytes`, `placement_bytes`, `embed_bytes`, and raw `upload`/`placement`/`embed` strings.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyChannels(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript and add tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI returns parseable JSON with channel strings and metadata for scene arrays.
- TypeScript returns a parsed object.
- Targeted FFI, ABI snapshot, and TypeScript tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: chrome JSON socket and CLI wrapper
description:
CLI/control-plane gap: chrome reservation metadata is embedded in STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON, but operators and scripts do not have a narrow command/wrapper to inspect just the WM chrome reservation.

Scope:
- Add native socket `CHROME_JSON` returning the same chrome reservation object plus workspace id.
- Add `kittwm --chrome-json` CLI wrapper mapping to `CHROME_JSON`.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON entries.
- Add targeted daemon and CLI command tests.
- Do not change live rendering or SDK types.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --chrome-json` can inspect top-bar reservation metadata without raw `--attach -c` or full STATUS_JSON parsing.
- Existing STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON remain unchanged.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed wait match results
description:
SDK ergonomics follow-up: `SurfaceHandle::wait_text[_ms]` and `wait_output[_ms]` currently return raw daemon reply strings like `MATCH_TEXT window=... bytes=...`. Add typed parsing helpers/results while preserving existing raw methods if useful.

Scope:
- Add a `WaitMatch` struct with match kind/text-vs-output, window, and byte count.
- Add typed helpers such as `wait_text_match_ms`, `wait_output_match_ms`, `wait_text_match`, `wait_output_match` or adjust existing helpers only if backwards-compatible.
- Parse successful daemon replies; continue returning daemon errors through existing Error path.
- Add targeted unit tests for parser and Unix socket command formatting.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK automation clients can get typed wait-match metadata without parsing strings.
- Existing wait helper behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket focus and close pane commands
description:
Large gap: the live native kittwm session socket can report panes and spawn new PTY panes, but it cannot control them. A DISPLAY-like terminal WM needs scriptable focus/close control, not just introspection.

Scope:
- Extend the native session socket queue with control actions in addition to PTY spawns.
- Add socket commands:
  - `FOCUS_PANE <window>` to focus a visible native pane by window token.
  - `CLOSE_PANE <window|focused>` to close a visible pane (when more than one pane exists).
- Drain and apply those actions in the native session loop, reflowing panes and updating status snapshots.
- Add queue parsing/action tests and small focus-selection tests.
- Document commands in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- Queue tests cover FOCUS_PANE and CLOSE_PANE command parsing/draining.
- Session helper tests cover applying focus/close actions to pane metadata.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: spawn browser surfaces via first-party browser app
description:
SDK transport gap: `SurfaceSpec::Browser` exists but `Kittwm::spawn_surface` returns an explicit unsupported error. Add a pragmatic first-party transport path that spawns `kittwm-browser TARGET` as a terminal-backed surface, matching `kittwm-launch --browser`, while keeping richer dedicated browser surface protocol as future work.

Scope:
- Add a helper on `SurfaceSpec` for browser targets if missing.
- Update `Kittwm::spawn_surface` so `SurfaceKind::Browser` queues `SPAWN_PTY kittwm-browser <target>` with safe command quoting/reuse existing conventions.
- Preserve unsupported behavior for `SurfaceKind::Other`.
- Add SDK unit/socket tests for browser spawn command formatting and docs comments noting this is a first-party PTY-backed browser transport.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can request browser surfaces without raw protocol strings.
- Terminal surface behavior remains unchanged.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui/kittwm use mcp-cli and updatable-cli crates
description:
Refactor kittui/kittwm CLI entrypoints to use `mcp-cli` and `updatable-cli` crates, following patterns from tendril/ring-mods/cacophony.

Goal:
- Enable `kittui update` and equivalent update plumbing for release artifacts from self-hosted GitHub runners.
- Align CLI structure with shared mcp/updatable command helpers used elsewhere.

Scope:
- Audit existing workspace and local examples for `mcp-cli` / `updatable-cli` usage.
- Add dependencies/config as needed.
- Wire update command into kittui and/or kittwm CLI without regressing existing commands.
- Preserve current clap UX and existing subcommands.
- Add focused tests/validation for command recognition/help where practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui UI component + markdown rendering layer
description:
Operator request: kittui has boxes, gradients, strips, etc., but needs a first-class UI component layer and a markdown renderer built on it. Scope includes textbox, headings (h1/h2/h3), title/banner/header/footer, text chips, link chips, tables, and markdown-to-kittui rendering. The renderer should use kitty virtual placement plus relative non-virtual positioning anchors where appropriate so composed UI pieces can share connected layout/z-space semantics. Parent/area: kittui renderer + kittwm operator UI.

Operator clarification: this epic should produce a full kittui Markdown viewer, not just a library renderer. The viewer should run as a normal terminal app for rich kitty-graphics Markdown rendering, and also work as a kittwm-native app when launched inside kittwm context.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: dogfood surface handles in built-in native session
description: Refactor the built-in native session to manipulate surfaces through the common handle/trait path where practical, preserving current shortcuts, socket commands, and rendering behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: share kittwm-bar model with live top bar
description:
Top-bar integration gap: `kittwm-bar` and live native session now render similar bar state separately. Extract/share the small bar model and scene builder so the SDK app and live session stay aligned.

Scope:
- Move the kittwm-bar model/render/scene helpers into a reusable library module in `kittui-cli` (not kittui-core).
- Update `kittwm-bar` binary to use the shared module.
- Update live native session top-bar text/scene construction to use the same shared model/scene builder where practical.
- Keep SDK socket loading in the binary; live session can construct the model from in-process pane count/socket/time.
- Preserve existing CLI outputs and live behavior.
- Add focused tests for shared model text/scene and existing binary/session tests.

Acceptance:
- One shared implementation defines top-bar text and scene labels.
- `kittwm-bar --scene-json` and live scene chrome use aligned scene labels/model text.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace substring-based protocol tests with exact-grammar regressions
description:
kittui-kitty unit tests assert .contains(...) on small substrings, which passed the previous malformed leading-comma upload chunks for months. Convert each public function to use exact-grammar regression tests (golden strings for fixed inputs, structural parsers for the rest) covering: upload still single chunk, upload still multi-chunk, upload animation 3 frames + control, placement direct + tmux + unicode, placeholder grid with diacritics for a 4x3 footprint and an image id with non-zero top byte, delete by id, delete by placement-id. Each test must also fail if a stray comma or missing q= regresses.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: replace substring tests with grammar regressions

title: kitwm daemon APPS verb: expose launcher candidates through --attach
description: Bridge the launcher candidate source into the daemon protocol. Add APPS and APPS_JSON daemon verbs so 'kitwm --attach -c APPS' lists default launcher + candidates and 'kitwm --attach -c APPS_JSON' returns the same machine-readable structure as 'kitwm apps --json'. This makes the running daemon expose launcher data to clients before the floating menu lands. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-7e7f3d/bd-13d80f.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add a=q query encoder and parser
description: Add pure kitty graphics a=q capability query encoder/parser helpers in kittui-kitty, with exact grammar/parser tests. No terminal I/O or render-loop integration. Follow-up from bd-02ef7b.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui inline row composition command
description:
Add inline row composition to avoid multiple process launches in prompts/statuslines.

Scope:
- Add a command such as `kittui inline row ...` or a practical equivalent that can render multiple inline chips/segments in one invocation.
- Goal use case: prompt/status script builds session/window/host chips without spawning kittui once per segment.
- Support at least JSON/config input or repeated simple flags; keep syntax pragmatic and testable.
- Preserve current single-chip command.
- Add tests for multiple components and width/ordering.
- Validate targeted kittui tests/build and diff check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: replace ASCII footer/status hints with graphical status components
description: Dogfood graphics gap: footer/status/action hints are currently text/ASCII. Replace runtime status hints (prefix active, last action, launch pid, logs, fps/transport) with graphical kittui status components/chips in graphics mode. Acceptance: no ASCII footer/status in graphics mode; status data remains visible and testable; terminal fallback stays compact.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: shell completions command
description:
Daily-driver UX: add shell completion generation.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm completions bash|zsh|fish` cooked-mode subcommand.
- Generate static completions from the local command/alias catalog plus common flags.
- Keep implementation dependency-free and presentation-only.
- Add focused tests for each supported shell and unsupported shell error.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users can install shell completions from kittwm itself.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: native kitty placement and row clamp
description: Improve kittwm-browser as a first-party kitty-graphics-native surface: clamp browser drawing/status rows to reported terminal dimensions, use cursor/absolute kitty placement options instead of placeholder grids, and add deterministic tests for row math/placement contract. Coordinate with kittui-dev; avoid kittwm session reservation/control-plane overlap.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-wm daemon: socket server + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY
description:
New binary kittui-wm-daemon (or kittui-wm serve subcommand) that listens on a Unix socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock). Owns all backends + Pumps. Wire format: length-prefixed bincode. v1 messages: CmdListWindows, CmdTick, CmdInput(InputEvent), CmdResize { cols, rows }, CmdQuit. Daemon survives client disconnects. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-624e22
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: implement cached image place subcommand
description:
Large gap: DESIGN documents `kittui place --id ...` as the shell workflow for re-placing cached/uploaded images, but the CLI does not implement it. Scripts can render an image id, but cannot later move/re-anchor it without going through Rust APIs.

Scope:
- Add a `kittui place --id ID --x X --y Y --cols C --rows R` subcommand that emits placement + placeholder bytes for an existing image id.
- Support decimal and `0x...` image id parsing.
- Honor existing output controls: `--json`, `--json-bytes`, `--dry-run`, `--placement-only`, and `--embed-only` where applicable.
- Add targeted CLI tests and update README/DESIGN if needed.

Acceptance:
- `kittui place --id 0x1234 --x 2 --y 3 --cols 4 --rows 2 --json --json-bytes` includes empty upload plus placement/embed channels.
- Non-JSON output emits placement+embed by default.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md pager supports arrow and page keys
description: Extend the kittui-md interactive pager input helper to understand common terminal escape sequences for Up/Down, PageUp/PageDown, Home/End, in addition to the existing letter controls. Add pure input tests using byte cursors so the behavior is covered without a real TTY.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: advertise NativeSurface extended capabilities
description:
NativeSurface capability metadata gap: the trait now supports exact-byte input, focus notifications, and side-effect event draining, but `SurfaceCapabilities` still only advertises coarse text input/capture/resize/title/restore.

Scope:
- Extend `SurfaceCapabilities` with explicit booleans for exact byte input, focus notifications, and side-effect event draining.
- Keep defaults false for compatibility.
- Set PTY terminal metadata to advertise exact bytes, focus notifications, and surface events.
- Keep X/Quartz/browser text input capabilities unchanged unless they truly support the new hooks.
- Capture-only adapters should keep the new flags false.
- Add focused tests for PTY and capture-only metadata.
- Do not change socket or live session behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface metadata accurately advertises the new hooks.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm doctor: one-shot diagnostics report (backends, displays, terminal, log, version)
description: Add 'kitwm doctor' subcommand that prints a self-contained health report: OS, binary version, available backends (sck/quartz/xvfb feature flags), display enumeration via Quartz (count + bounds), terminal probe (kitty graphics support, TERM, COLORTERM), /tmp/kittui-wm.log presence & last-modified, screen-recording permission hint. Output as plain text + JSON via --json. Useful for bug reports and quickly confirming a fresh checkout has the right toolchain. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: add NativeFrame inspection helpers
description:
NativeSurface ergonomics gap: callers repeatedly match `NativeFrame`/`SurfaceFrame` to inspect format and payload metadata.

Scope:
- Add small non-payload helper methods on `NativeFrame` and/or `SurfaceFrame`: format label, frame size, payload byte length, and RGBA/PNG predicates as appropriate.
- Keep helpers pure and allocation-free where possible.
- Add focused unit tests.
- Do not alter adapter behavior or live session defaults.

Acceptance:
- Composite/runtime code can inspect frame format/size/payload length without repeated matches.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: emit socket input events
description:
Runtime event gap: native socket input commands do not emit explicit input events. Add conservative queue-time events for socket-injected input so automation subscribers can observe control-plane input without reading raw command logs.

Scope:
- Emit `pane_input_sent` when SEND_TEXT/SEND_LINE, SEND_KEY/SEND_BYTES_B64, PASTE_BYTES_B64, and SEND_MOUSE queue successfully.
- Include target window/alias, input kind (`text`, `line`, `key`, `bytes`, `paste`, `mouse`), byte count or mouse event/col/row; do not include raw text/paste payload bytes.
- Preserve existing queue replies and command behavior.
- Add targeted daemon tests for event shape.

Acceptance:
- EVENTS subscribers can observe socket-injected input events without sensitive payloads.
- Existing automation commands remain compatible.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: common action subcommand aliases
description:
Daily-driver UX: add memorable action subcommands for common pane work.

Scope:
- Add source-level CLI aliases:
  - `kittwm spawn CMD [ARGS...]` -> `SPAWN_PTY <shell command>`
  - `kittwm read [WINDOW]` -> `READ_TEXT <window>` (default focused)
  - `kittwm read-json [WINDOW]` -> `READ_TEXT_JSON <window>`
  - `kittwm type [WINDOW] TEXT` -> `SEND_TEXT <window> <text>` (if one arg after type, default window focused)
  - `kittwm line [WINDOW] TEXT` -> `SEND_LINE <window> <text>`
  - `kittwm key [WINDOW] KEY` -> `SEND_KEY <window> <key>`
  - `kittwm wait [WINDOW] TEXT` -> `WAIT_OUTPUT <window> <text>`
- Keep existing flags working.
- Add focused parse tests; no daemon/runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Common daily-driver interactions are available without remembering long flags.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm keymap: Ctrl-A Ctrl-J launch alias for terminal Return compatibility
description: Superseded: do not add Ctrl-A Ctrl-J as launch alias because it conflicts with the operator-requested Ctrl-A C-j focus.down binding. Keep as a note only; future compatibility should use terminal key protocol / CSI-u rather than stealing C-j.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: handle tabs in native PTY snapshots
description:
Large terminal fidelity gap: native PTY text snapshots/rendering ignore TAB control characters. Many terminal programs output tabs; without tab-stop handling, automation `READ_TEXT` snapshots and rendered panes collapse tabular output incorrectly.

Scope:
- Teach native `TerminalState` to handle `\t` by advancing to the next 8-column tab stop, clamped to the row width.
- Add focused parser/text snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- `a\tb` appears with `b` at the next tab stop in `text_snapshot`.
- Existing native tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: raw RGB transmission (kitty f=32) instead of PNG re-encode per frame
description: kittui_wm_demo currently spends most of each frame PNG-encoding a fresh capture. The kitty graphics spec supports raw RGB(A) transmission via f=24 (RGB) and f=32 (RGBA), which skips PNG entirely. Wire that through kittui-kitty's upload_still + upload_still_ex (already accepts UploadMedium::Direct{bytes}; just needs a CaptureFormat option and a code-path in Runtime::place to skip PNG). Should drop the demo's per-frame cost from ~60ms to ~10ms on a 320x192 capture.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: flake: expose kittwm app and fix kittui package checks
description:
Packaging gap: users can only `nix run .#kittui`; there is no explicit `.#kittwm` flake app/package even though kittui-cli builds a kittwm binary. The flake should expose kittwm directly and build kittui-cli with platform-native kittwm backend features by default (sck/quartz on Darwin, xvfb on Linux). Also investigate/fix the current flake `kittui` checkPhase failure from `cargo test -p kittui-cli --lib`.

Scope:
- Add explicit `packages.kittwm` and `apps.kittwm` pointing at the kittwm binary from the same kittui-cli package.
- Preserve `packages.default` / `apps.default` as kittui.
- Ensure Darwin builds include quartz/sck backend features and Linux builds include xvfb backend features by default.
- Fix flake checkPhase test failure or make the package check run the correct feature/test set.
- Update README run instructions.

Acceptance:
- `nix run .#kittwm` is available.
- `nix run .#kittui` remains available.
- `nix build .#kittui` passes locally or the identified check failure is fixed.
- Targeted cargo/nix validation and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: expose render_json PNG bytes
description:
Large gap: Rust/FFI/Python/CLI now expose render-only PNG bytes, but TypeScript hosts still only have terminal placement APIs. JS platform preview/non-terminal embedding workflows need direct access to `kittui_render_json`.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_render_json` and `kittui_bytes_free` in the TypeScript koffi binding.
- Add `Kittui.render(scene): Uint8Array` (or Buffer-compatible Uint8Array) returning PNG bytes.
- Add declaration and fake-lib tests for render success/failure.
- Update TS README with render-only example.

Acceptance:
- TS tests pass.
- Render API returns bytes beginning with PNG signature in fake test and includes last_error on failure.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: type the architecture contract artifact
description: Move the kittwm architecture/platform contract into typed kittwm-sdk data structures so app authors can consume the same SDK/control-plane, tiling, surface-renderer, decoration-renderer, and kitty-compositor boundary contract that `kittwm architecture-json` prints. Keep implementation scoped to SDK types plus the CLI artifact using them; avoid live session reservation/control-plane internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: nix: fix mcp/updatable output hashes and showcase golden
description:
Fix Nix build after mcp-cli/updatable-cli update plumbing and recent graphical toast changes.

Issues:
- Nix build fails vendoring git deps without cargoLock.outputHashes for mcp-cli/updatable-cli.
- Nix package tests also need showcase golden/metrics updated for the toast scene now emitted by graphical shell showcase.

Acceptance:
- flake.nix has outputHashes for both git dependencies.
- `nix build .#kittui --no-link` succeeds.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md markup alias for HTML
description: Add --markup as a friendly alias for kittui-md --html so users can inspect preserved Markdown HTML fragments with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: font and glyph fallback matrix for terminal rendering
description: QA gap related to real font rendering: define and test glyph coverage/fallback for Fira Code, box drawing, powerline symbols, emoji/ambiguous-width characters, combining marks, wide CJK, and missing glyph display. Acceptance: matrix/golden outputs define expected rendering/fallback and prevent regression to placeholder A-Z glyphs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: publish browser semantic snapshots from DevTools
description: Wire the browser DOM/ARIA semantic extractor into HeadlessBrowserApp/kittwm so snapshots publish on navigation/focus/change with debounce and fallback to screenshots when extraction fails. Include tests or fakes for publish cadence and no-semantic fallback. Follow-up from bd-2250e1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md links-json inspection mode
description: Add --links-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for parsed Markdown links, emitting schema_version plus indexed label/url/title records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm default windows are native PTY terminals with KITTWM_SOCKET env
description: Operator direction: native PTY/browser support must be baked into kittwm, not demo subcommands. Default kittwm session and new splits/windows should create a kittui-native PTY terminal. The child environment should include KITTWM_SOCKET/KITTWM_DISPLAY-style context so apps launched inside the terminal know which WM/window they are in. Terminal apps such as htop should run normally inside that PTY. Parent epic: bd-031e54; continues bd-a9ec5b after native proof commands landed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: keep native socket responsive during wait-text
description:
Large terminal-WM control-plane reliability gap: native socket handling is sequential in the accept loop. Long-running requests such as WAIT_TEXT/WAIT_TEXT_MS block the socket server from answering unrelated inspection/control requests (PING, PANES_JSON, etc.) until the wait finishes. A DISPLAY-like WM control plane should remain responsive while one automation client waits for output.

Scope:
- Make the in-process native socket queue handle each accepted client connection concurrently.
- Keep shared pending/status state synchronization unchanged.
- Add a regression test: a WAIT_TEXT_MS timeout request must not block a concurrent PING.

Acceptance:
- Concurrent PING succeeds while another client is blocked in WAIT_TEXT_MS.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make inline script showcase clean and visibly animated
description:
Improve the inline script UI showcase after visual dogfooding feedback: the first version's absolute-positioned graphical overlays are messy in Ghostty screenshots and animations are too subtle/terminal-dependent.

Acceptance:
- Default graphics path is clean/sequential and screenshot-friendly.
- Old absolute layout remains opt-in for experiments.
- Script includes an obvious live pulse/status update when animation is enabled, while still passing --animated to kittui chrome.
- Smoke validate bash syntax plus text and graphics/static runs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
